IN THE CHAIR: CHRISTEL SCHALDEMOSE
Vice-President

2. Ukraine (joint debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The first item on the agenda is the joint debate on Ukraine:

芒聙聯 the statement by the Vice-President of the Commission/High Representative of the Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy on EU action to ensure security guarantees and just peace for Ukraine (2025/2851(RSP));

芒聙聯 the report by Michael Gahler, on behalf of the Committee on Foreign Affairs, on the 2023 and 2024 Commission reports on Ukraine (2025/2026(INI)) (A10-0154/2025).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kaja Kallas, Vice-President of the Commission / High Representative of the Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy. 芒聙聯 Good morning President, dear Members of the European Parliament, international efforts to end Russia's war have stepped up in the last few months.脗聽But Russia's response is more aggression: over the weekend, Russia launched the largest aerial assault of this war so far. Putin has zero interest in peace, and he will not stop the war until he's forced to.

This is why the European Union and our Member States have provided almost EUR脗聽169脗聽billion of financial support since the full-scale war started in 2022. This includes over EUR脗聽63脗聽billion in military support for Ukraine, and this year alone, Member States will provide more than ever before: EUR脗聽25脗聽billion to date. They have now also provided 80脗聽% of our target of two脗聽million rounds of ammunition. Our aim is to get to 100脗聽% by October, and this is all so that Ukraine can defend itself and its civilians and push back the aggression.

Europe is also the largest backer of Ukraine's own defence industry through windfall profits from the Russian frozen assets, and Europeans have stuck together through 18脗聽rounds of sanctions against Russia and those who enable Russia's war 芒聙聯 four in the last nine months alone. International sanctions have already deprived Russia of at least USD脗聽450脗聽billion in war funds, including USD脗聽154脗聽billion in revenues from oil. Europe was one of the largest markets of Russian energy, but not anymore: hopefully, by 2027, we won't be importing any Russian gas and oil at all. The European Union has also sanctioned 444脗聽shadow fleet ships that circumvent our sanctions and bring Russia illegal oil revenues.

Ukraine's bravery, backed up by international support, has already prevented Russia from achieving its military goals. But it is not enough. The only option we have is to pile more pressure on Russia, provide more support to Ukraine and prepare for the day after.

Honourable Members, more pressure on Russia means more sanctions and continued work to address circumvention, including dismantling the shadow fleet. Regarding sanctions, like I said, we are now working on the 19th脗聽package and collecting input from the Member States. We are also coordinating with our partners because the impact of the international coordinated sanctions is much stronger than the EU acting alone.

Our message to Russia is simple: the war is not winnable. Leave the battlefield and come to the table with Ukraine. When this happens, Russia must not be allowed to rearm and attack again. We have been there before, and Putin has made no secret that Ukraine is just the start: Putin wants to re-establish Russia's pre-1991 Soviet sphere of influence.

But the West is united in providing security guarantees to Ukraine. There are now serious pledges on the table of the coalition of the willing. That is why, the day after the coalition of the willing met last week, Putin threatened attacks on any Western presence on the ground, including peacekeepers. This is a serious escalation from Russia and directly undermines European security. Russia does not have a say in how we defend ourselves.

Honourable Members, that is why the European Union will keep doing its part to protect the security of our continent. Work is undergoing on the three main pillars. First, the European Union has already trained close to 80脗聽000脗聽Ukrainian soldiers. We are the largest training provider of Ukraine's armed forces, and we have also started working on changing the mandate of our mission so that we can also train soldiers in Ukraine. The strongest security guarantee is a strong Ukrainian army.

Second, the European Union will also keep supporting internal security and resilience in Ukraine through our civilian mission. We have already supported Ukraine's police force and help strengthen the rule of law in the country. And today there are many more challenges to address, including those related to war.

Third, we will keep strengthening Ukraine's defence industry, including helping Ukraine produce more military hardware in the European Union. The EU is already actively encouraging Member States to team up with Ukraine to support them and learn from Ukraine's war experience, and we are steadily integrating Ukraine into the European defence industrial base, including incentivising joint procurement through SAFE.

Honourable Members, Ukraine's accession to the European Union is something that gives hope to the Ukrainian people: the possibility to live free and prosper again. Ukraine has already shown a remarkable evolution in its reforms, even in the midst of war, but my colleague will talk about that more. Accession is a merit-based process, and Ukraine merits progress. It is time to open negotiations on Cluster 1.

The war will end, and when it does, Ukraine will recover and Russia will pay for the damage it has done. Russian sovereign assets should remain immobilised until Russia stops its war of aggression against Ukraine and compensates Ukraine for the billions of euros of damage it has caused and keeps causing. This is what the European Council has stated. It is essential that we deliver on this commitment.

Honourable members, what Russia may have thought inevitable is far from it: Russia has not broken Ukraine's resolve, and it must not break ours. For those who doubt, don't: Ukraine's security is European security, and so long as Russia pursues its illegal war, we must continue to fight.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael Gahler, Berichterstatter. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, sehr verehrte Hohe Vertreterin, liebe Erweiterungskommissarin! Wir haben heute eine verbundene Debatte, und ich danke zun脙陇chst der Hohen Vertreterin f脙录r ihre klaren Aussagen im Hinblick auf die j脙录ngsten russischen Terrorangriffe.

Putin will nichts weniger, als den russischen Endsieg 脙录ber die Ukraine erzwingen. Der unrechtm脙陇脙聼ige, ungerechtfertigte und unprovozierte Angriffskrieg hat nicht nur Tod und Zerst脙露rung 脙录ber ein souver脙陇nes Land gebracht, er ist zugleich ein Angriff auf die Grundwerte von Frieden, Freiheit und Demokratie in Europa.

Dieser Krieg muss beendet werden, doch Frieden entsteht nicht durch Worte allein. Russland hat in den vergangenen Monaten mit unseri脙露sen Vorschl脙陇gen und unehrlichen Verhandlungen gezeigt, dass es seinen Krieg fortsetzen will. Daher betonen wir: Ein bedingungsloser Waffenstillstand ist Voraussetzung f脙录r jede ernsthafte Friedensverhandlung! Solange dieser nicht gegeben ist, m脙录ssen wir unsere milit脙陇rische, materielle und finanzielle Unterst脙录tzung f脙录r die Ukraine verst脙陇rken, damit das Land sein Recht auf Selbstverteidigung wahren kann.

Die Ukrainer verteidigen nicht nur ihr Territorium, sondern auch ihren europ脙陇ischen Weg. Nie zuvor war allen B脙录rgern und politisch Handelnden so klar, warum sie angegriffen werden脗聽芒聙聯 n脙陇mlich deswegen, weil sie diesen europ脙陇ischen Weg zu Rechtsstaatlichkeit und Demokratie erfolgreich vollenden wollen. Die Alternative ist offensichtlich: Entweder man wird Teil des freien Europa oder man wird zur脙录ck in den sowjetischen Gulag gezwungen.

Wir unterst脙录tzen die Ukraine in ihrem Reformprozess, der im Detail im Anhang zur Ukraine-Fazilit脙陇t quartalsweise aufgef脙录hrt ist. Hierbei haben wir auch einen Hebel, denn die Zahlungen sind gebunden an die Erf脙录llung dieser Quartalshausaufgaben, und wenn die nicht erf脙录llt werden, werden auch die Zahlungen gek脙录rzt.

Auf diesem Reformweg kann es auch R脙录ckschritte oder Fehltritte geben. Hervorzuheben ist aber auch, dass die Reife der Gesellschaft inzwischen so weit fortgeschritten ist, dass solche Fehltritte durch den Druck der Gesellschaft auch wieder korrigiert werden k脙露nnen. Beeindruckend fand ich die Reaktion der Zivilgesellschaft auf den Versuch, das nationale Antikorruptionsb脙录ro der Ukraine, NABU, und die Sonderstaatsanwaltschaft SAPO f脙录r Korruptionsbek脙陇mpfung unter die Kontrolle des Generalstaatsanwalts zu stellen. Da gingen viele trotz des Krieges auf die Stra脙聼e, und auch wir sahen darin den Versuch, unabh脙陇ngige Ermittler unter die Kontrolle der Regierung zu bekommen. Der urspr脙录ngliche Zustand wurde wiederhergestellt脗聽芒聙聯 das ist gut.脗聽Wir sehen daneben beachtliche Fortschritte, die oftmals mithilfe und durch das Beharren der internationalen Gemeinschaft erreicht werden.

Die Ukraine muss ihr Reform-Momentum weiterhin nutzen: zur St脙陇rkung ihrer demokratischen Institutionen, zur Angleichung an die Gemeinsame Au脙聼en- und Sicherheitspolitik der EU, zur Bek脙陇mpfung von Korruption und Missst脙陇nden in der Justiz. Diese Schritte sind nicht nur entscheidend f脙录r den EU-Beitritt, sie sind auch ein Fundament f脙录r das Vertrauen der eigenen Bev脙露lkerung in Staat und Rechtsordnung. Daher fordern wir auch, dass politische Vielfalt, unabh脙陇ngige Justiz und transparente Verfahren gesch脙录tzt und gest脙陇rkt werden. Wir fordern, unzeitgem脙陇脙聼e und politisch motivierte Gerichtsverfahren und Sanktionen gegen Vertreter der Opposition zu unterlassen, den parlamentarischen Pluralismus zu wahren und einen konstruktiven Dialog zwischen den politischen Gruppierungen in der Werchowna Rada zu f脙露rdern; au脙聼erdem alle Beschr脙陇nkungen f脙录r mandatsbezogene und politische Auslandsreisen f脙录r Mitglieder der Werchowna Rada aufzuheben.

Der Europ脙陇ische Rat hat im Dezember 2023 den historischen Beschluss zur Aufnahme von Beitrittsverhandlungen gefasst. Die erste Regierungskonferenz fand im Juni 2024 statt. Nun gilt es, entschlossen weiterzugehen脗聽芒聙聯 ohne Blockaden oder Vorw脙陇nde. Wir rufen alle Mitgliedstaaten dazu auf, konstruktiv zu handeln und den Weg der Ukraine in Richtung EU-Mitgliedschaft zu ebnen. Es gilt, zeitnah Clusterverhandlungen aufzunehmen, damit m脙露glichst viele schnelle Fortschritte in Bezug auf den EU-Beitritt des Landes erzielt werden k脙露nnen.

Lassen Sie uns gemeinsam ein klares Signal senden! Die Ukraine geh脙露rt zur europ脙陇ischen Familie, und wir werden alles daransetzen, dass sie ihren Platz in der Europ脙陇ischen Union so bald wie m脙露glich einnimmt. Mit guten Reformen kann die Ukraine dieses Ziel schneller erreichen. Slawa Ukrajini!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Kos, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, I would like to begin with a big thank you and deepest gratitude to this House for your resolute commitment and continued support to Ukraine.

Your strong voice has been a source of strength in Kyiv. They need it. It is a beacon of hope that Ukraine's sacrifices are not in vain. Let me in particular thank you, dear Mr脗聽Gahler, the rapporteur on Ukraine, for your work in steering this House's positions forward with clarity, conviction and optimism.

Today, as we consider the Commission's enlargement reports for 2023 and 2024, and as we look ahead soon to the 2025 report already in preparation, we must remind ourselves what is truly at stake.

Ukraine's path to accession is a matter of survival, sovereignty and long芒聙聭term security, not only for Ukraine, but for Europe as a whole. Ukraine has chosen this path freely and legitimately. It is what Ukrainians are fighting for day after day, night after night, under continuous attacks. Their choice is clear: a future anchored in peace, stability and prosperity 芒聙聯 a future within the EU family.

But it is not an easy road. While heavy fighting continues, Russia's brutal attacks on civilian targets have intensified. As we know, on Sunday, Russia struck the Cabinet of Ministers building in Kyiv for the first time 芒聙聯 a direct attack on the seat of Ukraine's Government. And of course, we know that this attack was not an accident. It was a message. Russia does not want peace. Russia fears Ukraine's European aspirations and dismisses democracy. That is why our response must be resolute and united.

Despite this brutal war, Ukraine has advanced steadily on reforms. The Commission's assessment shows progress across key areas such as energy, customs, and foreign and security policy. That is a clear demonstration of determination.

But accession is not only about ticking boxes. It is about transforming a state and society to guarantee the rule of law, the fight against corruption, the independence of institutions and a functioning democracy, meaning following the Copenhagen criteria.脗聽This requires the whole of Ukraine: government, civil society, stakeholders and particularly the Verkhovna Rada, the Parliament of Ukraine.

We saw in July 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽Gahler, you mentioned it 芒聙聯 how fragile progress can be. Attempts to undermine the independence of the anti芒聙聭corruption bodies NABU and SAPO were deeply troubling. We were crystal clear that such moves are unacceptable. These institutions are the very foundations of the Ukraine that Ukrainians want to build: transparent, accountable, European.

On 31脗聽July, Ukraine corrected course. Key safeguards were restored. Other measures, including the appointment of the head of the Economic Security Bureau 芒聙聯 we waited for months for this 芒聙聯 were unblocked. These are positive steps and that calls for the vigilance of every actor for the sake of Ukraine's European future.

In this context, the Commission has said that Ukraine has met conditions to open Cluster 1, while we encourage them to continue work on the implementation of roadmaps and especially on an action plan for minorities. At this crucial moment, a strong sign of support is more than symbolic. It is strategic and it is deserved.

I would love to stress that it is not true what some people claim that we are stuck in the accession process. From the technical point of view, we are working really a lot and in a few weeks we will be in the position to be able to open all the clusters. But the decision has to be made in the Council. So, I call on the Council to decide on the opening of Cluster 1 without further delays on the only legitimate ground: the merits of Ukraine.

Much has already been achieved together and together we must continue. Honourable Members, Ukraine's EU accession is the most powerful guarantee of its security. Each reform consolidated, each cluster open, each step forward is a step that locks Ukraine more firmly into our common European home.

It is also a signal to Moscow that Ukraine's European destiny is irreversible. Let us not underestimate what this means. Ukrainians are defending not only their territory, but also freedom and democracy in Europe. The least we can do is match their courage with our political determination.

We will continue to support Ukraine in advancing reforms, particularly in the rule of law, and we will continue to ensure that the accession process remains merit芒聙聭based, credible and predictable.脗聽I count on this House to remain fully engaged, to encourage all political forces in Ukraine to work constructively and to remind all Member States that the time for hesitation has passed.

Ukraine's future is in Europe. Its people have chosen this path with courage, with sacrifice and with hope. Now it is for us, all of us, to be up to these circumstances, to help make this choice irreversible and to ensure that one day soon, Ukraine takes its rightful place also in this House, in the European Parliament.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pekka Toveri, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, I totally agree with Commissioner脗聽Kallas in her very wise words.

Europe is not safe before Ukraine is safe, and to safeguard Ukraine's safety and freedom, it is crucial that Ukraine can join the EU. Even though Ukraine is fighting existential war to survive, it has been able to take big steps towards its future EU membership. It is extremely important that this positive development can continue, that we stay on our course to accept Ukraine to the Union as fast as possible when Ukraine is fulfilling the requirements.

At the same time, we have Member States like Hungary which are working with Russia to sabotage and prevent Ukraine's EU integration. This is totally unacceptable. EU Parliament, Commission and Council must use all tools and pressure to prevent Hungary or any other Member State to sabotage our joint future with Ukraine.

Slava Ukraini!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thijs Reuten, on behalf of the S&D Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, with this report Parliament sends a strong signal. Ukraine is part of our European family, and our support for its accession is unwavering.

Despite the war, Ukrainian authorities continue to deliver on reforms, and we recognise the progress, but also the difficulties. As true partners, we offer honest, constructive recommendations to Ukraine and to the Commission, so momentum is not lost.

But let's be clear. Accession is overshadowed by Ukraine's 芒聙聯 and therefore our 芒聙聯 existential fight against Russia's aggression, and here it's time to be blunt. We see the same cycle. The US pretends that there will be additional pressure. Putin pretends to talk. Europe hesitates. Pressure fades. Then Putin escalates his violence 芒聙聯 and repeat.

Ukraine suffers while defending itself and us, and we need to be honest: an unchecked Russia would be a disaster for the whole Union, from Poland to Ireland, from Finland to Greece.

Too many Member States still fail to face the truth. We talk in circles 芒聙聯 too much talk about Trump, too little action and too little leadership.

Let's not fool anyone. This war is not over. Ukraine is brilliantly defending itself and holding the line. It is not losing, but there is no fair and just peace on the horizon, because Putin doesn't want it. Every signal says the opposite, and there's no point talking about security guarantees when we cannot pressure Putin into wanting peace.

So our strategy must change: more pressure; more weapons; stronger sanctions; real enforcement; seizing the assets 芒聙聯 the Russian assets.

Avoiding risk now only guarantees greater risk later. The level of our support determines the level of Ukrainian suffering.

We know what to do. We can do more than we think as Europeans, and it's time for Europe not just to support, but to lead. That is how we achieve a just, fair and lasting peace.

Thank you to the rapporteur for the cooperation, and Slava Ukraini!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kinga G脙隆l, a PfE k脙漏pvisel脜聭csoport nev脙漏ben. 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k Asszony, F脜聭k脙漏pvisel脜聭 Asszony, Biztos Asszony! Mikor olyan javaslatokat hallok, hogy az EU csapatokat 脙隆llom脙隆soztatna Ukrajn脙隆ban, akkor n脙漏gy fi脙潞gyermek 脙漏desanyjak脙漏nt r脙漏m脙录let t脙露lt el. Minden ilyen meg脙隆llap脙颅t脙隆s m脙漏g ink脙隆bb a h脙隆bor脙潞t hozza k脙露zelebb az Eur脙鲁pai Uni脙鲁hoz, nem pedig a b脙漏k脙漏t az ukr脙隆noknak.

Eur脙鲁pa ma gyeng脙漏bb, mint valaha. Ez az amerikai eln脙露k b脙漏ke ir脙隆nti er脜聭fesz脙颅t脙漏sei kapcs脙隆n nyilv脙隆nval脙鲁v脙隆 v脙隆lt. Ukrajna ma tov脙隆bbra is az orosz agresszi脙鲁, a puszt脙颅t脙隆s legsz脙露rny脜卤bb form脙隆it tapasztalja. Mi itt pedig sz脙隆mtalan vita 脙漏s hat脙隆rozat, rengeteg Ukrajn脙隆ba k脙录ld脙露tt p脙漏nz ellen脙漏re, egyhelyben toporgunk, hiszen hi脙隆nyzik a beismer脙漏s 脙漏s a tettek sora, hogy ezt a h脙隆bor脙潞t nem csatat脙漏ren, hanem csakis diplom脙隆ciai 脙潞ton lehet megnyerni.

Nem lehet a b脜聭v脙颅t脙漏spolitik脙隆t 脙露sszemosni a biztons脙隆gi garanci脙隆kkal. A Bizotts脙隆g 脙隆ls脙隆gosan 脙潞gy tesz, mintha Ukrajna m脙隆r most teljes脙颅ten脙漏 a felt脙漏teleket, mik脙露zben messze nincs felk脙漏sz脙录lve a gyors脙颅tott csatlakoz脙隆sra. Ez nem 脙漏rdem alap脙潞 b脜聭v脙颅t脙漏spolitika, hanem politikai hiba, s脙潞lyos k脙露vetkezm脙漏nyekkel, amelyeket nem szabad elhallgatni. K脙录l脙露n脙露sen cinikus mindez a sok 脙漏ve teljes脙颅t脜聭 nyugat balk脙隆ni tagjel脙露lt orsz脙隆gokkal szemben. Az eg脙漏sz Uni脙鲁 alapvet脜聭 脙漏rdeke, hogy csak egyhang脙潞s脙隆ggal lehessen ezeket a komoly d脙露nt脙漏seket meghozni, nem pedig tag脙隆llamokat megker脙录lve. Az emberek feje felett nem sz脙录lethetnek meg elhamarkodott d脙露nt脙漏sek, hiszen ezek arr脙鲁l is sz脙鲁lnak, milyen Eur脙鲁pai Uni脙鲁t hagyunk a k脙露vetkez脜聭 gener脙隆ci脙鲁kra. Egy legyeng脙颅tettet, vagy olyat, amelyik k脙漏pes saj脙隆t 脙漏rdekei mellett ki脙隆llni? Ha nem n脙漏z脙录nk 脜聭szint脙漏n szembe a val脙鲁s脙隆ggal, Eur脙鲁pa hamarosan hideg zuhanyra 脙漏bred.

脗聽脗聽Micha脜聜 Dworczyk, w imieniu grupy ECR. 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Sprawozdanie, nad kt脙鲁rym dzi脜聸 debatujemy, to nie tylko dokument analityczny. To r脙鲁wnie脜录 脜聸wiadectwo niezwyk脜聜ej determinacji Ukrainy, kt脙鲁ra pomimo trwaj脛聟cej rosyjskiej agresji nie tylko broni swoich granic, ale r脙鲁wnie脜录 nieprzerwanie reformuje instytucje oraz wzmacnia demokratyczne mechanizmy, dostosowuj脛聟c swoje prawo do wymog脙鲁w Unii. Sprawozdanie wskazuje na znacz脛聟ce post脛聶py, od walki z korupcj脛聟 po reform脛聶 systemu s脛聟downictwa. Jednocze脜聸nie widzimy r脙鲁wnie脜录 wyzwania: reforma administracji pa脜聞stwowej na poziomie lokalnym, potrzeba wi脛聶kszej niezale脜录no脜聸ci s脛聟d脙鲁w, skuteczniejszej ochrony wolnych medi脙鲁w i wsparcia spo脜聜ecze脜聞stwa obywatelskiego.

Dla Polski niezwykle wa脜录ne jest wznowienie od ko脜聞ca 2022 roku wydawania przez ukrai脜聞skie w脜聜adze pozwole脜聞 na prace poszukiwawcze i ekshumacje Polak脙鲁w zamordowanych przez ukrai脜聞skich nacjonalist脙鲁w z UPA w czasie II wojny 脜聸wiatowej. To bardzo istotny proces, kt脙鲁ry musi by脛聡 kontynuowany nie tylko dla dobra relacji dwustronnych, polsko芒聙聯ukrai脜聞skich, ale r脙鲁wnie脜录 w duchu warto脜聸ci chrze脜聸cija脜聞skich b脛聶d脛聟cych fundamentem zjednoczonej Europy.

Cz脜聜onkostwo Ukrainy le脜录y w strategicznym interesie ca脜聜ego kontynentu. To wi脛聶ksze bezpiecze脜聞stwo energetyczne i stabilno脜聸脛聡 wschodnich granic. Europa silna to Europa z Ukrain脛聟. Dzi脜聸 zatem naszym obowi脛聟zkiem jest nie tylko solidarno脜聸脛聡, ale odwaga, by t脛聶 przysz脜聜o脜聸脛聡 zbudowa脛聡 razem, wspieraj脛聟c Ukrain脛聶 w jej walce oraz w drodze dalszych reform.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Petras Au脜隆trevi脛聧ius, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear Kaja, dear Marta, dear colleagues, Russia's unprovoked and illegal aggression against Ukraine, which has been ongoing for 42 months, has undermined the foundations of European security.

To stop Russia, we must create a security and defence mechanism which is stronger than its militaristic ambitions. To deter the aggressor, Europe has no choice but to extend the European security system to Ukrainian territory and strengthen its defence forces.

Applying Newton's principle of physics 芒聙聯 energy cannot be created or destroyed 芒聙聯 security guarantees will be effective if they are genuine and sufficient. A secure sky over Ukraine, the deployment of EU Member States' military forces on its territory, and further military assistance to Ukraine are only part of the security guarantees that can truly deliver.

Russia must pay military reparations to Ukraine.脗聽I warmly welcome the 9脗聽July decision of the European Court of Human Rights in the case of Ukraine and the Netherlands v. Russia, which found Russia guilty of systematic violations in Ukraine, including the downing of MH17.

This court decision provides the legal basis for using Russia's frozen assets to compensate the Ukrainian people for immense damages and losses.

Security and justice are integral parts of European life. Let us extend them to the people of Ukraine now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sergey Lagodinsky, im Namen der Verts/ALE-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Es muss klar sein, es muss uns allen klar sein: Es wird keinen Frieden geben ohne Friedensgarantien. Es ist nicht m脙露glich, eine Demokratie, einen Investitionsstandort, einen unabh脙陇ngigen Staat abzusichern im Schatten der milit脙陇rischen Erpressung durch den脗聽Nachbarn. Die EU muss f脙录r Einsatz sorgen, die USA f脙录r mehr Klarheit脗聽芒聙聯 Klarheit, dass die Ukraine nicht den russischen Drohungen ausgeliefert wird und dass Europa nicht alleine steht, wenn es zu einem Ernstfall kommt.

Das bedeutet dreierlei. Erstens: Europ脙陇ische Pr脙陇senz ist kein Krieg; sie ist Frieden, sie ist Teil der Diplomatie. Wenn wir nach einer Befriedung Pr脙陇senz sichern, dann ist das eine Friedensgarantie und kein Kriegseinsatz.

Zweitens: Amerikaner m脙录ssen beteiligt sein. Es kann nicht sein, dass Br脙录ssel mehr unter Druck steht als Moskau. Es kann nicht sein, dass wir Kooperation im Bereich Abwehr der Desinformation, wie heute bekannt wurde, beenden, dass Amerikaner diese Kooperation beenden. Nein, der Zusammenhang zwischen Russland und China muss auch unseren amerikanischen Partnerinnen und Partnern klarer werden. Es ist in der Verantwortung der EU, dass die Ukraine sicher bleibt, aber es ist auch im Interesse der USA, dass dies gelingt.

Und drittens: Die F脙录hrungsverantwortung Deutschlands steht au脙聼er Frage. Vorbei sind die Zeiten, wo Deutschland durch Zur脙录ckhaltung Gew脙陇hr f脙录r Frieden war. Jetzt m脙录ssen wir Verantwortung 脙录bernehmen, nicht mit S脙露der'schem Provinztum, sondern mit dem europ脙陇ischen Geist, und das bedeutet auch Pr脙陇senz der europ脙陇ischen und der deutschen Soldaten, auch bei einer Friedensabsicherung.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脙聳zlem Demirel, im Namen der Fraktion The Left. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Frau Kallas, voller Stolz haben Sie hier jetzt das 19.脗聽Sanktionspaket angek脙录ndigt. Aber ich m脙露chte Sie fragen: Welche Ma脙聼nahme haben Sie denn jetzt w脙陇hrend des Genozids an den Pal脙陇stinensern gegen脙录ber Israel ergriffen? Wann wollen Sie das EU-Assoziationsabkommen endlich mal aussetzen? Ich finde, an dem Punkt wird Ihre Doppelmoral ziemlich, ziemlich deutlich.

Liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, seit drei Jahren h脙露re ich mir Reden an, dass es in der Ukraine um einen Krieg 芒聙聻Autokratie versus Demokratie芒聙聹 ginge. Und selbstverst脙陇ndlich steht das Putin-Regime f脙录r eine autokratische Politik. Aber stimmt es, dass Herr Selenskyj und die Ukraine so demokratisch sind? Ich meine, Herr Selenskyj ist nicht nur f脙录r seine korrupten Offshore-Deals bekannt. Es ist auch so, dass in der Ukraine im Moment Gewerkschaftsrechte ausgeh脙露hlt werden, jegliche Oppositionspartei verboten wurde, und ganz egal, wie man zu diesen Parteien steht脗聽芒聙聯 das steht nicht unbedingt f脙录r Demokratie! Was ist mit den Arbeitnehmerrechten? Was ist mit den Gewerkschaftlern, die in Hausarrest sitzen? Das alles, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, verdeutlicht, dass es hier nicht um Demokratie geht, sondern um knallharte Geopolitik von Ihnen.

Ich will es deutlich sagen: Wenn Sie jetzt schon sagen脗聽芒聙聯 bevor Sie am Verhandlungstisch sitzen脗聽芒聙聯, dass Sie jetzt Truppen in der Ukraine installieren wollen, Frau Kallas, wie Sie und Frau von der Leyen es angek脙录ndigt haben, ist das einfach unhaltbar. Das verdeutlicht: Sie wollen keinen Frieden. Die Menschen in Europa stehen aber f脙录r den Frieden in der Ukraine, in Europa und weltweit!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hans Neuhoff, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Die einzige wirksame Sicherheitsgarantie f脙录r die Ukraine w脙陇re ihre R脙录ckkehr zur Neutralit脙陇t. Die EU behauptet, einen Waffenstillstand anzustreben. Doch indem sie verlangt, dass EU- und NATO-Staaten ihn 脙录berwachen, gibt sie Russland nur den Anreiz, noch mehr ukrainisches Territorium zu besetzen.

Warum dieses widersinnige Handeln? Erstens: Jeder Kompromiss mit Russland gilt als geopolitischer Gesichtsverlust脗聽芒聙聯 Moral schl脙陇gt Realismus. Zweitens: Die EU-Kommandeure wollen sich als Gestalter inszenieren, obwohl sie in Wahrheit Getriebene sind. Drittens: Sie alle stecken in der Psychologie eskalierender Selbstbindung fest脗聽芒聙聯 too much invested to quit.

Kolleginnen und Kollegen, Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, erwachen Sie, bevor Sie alles verspielt haben!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rasa Juknevi脛聧ien脛聴 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President,脗聽dear colleagues, let's be clear: Ukraine and its European future are as important to European security today as the United States was to Europe in the decades after World War II.

All normal people want peace. However, peace at the expense of Ukraine and on Russia's terms could be the beginning of a major catastrophe for all of Europe.

Europe needs a strategy for Ukraine if Trump's bid to end the war fails. We need a European plan to lead the fight for Ukraine's and our future. Its core is to help contain Russia and integrate Ukraine into a pan-European defence alliance.

What must we do today? Provide significantly greater financial support for Ukraine's defence. This is money for our own defence as well.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tobias Cremer (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, colleagues, I want to begin by thanking Donald Trump. Seriously. Because this summer he's disproven once and for all a zombie theory that some in this House have been riding like a dead horse and have been riding again today, that we, if only we accommodated Putin, only reached out to him, we could have an easy peace.

That's what Trump tried and he failed. He has given everything to Putin. He rolled out the red carpet. He made concessions. And what did he get? Nothing. Nothing at all. No ceasefire. No compromise. Only more attacks, more escalation, more aggression. And in a despicable mockery of diplomatic solutions, Putin bombed our own embassy in Kyiv, endangering the very men and women who sacrifice their life, and who dedicate their life to diplomatic solutions.

So what more do we need to finally understand that the only language that Putin speaks is strength, strength and strength?

So let's finally show some strength and strengthen Ukraine's armed forces, strengthen our sanctions regime, and strengthen Western unity by showing European rather than Trumpian leadership. Appeasement has failed. Now it's time to get real about strength in peace.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pierre-Romain Thionnet (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, permettez-moi tout d'abord de rendre hommage aux nombreuses victimes civiles ukrainiennes des derniers jours. Les engins russes qui traversent impun脙漏ment le ciel ukrainien leur ont apport脙漏 la mort.

On nous dit que les plans d'une force europ脙漏enne de r脙漏assurance sont pr脙陋ts. Fort bien. Mais ces plans sont des plans de l'esprit. Leurs d脙漏tails sont certainement savants, leurs logistiques bien pens脙漏es, mais ils valent pour l'apr脙篓s.

Tandis que la guerre fait toujours rage et que le sang des Ukrainiens macule les forteresses du Donbass, ces plans sont un terrible aveu d'impuissance. Non seulement ils ne valent qu'en cas de feu vert am脙漏ricain, sans lequel il n'y aura ni renseignement, ni surveillance, ni d脙漏tection, mais, surtout, ils disent au monde: 脗芦Nous, Europ脙漏ens, n'avons pas su pr脙漏venir cette invasion, nous n'avons pas pu dicter l'issue du conflit, mais croyez-nous, nous agirons quand ce sera fini et si on veut bien nous y autoriser.脗禄

Au milieu de l'ensauvagement du monde, l'Europe court vers de grands malheurs si elle persiste 脙聽 croire que la puissance se donne plut脙麓t qu'elle ne se prend.

脗聽脗聽Alberico Gambino (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signora Commissaria, onorevoli colleghi, dopo anni in cui Mosca pretendeva soltanto la resa di Kiev, oggi, a mio avviso, si aprono spiragli di dialogo, resi possibili dalla resistenza del popolo ucraino e dal sostegno compatto dell'Occidente, come ha ricordato la presidente Meloni.

In questo quadro, l'Italia ha avuto un ruolo significativo al recente vertice della Casa Bianca, proponendo un sistema di garanzia di sicurezza ispirata all'articolo 5 della NATO: non un invio di truppe, ma l'impegno a una risposta rapida e credibile contro nuove aggressioni.

Eppure, purtroppo, ahi noi, la realt脙聽 resta drammatica: la Russia ha appena lanciato un vasto attacco aereo dall'inizio della guerra, con centinaia di missili e droni su Kiev, che hanno provocato morte e devastazione.

Di fronte a questa ferocia non possiamo cedere allo scoraggiamento. Come ha ricordato papa Leone XIV, serve il coraggio di uscire dalla spirale dell'odio e percorrere la via del dialogo. 脙聢 questa la bussola che deve guidare l'Europa: libert脙聽, sicurezza e dignit脙聽 per l'Ucraina.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie-Agnes Strack-Zimmermann (Renew). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, ich m脙露chte mich erst mal bei Ihnen beiden bedanken f脙录r Ihr Engagement. Meine Damen und Herren, die Ukraine wurde angegriffen in einem unvorstellbaren Ma脙聼e; Trump hofiert Putin; von der Leyens Flugzeug wird gejammt auf dem Anflug nach Bulgarien; unsere EU-Vertretung wird zerst脙露rt in Kyjiw脗聽芒聙聯 was muss noch passieren?

Ich h脙露re von der Koalition der Willigen, sehe aber vor allen Dingen nationale Egoismen. Wenn die EU in Zukunft eine Rolle spielen will im Theater auf dieser Welt, dann nicht als Zwerg, sondern als Riese, als Riese um die USA, neben China, neben Russland; anders haben wir 脙录berhaupt keine Chance.

Das bedeutet, meine Damen und Herren, dass wir alle in unsere Heimat zur脙录ckfahren m脙录ssen, in unsere Nationen, um unseren Regierungen脗聽芒聙聯 egal ob wir zur Regierung oder zur Opposition geh脙露ren脗聽芒聙聯 klarzumachen: Wir alle sind verantwortlich f脙录r 450脗聽Millionen Menschen, die in Frieden und Freiheit leben wollen. Deswegen muss die EU pr脙陇sent sein und darf sich nicht ducken, und vor allen Dingen d脙录rfen sich die Nationen nicht wegducken.

(Die Rednerin ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹 zu antworten.)

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI), Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, im Einklang mit unserer Gesch脙陇ftsordnung w脙录rde ich gerne auch auf die Antwort reagieren k脙露nnen, die Frau Strack-Zimmermann gibt. Frau Strack-Zimmermann, Sie haben dar脙录ber gesprochen, dass wir keine nationalen Alleing脙陇nge mehr machen d脙录rfen, und Sie haben dar脙录ber gesprochen, dass wir nach Hause gehen m脙录ssen und unseren Regierungen in den Arsch treten m脙录ssen.

Es gibt ein Konzept, das bisher nur eine europ脙陇ische Regierung f脙陇hrt, und zwar, dass man seinen Staatsb脙录rgern erlaubt, in der ukrainischen Armee zu k脙陇mpfen. Das erlaubt allein Polen. W脙录rden Sie mir zustimmen, wenn ich sage, dass wir das europaweit erlauben sollten, um jenen Staatsb脙录rgern unserer Nationen, die f脙录r die Freiheit k脙陇mpfen m脙露chten, das auch erm脙露glichen zu k脙露nnen?

脗聽脗聽Marie-Agnes Strack-Zimmermann (Renew), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Ich bin der Meinung, dass jeder Staat sich im Klaren sein muss: Er ist Teil der Europ脙陇ischen Union, und das hei脙聼t nicht nur, die Give-aways zu bekommen, nicht nur das Geld zu nehmen, sondern auch Verantwortung zu 脙录bernehmen. Wie das im Einzelnen aussieht, das sollten wir hier ganz genau diskutieren; darum geht es. Weil ich immer noch L脙陇nder sehe, die reden viel, aber es passiert wenig. Andere machen etwas, das reicht aber vorne und hinten nicht.

Wenn wir ernst genommen werden wollen in diesem Dreiklang脗聽芒聙聯 was im Indopazifik passiert, was Russland nach wie vor macht und wie sich der amerikanische Pr脙陇sident geriert脗聽芒聙聯, kann ich nur sagen: Wir haben eine Chance, wenn wir hier zusammen auftreten. Darin liegt unsere St脙陇rke, und das sollten wir auch tun.

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI), Reaktion nach dem Verfahren der "blauen Karte". 芒聙聯 Ich stimme allem zu, was Sie gesagt haben. 脗聽脗聽Virginijus Sinkevi脛聧ius (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, High Representative, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, Ukrainian cities are under the heaviest bombardment in months, and this just after the red carpet was rolled out to Putin in Alaska. Power stations, hospitals and homes are being hit night after night, and yet Ukraine still finds the strength to push forward with reforms from justice to decentralisation, because the Ukrainian people know their future is European. Last week in Paris, 26脗聽nations pledged to form a reassurance force to protect Ukraine once there is a peace agreement. This is not a theory anymore: it is Europe showing it can act even when Washington hesitates.

Dear colleagues, let us be honest: Moscow has zero interest in peace. It wants time to rearm, not to reconcile. And that is why the EU must lead. Our responsibility is to deliver weapons fast, close all the loopholes in sanctions, and keep Ukraine's accession path alive while giving it real security guarantees. That will deter the next possible attacks.

If we get this right, we will not be protecting just Ukraine. We will be protecting...

(The President cut off the speaker)

Za podm脙颅nek prosazovan脙陆ch Evropskou uni脙颅 nem脜炉脜戮e b脙陆t dosa脜戮eno m脙颅ru a lidsk脙漏 ztr脙隆ty a materi脙隆ln脙颅 脜隆kody se budou nad脙隆le stup脜聢ovat s rizikem vzniku t脜聶et脙颅 sv脛聸tov脙漏 v脙隆lky.

脗聽脗聽Micha脜聜 Szczerba (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Dzie脜聞 dobry Polsko! Dzie脜聞 dobry Europo! Dzisiaj jest dzie脜聞 dobrych wiadomo脜聸ci. Fundusz Safe zosta脜聜 rozdysponowany. Przed nami dwa kluczowe zadania. To gotowo脜聸脛聡 i odstraszanie. Nie jest to mo脜录liwe bez silnego potencja脜聜u obronnego. Konieczna tu jest wsp脙鲁脜聜praca z ukrai脜聞skim przemys脜聜em dronowym. Tu jest przestrze脜聞 na wsp脙鲁脜聜prac脛聶 z Ukrain脛聟. Osi脛聟gn脛聶li w tym zakresie mistrzostwo.

Ale r脙鲁wnie脜录 bardzo wa脜录na rzecz. W tym sprawozdaniu nie zapominamy o historii, inwestuj脛聟c w przysz脜聜o脜聸脛聡. Rze脜潞 wo脜聜y脜聞ska. Ka脜录da ofiara tej rzezi musi zosta脛聡 upami脛聶tniona. Rozpocz脛聶脜聜y si脛聶 ekshumacje, rozpocz脛聶脜聜y si脛聶 poch脙鲁wki. Bardzo dzi脛聶kuj脛聶, 脜录e w tym sprawozdaniu na temat Ukrainy ta sprawa zosta脜聜a podkre脜聸lona.

脗聽脗聽Hel脙漏ne Fritzon (S&D). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! V脙陇rldsordningen 脙陇r i gungning och Europa st脙楼r inf脙露r sin sv脙楼raste pr脙露vning p脙楼 decennier. 脗聽

脗聽脗聽Roberto Vannacci (PfE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, nella mia vita non avrei mai pensato di dover dar ragione a una pasionaria del socialismo, del green deal脗聽e della cessione della sovranit脙聽 nazionale alla tecnocrazia di Bruxelles. Ma una cosa giusta, nella sua vita, il commissario Teresa Ribera l'ha detta: questa Unione europea ha fallito.

Ha fallito sui dazi, cos脙卢 brillantemente negoziati da "Frau von der Leyen"; ha fallito sull'immigrazione, riempiendo l'Europa dei disperati della Terra; ha fallito sull'energia, che prima compravamo economicamente dai russi, e oggi spendiamo 700 miliardi di euro per comprarla da Washington; ha fallito sulle armi, che oggi compriamo dagli Stati Uniti per darle e regalarle agli ucraini; ha fallito sulla guerra, pensando di poterla vincere con sanzioni economiche, con roboanti dichiarazioni e con tanta propaganda.

Oggi continua a fallire sulla pace, rinunciando alla pace possibile e giusta, quella che si pu脙虏 ottenere subito, e ubriacando i popoli europei con il concetto di "pace giusta", che non 脙篓 mai esistito nella storia dell'umanit脙聽.

E fallisce anche sulle garanzie di sicurezza, perch脙漏 non esiste garanzia senza il rischio di dover andare a combattere, se necessario.

E ve lo dico da patriota e sovranista convinto: non una goccia di sangue italiano verr脙聽 versato per questa guerra che non difende la nostra patria e non difende i nostri interessi nazionali.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Bielan (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, High Representative, the ECR Group has always been clear: there can be no long-lasting peace without justice and no justice without Ukraine's full territorial integrity and sovereignty.

Any so-called compromise that rewards the Russian aggression will be seen as weakness and will only embolden the imperialist regime.

We must stand with Ukraine, not only out of solidarity, but because their victory is paramount to European security.

In light of this, I applaud newly inaugurated Polish President Nawrocki's recent success in obtaining clear security guarantees from US President Donald Trump 芒聙聯 a powerful signal for the entire region.

I would like to thank you very much for raising the issue of genocide in this resolution. The Ukrainian side must finally start exhuming the murdered.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dan Barna (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, I stand before you not just as a European, but as a Romanian as well. My country shares the longest EU border with Ukraine. We are not just neighbours; we are brothers and sisters who have sheltered millions from the storms of Russian aggression. We have seen their fear in their eyes and heard the sirens first-hand.

We talk of peace, and we must. But let us be clear: a peace dictated by the aggressor is not peace, it is surrender. And a 'just peace' is not a negotiation over how much of a sovereign nation is fed to the wolves.

That peace will never hold without real ironclad security guarantees. Vague promises are an invitation for future aggression. Ukraine needs more than our resolutions. It needs our resolves.

So I ask you, look east from this Chamber. See the courage of a people fighting for their very values we represent. History will not forgive us if we answer their sacrifice without hesitation. Let our legacy be that when tyranny challenged our continent, we did not flinch. We acted. We secured a just peace.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ewa Zaj脛聟czkowska-Hernik (ESN). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Polska przyj脛聶脜聜a milion Ukrai脜聞c脙鲁w uciekaj脛聟cych przed wojn脛聟. Dali脜聸my Ukrainie nasze czo脜聜gi, transportery i samoloty. I mimo naszego gigantycznego zaanga脜录owania spotkali脜聸my si脛聶 z ogromn脛聟 niewdzi脛聶czno脜聸ci脛聟 ze strony ukrai脜聞skiej. Ukraina nie chce przeprosi脛聡, pot脛聶pi脛聡 i nazwa脛聡 ludob脙鲁jstwem rzezi wo脜聜y脜聞skiej, mord脙鲁w dokonanych na polskiej ludno脜聸ci cywilnej przez ukrai脜聞skich nacjonalist脙鲁w podczas II wojny 脜聸wiatowej.

Na Ukrainie kwitnie kult Bandery, zbrodniarza i inicjatora mord脙鲁w na Polakach. Na polskich ulicach mo脜录na spotka脛聡 mn脙鲁stwo m脜聜odych Ukrai脜聞c脙鲁w, m脛聶脜录czyzn, kt脙鲁rzy zamiast broni脛聡 swojego kraju, imprezuj脛聟 w Polsce. Nasz rynek zosta脜聜 zalany ukrai脜聞skimi produktami spo脜录ywczymi, a polskie firmy transportowe mierz脛聟 si脛聶 z nieuczciw脛聟 konkurencj脛聟 ze strony ukrai脜聞skich przewo脜潞nik脙鲁w. Czas najwy脜录szy, by Europa dowiedzia脜聜a si脛聶, jak niewdzi脛聶cznym i skorumpowanym pa脜聞stwem jest Ukraina. Zdecydowane 芒聙聻nie芒聙聺 dla obecno脜聸ci Ukrainy w Unii Europejskiej.

脗聽脗聽Sandra Kalniete (PPE). 芒聙聯 Cien脛芦t脛聛 s脛聯des vad脛芦t脛聛ja! Kol脛聯脛拢i! Vai 脜隆eit, Eiropas Parlament脛聛, ir v脛聯l k脛聛ds, kas tic, ka Putins tie脜隆脛聛m v脛聯las mieru? Ja ir, tad tie ir naivi 脛录audis vai ar脛芦 芒聙聯 Krem脛录a draugi. Krievija ir pastiprin脛聛jusi izn脛芦cino脜隆us uzbrukumus Ukrainas civiliedz脛芦vot脛聛jiem, infrastrukt脜芦rai, nesaudz脛聯jot ar脛芦 vald脛芦bas m脛芦tni.

Vien脛芦gais ce脛录脜隆 uz mieru ir turpin脛聛t palielin脛聛t spiedienu uz agresoru un pastiprin脛聛t sankcijas pret Krieviju.

Ikvienam ir j脛聛apzin脛聛s, ka Ukraina ir Eiropas dro脜隆脛芦bas garantija pret Krem脛录a imp脛聯riskajiem pl脛聛niem.

T脛聛p脛聯c ir 脛录oti svar脛芦gi turpin脛聛t apbru脜聠ot Ukrainu, nodro脜隆inot, ka Ukrainai ir sp脛聯c脛芦ga, robusta, moderna, labi apr脛芦kota un labi apm脛聛c脛芦ta armija, k脛聛 ar脛芦 att脛芦st脛芦ta milit脛聛r脛聛 industrija.

Un galvenais 芒聙聯 mums ir j脛聛nodro脜隆ina maksim脛聛ls atbalsts Ukrainas integr脛聛cijai Eirop脛聛, ir j脛聛s脛聛k Eiropas Savien脛芦bas pievieno脜隆an脛聛s sarunas. Slava Ukrainai!

脗聽脗聽Brando Benifei (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, il tappeto rosso americano per il criminale di guerra Putin ha prodotto solo altre bombe su Kiev, uccidendo civili inermi. A sentire Trump, la guerra doveva finire a 24 ore dal suo insediamento, invece siamo ancora qui a farci prendere in giro da un autocrate assassino.

Nessun cessate il fuoco 脙篓 stato raggiunto, mentre doveva essere questa la precondizione per proseguire le negoziazioni.

Basta perdere tempo, serve un passo avanti concreto nell'utilizzo degli asset russi congelati per sostenere l'Ucraina in modo che paghi davvero chi ha causato tutto questo, superando le divisioni fra i paesi europei.

Ora la comunit脙聽 internazionale si unisca nel definire condizioni giuste per una pace sostenibile e sicura, che sia rispettosa del popolo ucraino e dell'Europa, di cui l'Ucraina 脙篓 parte, e che ricostruisca un ordine internazionale fondato sul diritto e non sulle sfere di influenza e sulla prepotenza.

El脜聭sz脙露r is azt szeretn脙漏nk k脙漏rni, hogy amikor kidolgozz脙隆k az Ukrajna-politik脙隆jukat, akkor v脙漏gre vegy脙漏k figyelembe a gazdas脙隆gi k脙露vetkezm脙漏nyeket is. To vi脜聠i pa脜隆i deklar脛聯 ik pa laikam. T脛聛 k脛聛 tikai agresora bailes palikt bez 脜隆iem zobiem ir tas, kas var nodro脜隆in脛聛t dro脜隆脛芦bas garantijas gan Ukrainai, gan Eiropai. 脗聽脗聽Helmut Brandst脙陇tter (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Madam Vice芒聙聭President,脗聽dear Commissioner, Ms脗聽Kos, you rightly said that it's a strong sign that Ukraine comes to Europe. It's also a strong signal to Moscow.

But there are other signals to Moscow from the very left and from the very right. These signals are that they are parrots. They just say the same as Putin, we just heard it.

And then, Ms脗聽G脙隆l 芒聙聯 she already left 芒聙聯 talked about her boys. But you know, when Putin kills children, she shrugs her shoulders. She doesn't care.

You know, think about the children in Ukraine. They can't go to school. Think about them. They can't go to a hospital because he's also bombing hospitals. You don't care about that and we know that.

On the other hand, we know that if Ukraine flourishes, that's a great sign that being part of the European Union is a better life and also that democracy is a better life. That's also our sign to them.

One message to the people in Ukraine: there is a huge majority that are in favour of you. Don't listen to those extremists. There's a huge majority for you and we will fight for you. We will work for you. Let's work together for a better Europe.

脗聽脗聽Nicol脙隆s Pascual de la Parte (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, High Representative, I extend my congratulations to the rapporteur on the report on Ukraine. I think that it is time for the European Union to cobble together a comprehensive initiative on peace for Ukraine.

During the summer we have been attending a lot of meetings and encounters in Alaska and in Washington, and I think that the people, our public opinion outside, need to have a clear idea of what the European Union stands for with regard to Ukraine. We have to put together, to cobble together, an integral, comprehensive peace plan together with our Ukrainian friends.

The main idea is that Russia cannot win this war. We have to support our Ukrainian friends 芒聙聯 not to defend themselves, but to win the war, which is something quite different. And for that, we have to promise them a quick integration into the European Union. We have to strengthen and reinforce their armed forces and to integrate the army into our European defence infrastructure. Only with these clear messages can we set up a clear deterrence against Putin.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Javi L脙鲁pez (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, mientras la brutal invasi脙鲁n ilegal por parte de Putin y Rusia a Ucrania contin脙潞a, durante estos 脙潞ltimos meses estamos viendo una resistencia heroica de Ucrania en defensa de su integridad territorial y, tambi脙漏n, de los valores europeos.

Pero 脗驴qu脙漏 hemos visto durante este verano? Hemos visto la rehabilitaci脙鲁n de Putin en la escena internacional 芒聙聰con una alfombra roja芒聙聰, c脙鲁mo la guerra en Ucrania se sit脙潞a en los t脙漏rminos que interesan a Putin 芒聙聰ganar tiempo芒聙聰 y, en tercer lugar, c脙鲁mo Washington ha dejado de pedir el alto el fuego, que es la petici脙鲁n central que nosotros estamos haciendo como europeos. Esto es lo que hemos visto este verano.

Yo lo que les dir脙颅a, se脙卤or脙颅as, es que tenemos que acabar con nuestra posici脙鲁n de dependencia en materia de seguridad y defensa y en relaci脙鲁n a Ucrania. 脗驴Por qu脙漏? Porque sin un nuevo papel aut脙鲁nomo, sin una autonom脙颅a estrat脙漏gica y propia de la Uni脙鲁n Europea, no vamos a poder defender nuestra seguridad, nuestra democracia y el futuro de Ucrania.

Lo que reclamamos es reforzar y mejorar la competitividad de nuestra industria, fortalecer nuestras democracias y redoblar el apoyo a Ucrania, porque la seguridad de su futuro no puede depender de la vanidad de l脙颅deres extranjeros.

脗聽脗聽Petra Steger (PfE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, sehr geehrte Damen und Herren! Frau Kallas, ich muss Sie genauso wie Frau von der Leyen ernsthaft fragen, ob Sie mittlerweile wirklich von allen guten Geistern verlassen sind. Was wollen Sie eigentlich bezwecken? Wollen Sie Europa mitten in den Krieg ziehen, ist das Ihr Ziel? Sie wollen die Ukraine aufnehmen脗聽芒聙聯 ein Kriegsland, ernsthaft?

Sie sprechen von 脙录ber 10脗聽000 Mann f脙录r sogenannte Friedenstruppen, sprich Bodentruppen, die nicht nur einmal in der Vergangenheit von Russland als Provokation bezeichnet wurden; Truppen, von denen offenbar nicht einmal irgendwer etwas wissen will, weder der deutsche Verteidigungsminister noch US-Pr脙陇sident Trump. Ihre angeblichen Friedenstruppen sind genauso real wie der russische Angriff auf das GPS-System von Frau von der Leyens Flugzeug oder Ihre F脙录hrungsmacht auf der Welt脗聽芒聙聯 reine Fake News.

Zuerst lassen Sie sich bei den Zollverhandlungen 脙录ber den Tisch ziehen, und dann sitzen Sie auch noch in Washington vor der T脙录r, wenn die Staatschefs die wichtigen Gespr脙陇che f脙录hren. Wissen Sie eigentlich, was Sie f脙录r ein Bild auf der Welt abgeben, Frau von der Leyen genauso wie Sie, Frau Kallas? Auch in Zukunft脗聽芒聙聯 das kann ich Ihnen jetzt schon prophezeien脗聽芒聙聯 werden Sie bei Gespr脙陇chen oder potenziellen Friedensverhandlungen drau脙聼en vor der T脙录r sitzen. Warum? Weil Sie keiner mehr ernst nimmt, und das haben Sie allein sich und Ihrer Politik zu verdanken.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alexandr Vondra (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, ladies and gentlemen, efforts to draw Vladimir Putin to the negotiation table led nowhere. Our experience proves that Russia understands only one language, and that is power, not the nice talks which we have here.

Therefore, we must follow two goals at the same time. Firstly, arming Ukraine and rearming ourselves. With the EU machinery, any project can become a bureaucratic nightmare. We must make the development, production and distribution of weaponry in the EU as simple as possible.脗聽Don't forget, our enemy is not environmentally considerate.

Secondly, keep the relations with the US. Gigantic fines of Google will not improve our relationship. In this sense, a pragmatic Maro脜隆 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧 is doing more sensible work than the ideologue Teresa Ribera.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hilde Vautmans (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, it is very good that there is a coalition of the willing who want to provide Ukraine with security guarantees, but let us not get ahead of ourselves. Europe has increased its military help for Ukraine, but it is far from enough. Last week we all saw Russia launch one of its deadliest attacks on Ukraine.

The Russian economy is not collapsing as predicted. Our sanctions, colleagues, don't bite hard enough. We are now on our 19th sanctions package. We are slow. The implementation remains weak and we lack a central authority for enforcement. We are also 芒聙聯 and the Commissioner and Kaja know this 芒聙聯 too soft on third countries and companies who still supply Russia with chips and other precision tools that allow them to build strategic weapons.脗聽So please, colleagues, let us strengthen Ukraine to the point where they can win the peace.

Commissioner Kos, I really want to say I appreciate your efforts for脗聽enlargement, but I didn't hear anything on the reform of Europe, so please...

(The President cut off the speaker)

脗聽脗聽Isabel Wiseler-Lima (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, trois ans et demi. Trois ans et demi que les Ukrainiens sont r脙漏veill脙漏s, nuit apr脙篓s nuit, par les sir脙篓nes. Trois ans et demi qu'ils enterrent leurs morts, tomb脙漏s sous les coups de l'arm脙漏e russe. Une guerre men脙漏e contre l'Ukraine de mani脙篓re tout 脙聽 fait injustifi脙漏e, sans qu'il n'y ait aucune provocation. Nous n'oublierons jamais les horreurs de Boutcha.

Nous continuons 脙聽 demander que les prisonniers civils soient lib脙漏r脙漏s, que les enfants d脙漏port脙漏s reviennent en Ukraine. Nous avons d脙漏cid脙漏 de soutenir les Ukrainiens aussi longtemps qu'il le faudra, et c'est le minimum que nous puissions faire, non seulement parce que nous devons cette aide 脙聽 nos voisins, qui ont d脙漏cid脙漏 qu'ils voulaient vivre libres et en d脙漏mocratie, comme nous, mais aussi parce qu'ils sont notre rempart contre un r脙漏gime autoritaire qui prof脙篓re des menaces militaires contre nous et m脙篓ne une guerre hybride bien r脙漏elle et dangereuse contre nos d脙漏mocraties.

Nous devons donc apporter tout le soutien militaire possible 脙聽 l'Ukraine, intensifier les sanctions 脙聽 l'脙漏gard de la Russie et aussi accompagner les efforts de l'Ukraine pour l'accueillir, comme nous l'avons promis, dans l'Union europ脙漏enne.

脗聽脗聽Maria Guzenina (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, last weekend, Russia launched 818 drones against Ukraine. Meanwhile, President Putin is laughing in Moscow.

President Trump sends mixed signals: one day promising peace; the next, praising the leader of Russia.

In Beijing, President Xi gave Putin yet again a global stage and Europe were absent.脗聽Peace talks are shaped by others.

The days when trade power gave us leverage is over. This has given Putin a clear table to push harder and further crush Ukraine's fight for freedom.

As said in the report, Ukraine is the single most important area of EU activity in the external relations realm.

Madam脗聽President, if Europe is not at the table, we are on the menu.

脗聽脗聽Vikt脙鲁ria Ferenc (PfE). 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k Asszony! Fontos, hogy a mai vita c脙颅me a b脙漏ke sz脙鲁 eml脙颅t脙漏s脙漏vel v脙漏gre t脙录kr脙露zi azt, ami az embereknek fontos. Ugyanis az Eur脙鲁p脙隆ban 脙漏s az Ukrajn脙隆ban 脙漏l脜聭 emberek is b脙漏k脙漏t szeretn脙漏nek. Diszkriminat脙颅v b脙隆n脙隆sm脙鲁dban r脙漏szes脙录lnek hat脙隆r脙隆tkel脙漏skor, s mindez egy Uni脙鲁ba t脙露rekv脜聭 orsz脙隆gban t脙露rt脙漏nik.

Ez elfogadhatatlan. Magyarorsz脙隆g tiszteletben tartja az Eur脙鲁pai Uni脙鲁 csatlakoz脙隆si felt脙漏teleit, a nemzeti kisebbs脙漏gek jogait 脙漏s a b脙漏k脙漏t szorgalmazza. Ez 脙聳n脙露kt脜聭l is joggal elv脙隆rhat脙鲁. 脗聽脗聽Bernard Guetta (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, rien n'est fait, h脙漏las, rien n'est jou脙漏, mais il semble pourtant bien que ses yeux s'ouvrent. D脙漏脙搂u, dit-il, par Vladimir脗聽Poutine et surtout inquiet du resserrement des liens entre la Chine et la Russie, Donald脗聽Trump para脙庐t enfin comprendre脗聽芒聙聯脗聽commencer 脙聽 comprendre脗聽芒聙聯 qu'il a besoin d'alli脙漏s pour faire face 脙聽 l'entente sino-russe.

Nous verrons si ce pr脙漏sident passe maintenant 脙聽 l'acte, mais une chose est s脙禄re: depuis l'humiliation subie par Volodymyr脗聽Zelensky dans le bureau ovale, nous avons, nous, l'Union europ脙漏enne, resserr脙漏 nos rangs, fait front avec la Grande-Bretagne et arrim脙漏 脙聽 notre bataille pour la libert脙漏 le Canada, l'Australie, le Japon et bien d'autres d脙漏mocraties de taille. Nous instaurons de nouveaux rapports de force qui affirment un nouveau p脙麓le de puissance sur la sc脙篓ne internationale.

Mais tout reste 脙聽 faire. Le martyre de l'Ukraine et notre propre s脙漏curit脙漏 exigent de nous bien plus encore.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脜陆eljana Zovko (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, po脜隆tovana povjerenice, 脜戮ao mi je 脜隆to nije ovdje visoka predstavnica Kaja Kallas, ona je na kolegiju. 脜陆elim pozdraviti ministra pravde Bosne i Hercegovine koji je ovdje s nama i govori o pro脜隆irenju i u stvari o potrebi da se sa脛聧uva Bosna i Hercegovina u ovom trenutku.

Ukrajina je problem s kojim se susre脛聡emo i s kojim 脛聡emo se susretati u budu脛聡nosti jer odjednom nema interesa s lijeve strane da budu ovdje. Kada je agresija po脜隆la na Ukrajinu ovdje je bila puna lijeva strana spektra, a sada ih nema. Gdje su oni?

脛聦estitam Michaelu Gahleru koji se toliko godina bori i sada kona脛聧no imamo Izvje脜隆脛聡e o Ukrajini, ali 脛聧injenica jest - pro脜隆irenje je jedini na脛聧in obrane Europe.

Jedini na脛聧in obrane Europe, i svim mojim kolegama koji su ovdje govorili da mi moramo promijeniti neku taktiku sa Sjedinjenim Ameri脛聧kim Dr脜戮avama, to nije to脛聧no. Bez Amerike ni Hrvatska nije mogla biti spa脜隆ena. Hrvatska se sama obranila, bez Amerike...脗聽

(Predsjedavaju脛聡a je govornici oduzela rije脛聧)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tonino Picula (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Madam Commissioner and colleagues, when our peace efforts are responded to with drones and missile attacks on civilian infrastructure, government buildings and the EU representation office, the conclusion should be clear to all: there is no genuine will from Putin to talk or act towards peace.

While discussions on security guarantees are welcomed, there are more concrete actions we can do now: bolder decisions related to immobilised Russian state assets, energy independence from Russia, and to pursue it in general, to work on a genuine and predictable partnership globally to support international law and Ukraine.

Apart from military and economic support, we need sufficient assistance for Ukraine to strengthen its judiciary and the rule of law, while fighting corruption, as rightfully underlined by the report. Progress in these areas is vital for Ukraine now.

By defending itself, Ukraine is defending Europe. And by strengthening itself, the European Union can better support Ukraine. Slava Ukraini!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mika Aaltola (PPE). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, arvoisat komission j脙陇senet, Thukydides, kreikkalainen historioitsija, totesi aikoinaan, ett脙陇 sodan eskalaation ajurit ovat nousevan,脗聽nousukkaan脗聽suurvallan yli-itsevarmuus ja sen muissa aiheuttama pelko. Kun Putin katsoo meit脙陇 silmiin, h脙陇n n脙陇kee meiss脙陇 pelkoa. H脙陇n n脙陇kee hajanaisen l脙陇nnen ja h脙陇n uhittelee.

Eilen Ven脙陇j脙陇n turvallisuusneuvoston varapuheenjohtaja, entinen presidentti, uhkaili Suomea sodalla. N脙陇m脙陇 eiv脙陇t ole leikin asioita ja voisi sanoa, ett脙陇 olemme yhden h脙陇vityn sodan p脙陇脙陇ss脙陇 isosta eurooppalaisesta sodasta. Meid脙陇n on pakko mobilisoitua. Euroopan historia on n脙陇hnyt koalitioita tahtovien koalitioita Iberian niemimaan vapauttamisesta Wienin porteille ja Suureen Pohjan sotaan. Nyt on t脙陇rke脙陇脙陇, ett脙陇 t脙陇ss脙陇 mobilisaatiossa me kerrankin olisimme ajoissa liikkeell脙陇 ja onnistuisimme.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Massimiliano Salini (PPE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, alcuni analisti hanno utilizzato una metafora efficace per descrivere lo scontro tra Ucraina e Russia: quello delle clessidre, una gara tra clessidre.

La clessidra russa 脙篓 una clessidra economica: il tempo avverso alla Russia, il tempo dell'economia di guerra che non dovrebbe reggere per pi脙鹿 di 18-24 mesi, si dice.

Spetta a noi il compito di rallentare, anzi in questo caso di velocizzare, il tempo della scadenza del flusso di quella clessidra, con sanzioni vere, pi脙鹿 forti, soprattutto secondarie, per evitare l'elusione delle sanzioni che fino ad ora abbiamo applicato.

Poi c'脙篓 la clessidra ucraina, che 脙篓 militare. Anche in questo caso, il nostro compito 脙篓 quello di intervenire fornendo gli strumenti perch脙漏 si rallenti il corso di quel tempo negativo per l'Ucraina, consapevoli che l'azione ucraina, difendendo l'Ucraina, difende l'Unione europea; cos脙卢 come l'attacco russo, attaccando l'Ucraina, attacca l'Unione europea.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Davor Ivo Stier (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, once again we need to stress that it was Putin who started this war of aggression against Ukraine, and it's Putin who doesn't want to end it. The attacks on Kyiv, including the government building and the EU delegation, are the clearest reminder.

The fact that Putin fiercely opposes the deployment of Western peacekeeping forces, even after a deal is reached, shows everyone that his intention is not to stop, but to carry out his plan of total submission of Ukraine into the so-called Russian world. And for that, obviously he has the support of China, since Beijing wants a multi-polar world based on spheres of influence dominated by regional hegemony. The initial attempt of the Trump administration to distance Moscow from Beijing has not borne fruit, and we should now continue to work with Washington on the careful design of secondary sanctions.

Finally, it is imperative for Kyiv to avoid any back-pedalling on European values and standards, and for us in Europe it's imperative to unblock the EU accession negotiations with Ukraine.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matej Tonin (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear Commissioners, dear colleagues, it seems that we are waiting for the war in Ukraine to end on its own, or for President Trump or the Pope to bring it to an end. None of this has happened.脗聽The war will end when Putin realises that he has nothing to gain on the battlefield.

That is the reason for our unwavering support for Ukraine. Our support for Ukraine is the path towards peace. History teaches us a clear lesson: an aggressor does not stop by choice, but only when confronted with limits.

The unity of this House is more important today than ever. Europe is stronger when we stand together. Our response must be clear. Putin will not stop on his own. Only together we can stop it.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Liudas Ma脜戮ylis (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pirmininke, n脛聴 minut脛聴s netik脛聴jau agresori脜鲁 rus脜鲁 gera valia ir kad jie panor脛聴s taikos. Ne, tikrai ne. Mums lieka tik ginkluoti Ukrain脛聟, ginkluotis patiems, pajungiant moksl脛聟 ir pa脜戮angiausias technologijas, finansus, nusiteikti hibridiniam karui. M脜芦s脜鲁 pasirinktas sankcij脜鲁 paketo metodas tur脛聴jo suveikti daug anks脛聧iau. Depozitus b脜芦tina konfiskuoti, naryst脛聴s deryb脜鲁 neatid脛聴lioti. O dabar agresoriaus 脜戮iaurumas did脛聴ja de脜隆imteriopai. Norma jau dabar nebe 600 dron脜鲁, o 6000. Tiek kraujo dar nebuvo. Terminas 芒聙聻Realpolitik芒聙聹 kei脛聧ia prasm脛聶 ir rei脜隆kia grubi脛聟 realyb脛聶. Kraujo kaina 芒聙聯 lemtingai perlau脜戮ti kar脛聟. Nesame karo kurstytojai, bet agresorius mus ver脛聧ia vaikus nuo ma脜戮ens mokyti konstruoti dronus ir dron脜鲁 u脜戮dangas. Sakydami r脜芦s脛聧i脛聟 teisyb脛聶 b脜芦tume bent jau s脛聟脜戮iningi. Slava Ukraini!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alvise P脙漏rez (NI). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidente, yo ya suelo evitar venir a este tipo de debates sobre Ucrania porque me resulta muy inc脙鲁modo escuchar las opiniones de tant脙颅simas marionetas.

脗驴Podemos hablar con cierta sinceridad por una vez? 脗驴Podemos decir en voz alta mir脙隆ndonos los unos a los otros que los muertos en Ucrania nos dan exactamente igual? Igual que nos importan absolutamente nada los 5脗聽000脗聽cristianos asesinados en 脙聛frica, en un pa脙颅s de los muchos que est脙隆n en guerra.

Al final, de lo que tenemos que hablar es de que aqu脙颅 somos marionetas de poderes m脙隆s all脙隆 de Europa. Somos marionetas y nos vestimos de traje y corbata y nos sentamos en estos 720脗聽esca脙卤os como si pint脙隆ramos algo.

No, no, esa es la verdad. Al final, 脗芦Von der Lucifer脗禄, 脗驴ante qui脙漏n se arrodilla? Ante Estados Unidos. 脗驴Bases militares de Estados Unidos en Europa? Treinta y ocho, cientos de ojivas nucleares en Europa. Somos, literalmente, esclavos de intereses estadounidenses; que no es que lo critique, digo 脙潞nica y exclusivamente que hablemos con sinceridad, porque ya que hemos sido elegidos por tant脙颅simos europeos, que menos que, por una vez, digamos las cosas claras, 脗驴no?

脗聽脗聽Vytenis Povilas Andriukaitis (S&D). 芒聙聯 Pos脛聴d脜戮io pirmininke, gerbiamos komisar脛聴s, kolegos. Kai klausausi 脜隆ioje sal脛聴je pasisakym脜鲁 i脜隆 t脜鲁 atstov脜鲁, kurie save 脛炉vardija patriotais, nenorom prisimenu sentencij脛聟: patriotizmas da脜戮nai b脜芦na paskutin脛聴 niek脜隆脜鲁 prieglauda. Ar taip save vadinantys patriotai kartais nesuvokia 脜隆ios sentencijos prasm脛聴s? O ypa脛聧 pranc脜芦z脜鲁 patriotai gal脛聴t脜鲁 atsisukti 脛炉 istorij脛聟 ir paklausti sav脛聶s, kokia taika su Hitleriu? 脛庐domi b脜芦t脜鲁 istorin脛聴 pamoka pranc脜芦z脜鲁 patriotams? Ir d脛聴l to atsakymas labai akivaizdus. 脜聽iandien, be kita ko, mes minime 50-met脛炉 Helsinkio baigiamojo akto, kuris labai ai脜隆kiai pasak脛聴: sien脜鲁 nelie脛聧iamumas, teritorinis integralumas ir j脛聴gos nenaudojimas. Putinas privalo b脜芦ti nugal脛聴tas! Tai vienintelis kelias.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinier Van Lanschot (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, while Trump rolled out the red carpet for Putin in Alaska, the largest air strike on Ukrainian civilians happened. It seems that the more we talk about peace, the more aggressive Putin becomes. The more the US bows, the more bombs he throws, and the more we are paralysed, the more he prolongs this war.

We want this war to end as soon as possible and to prevent it from ever restarting. So make sure that Putin realises that he will not gain anything.

Conversations about security guarantees are good, but should never become a substitute for making Ukraine win, and the coalition of the willing should never become a substitute for building a true European Defence Union.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Siegbert Frank Droese (ESN). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Die sehr, sehr Hohe Vertreterin kommt zu dieser wichtigen Debatte erstmal zehn Minuten zu sp脙陇t: auch eine Missachtung dieses Scheinparlaments. Was Kallas zu berichten wei脙聼, ist ziemlich viel gequirlter Mist. Das einzig Richtige, was sie sagte: Der Ukraine-Krieg ist nicht zu gewinnen. Richtig, die Ukraine kann diesen Krieg nicht gewinnen. Warum berichtet Kallas nicht davon, wie das korrupte Regime der Ukraine unsere Infrastruktur angreift?

Und wenn der Berichterstatter Gahler, CDU, vom Endsieg Russlands redet, dann scheint das tief aus seinem Bewusstsein zu kommen, endlich an Russland Revanche zu nehmen f脙录r die Ergebnisse des Zweiten Weltkrieges. Das ist widerlich, Kollege Gahler, das ist widerlich, sch脙陇men Sie sich!

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Ich bitte Sie, demn脙陇chst Respekt vor diesem Haus zu haben. Dieses Haus ist die Vertretung der B脙录rgerinnen und B脙录rger Europas und kein Scheinparlament.

脗聽脗聽Diana Iovanovici 脜聻o脜聼oac脛聝 (NI). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, cred c脛聝 nu a脠聸i 脙庐n脠聸eles c脛聝 sferele de influen脠聸脛聝 s-au schimbat 脠聶i polii de putere sunt al脠聸ii. Cred c脛聝 nu a脠聸i 脙庐n脠聸eles c脛聝 Uniunea European脛聝 a r脛聝mas singur脛聝 脠聶i s-a format o cu totul alt脛聝 putere la nivel mondial, cu toate acele 脠聸脛聝ri pe care dumneavoastr脛聝 le numi脠聸i dictaturi, 脙庐n timp ce 脙庐n Europa se instaureaz脛聝 cea mai cumplit脛聝 dictatur脛聝.

Prin Rom脙垄nia au trecut 脠聶i trebuiau s脛聝 treac脛聝 vreo 270脗聽de脗聽miliarde de dolari din Statele Unite ale Americii c脛聝tre Ucraina. 脠聵ti脠聸i ce s-a 脙庐nt脙垄mplat 脙庐n timpul prim-mini脠聶trilor Ciuc脛聝 脠聶i Ciolacu, pe care i-a脠聸i sus脠聸inut? 100脗聽de脗聽miliarde de dolari au disp脛聝rut 脠聶i sunt implica脠聸i 脙庐n furtul lor nu numai prim-mini脠聶trii rom脙垄ni, dar 脠聶i personalit脛聝脠聸i din conducerea Uniunii Europene. 脠聵i nimeni nu v脛聝 spune asta.

Rom脙垄nii nu sunt de acord s脛聝 murim pe front pentru Ucraina, dar s脛聝 脠聶ti脠聸i c脛聝 avem 脠聶i noi acolo un milion de rom脙垄ni care sunt asupri脠聸i 脠聶i discrimina脠聸i de regimul dictatorial (...)

(Pre脠聶edinta a retras cuv脙垄ntul vorbitoarei)

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, alta representante Kallas, hay dos debates esta ma脙卤ana en el pleno sobre Ucrania y Gaza, conflictos de causalidad y naturaleza muy distintas, pero con un hilo conductor que denuncia la impotencia que amenaza la irrelevancia y la hipocres脙颅a de la pol脙颅tica exterior de la Uni脙鲁n Europea.

La 脗芦coalici脙鲁n de los dispuestos脗禄 es un subrogado para la ausencia de unanimidad, lo que significa que no existe una posici脙鲁n de la Uni脙鲁n Europea, y parte de esa coalici脙鲁n integra a pa脙颅ses que ya no est脙隆n en la Uni脙鲁n Europea, como el Reino Unido.

Lo m脙隆s grave no es que la premisa sobre la seguridad de Ucrania requiera una paz que est脙隆 muy lejos de la vista, ni que Putin diga, con claridad, que la implicaci脙鲁n de la Uni脙鲁n Europea en la seguridad de Ucrania no se traduce en seguridad para Ucrania sino en inseguridad para el conjunto de la Uni脙鲁n Europea. Lo m脙隆s grave es que, en los escenarios en los que todo esto se discute, la Uni脙鲁n Europea no est脙隆 ni se la espera, en flagrante traici脙鲁n a la promesa proclamada de su autonom脙颅a estrat脙漏gica.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alexander Sell (ESN). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! 169脗聽Milliarden Euro hat die EU in den vergangenen drei Jahren nach Kyjiw 脙录berwiesen, Frau Kallas hat es gerade gesagt. Frau Kallas hat au脙聼erdem angek脙录ndigt, dass Europa ab 2027 kein russisches Gas mehr importieren werde. Was Sie allerdings nicht erw脙陇hnt haben, Frau Kallas, sind die Konsequenzen Ihrer Politik.

In Deutschland geht n脙陇mlich gerade die Chemieindustrie kaputt, und zwar infolge von Ihrer Politik. Infolge von viel zu hohen Energiepreisen gibt es Massenentlassungen bei Bayer, BASF usw. Tausende Familien in Leverkusen, in Ludwigshafen, in Bitterfeld werden ihrer Existenzgrundlage beraubt, und zwar wegen Ihrer Politik. Mit vollen H脙陇nden geben Sie Geld aus, was eigentlich gar nicht mehr vorhanden ist.

Gestern ist in Frankreich die Regierung geplatzt, weil sie sich nicht auf einen Sparhaushalt einigen konnten. Die Bundesregierung in Deutschland sucht h脙陇nderingend nach neuen Einnahmequellen. Der Bundeskanzler sagt, die Deutschen m脙录ssten mehr arbeiten, die Deutschen sollten erst mit 70 in Rente gehen. Der Sozialstaat sei nicht mehr finanzierbar, sagt Friedrich Merz.

Diese Abgehobenheit hier in diesem Haus in Stra脙聼burg, aber auch in Berlin, 脙录berrascht mich jedes Mal aufs Neue. Die Missachtung, Ihre Missachtung f脙录r die Menschen, die das alles hier finanzieren m脙录ssen, das ist der Grund, weshalb meine Partei in den Umfragen so stark ist. Der Krug geht so lange zum Brunnen, bis er bricht.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Ende der spontanen Wortmeldungen)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Kos, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members, today's debate has proven support to Ukraine's path to the European Union, even while challenges remain.

What distinguishes Ukraine is its determination to confront these challenges, even while fighting for its very survival. We have seen that Ukraine is willing to take the difficult decisions needed to stay on course.

At the same time, we cannot forget that Ukraine's accession is not only about reform, it is also a strong security guarantee for Ukraine and for Europe. Each step forward is a step that makes our Union safer, stronger and more complete.

This House, the Commission and the Council must remain united. We must send a clear message: Ukraine's European destiny is irreversible. Ukrainians are defending our shared values with their lives. The least we can do is rise to the occasion with political courage. Ukraine's future is in Europe and together we must ensure that the future becomes reality.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael Gahler, Berichterstatter. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Eine Vorbemerkung: Ich finde, es ist eine Schande, wenn es nach zwei gescheiterten deutschen Diktaturen immer noch Leute gibt hier im Hause, die sich einer dritten Diktatur andienen. Sch脙陇men Sie sich!

We could note the usual coalition of the willing of five political groups and additional individual Members that send a clear message: Ukraine's future is in the EU. On the way, we are supportive of the reform process and as Putin does not want peace, not even a ceasefire, we agree to demand from our governments to strengthen Ukraine's ability to defend itself. Everyone must deliver what they can provide.

For the German part, to fulfil their task, we should start training Ukrainians on the Taurus missile and deliver it as soon as possible to make a difference, hitting production sites of weapons, launch sites of missiles and important logistical infrastructure that transport the lethal weapons and soldiers to the frontline. Contrary to certain claims, no German soldiers are needed to install it all.

And there is one aspect that has not been addressed, and I think that is one of the motivations why the Ukrainians continue to fight and we should support them: it is about the Ukrainians, millions of them, who live in the Russian occupation zone. Their daily fate is incredible: torture, arrests, no rule of law at all. I think that is the population that is at risk especially, and that is why this motivation is so strong to regain these occupied territories. It's about the people and not about the territory that is in the focus, and I think we have all reason to support Ukraine in these efforts as well.

And in so far, I thank you once again, dear colleagues from these five groups and additional Members, for this solidarity that we have pursued over the years. I am confident that we will succeed with our efforts. Thank you very much.

Once again, slava Ukraini!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kaja Kallas, Vice-President of the Commission / High Representative of the Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, I want to thank the European Parliament for your unwavering support for Ukraine and its people. They are living very hard times and you have been with them for three and a half years, so that is also reflected in Mr Gahler's report.

Your commitment has been crucial not only for Ukraine, but also in defending the very principles that we as Europeans stand for. And we have stood united in these very turbulent times, so thank you for that.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The debate is closed.
