脗聽脗聽Urmas Paet (Renew), kirjalikult. 芒聙聯 Ukraina on ELi v脙陇lissuhete valdkonna k脙碌ige olulisem tegevusvaldkond. Ukrainas j脙陇tkuv s脙碌da on juba avaldanud ELile s脙录gavat m脙碌ju ja teeb seda ka edaspidi. Selle s脙碌ja tulemus on ELi jaoks eksistentsiaalse t脙陇htsusega ning EL peab andma endast k脙碌ik, et tagada Ukraina v脙碌it agressorriik Venemaa vastu. Seep脙陇rast on EL ja selle liikmesriigid andnud Ukrainale j脙陇rjepidevat poliitilist, diplomaatilist, rahalist ja s脙碌jalist abi. Praeguse Ameerika 脙聹hendriikide administratsiooni j脙陇rsk suunamuutus muudab ELi vastutuse veelgi suuremaks. Euroopa Komisjonil tuleks avada l脙陇bir脙陇脙陇kimisklastrid, edendamaks Ukraina ELiga 脙录hinemist v脙碌imalikult kiires tempos. Selle protsessi edukas l脙碌puleviimine annab tunnistust juhtrollist, otsusekindlusest ja etten脙陇gelikkusest ning see on 脙录ha muutlikumas rahvusvahelises keskkonnas geostrateegiline investeering 脙录htsesse ja tugevasse Euroopasse.

脗聽

3. Gaza at breaking point: EU action to combat famine, the urgent need to release hostages and move towards a two-state solution (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache 脙录ber die Erkl脙陇rung der Vizepr脙陇sidentin der Kommission/Hohen Vertreterin der Union f脙录r Au脙聼en- und Sicherheitspolitik zu dem Thema 芒聙聻Der Gazastreifen am Rande der Belastbarkeit脗聽芒聙聯 Ma脙聼nahmen der EU zur Bek脙陇mpfung der Hungersnot sowie dringend notwendige Freilassung der Geiseln und Umsetzung der Zweistaatenl脙露sung芒聙聹 (2025/2852(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kaja Kallas, Vice-President of the Commission / High Representative of the Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable members of the European Parliament, unlike in Ukraine, the humanitarian catastrophe in Gaza is testing Europe's resolve, because we are not united.

Europe has not sat on its hands. Europe has been the most active actor for Gaza in the world. We are the largest humanitarian donor and a constant supporter of the Palestinian Authority and the two-state solution. We have helped more than any other international actor.

Day in and day out, I've pushed for aid to reach those in desperate need and I have applied the political leverage we have. I negotiated directly with my counterpart to reach humanitarian understanding with Israel, with specific benchmarks, because earlier efforts had not resulted in any change.

For example, I tasked our Special Representative for the Middle East Peace Process to work with the External Action Service and DG ECHO to engage directly with Israel to open the gates of Gaza for humanitarian assistance. Not a single truck entered the Gaza Strip from March to July. Since our humanitarian understanding with Israel, there have been some positive developments: 2904 trucks entered between 10 July and 1 September, the reopening of more crossing points, the reopening of the Jordanian and Egyptian routes, an increasing the amount of fuel entering Gaza 芒聙聯 the minimum agreed 196脗聽000 litres was reached in mid-August. However, we see fluctuations in quantities entering the strip in the last two weeks.脗聽On structural interventions, repairs to vital infrastructure have improved water access, and the South Gaza desalination plant is also operational. However, three main water pipelines suffer recurrent damages due to ongoing hostilities.脗聽And, in August, a ship carrying 1200 tonnes of aid departed from Limassol, Cyprus, and further shipments will continue. I would like to thank Cyprus in particular for their efforts.脗聽I agree with you: it is not enough. It is not enough. We need to do more.

I also have led a full review of Israel's compliance with Article 2 of our Association Agreement. We drew up an inventory of possible options to pressure the Israeli Government to change its course of action. The Commission then followed with a proposal to suspend parts of Horizon, but there is no required support for that yet, and I note that this is a qualified majority decision.

While there was support for these steps, there is no unity on what should come next. Now we have a statement of famine in northern Gaza. There are major safety and security concerns for international humanitarian staff, which hinder efforts to distribute aid. Trucks are looted and aid is not distributed properly. War continues, including now in Gaza City.脗聽And so I ask you, what more can we do?

Honourable Members, I believe we must continue diplomatic efforts with the Israeli Government, because we cannot get anywhere if we do not talk. In my contacts with the Foreign Minister, I have consistently raised the restrictive requirements for international NGOs to register. He has informed me that the deadline for registration was now extended to December.

Now how we uphold the European values internationally is a collective responsibility that demands a collective European response. On my side, I can reassure you that I leave no stone unturned in my efforts to make progress. I will continue this way.

Our options for more action are clear and remain on the table, but Member States disagree on how to get the Israeli Government to change course. That is why several Member States have now taken national measures in response to the actions of the Israeli Government. But we cannot move as a union until Member States share the same view on what to do. This is not the time to point fingers, but to unite for solutions. And there are many places where we do agree. We all agree on the diagnosis and on the goals, and the suffering and the conflict, and securing the release of all hostages.

Yesterday's attack in Jerusalem underlines the incredible dangers facing both Palestinians and Israelis. Aggression breeds more aggression and radicalisation. If there was a military resolution, the war in Gaza would have long been over.脗聽Now we must summon the political will to agree on a European way forward, and I call on the European Parliament's support in this endeavour. Member States actions are crucial, but only our unity can have amplified effect.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Antonio L脙鲁pez-Ist脙潞riz White, en nombre del Grupo PPE. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora alta representante, la situaci脙鲁n que estamos viviendo en Gaza e Israel es insostenible y requiere de este Parlamento una respuesta firme, serena y responsable.

La prioridad debe ser siempre la protecci脙鲁n de las vidas humanas y el respeto al Derecho internacional. No podemos permitir que el sufrimiento de las v脙颅ctimas se convierta en un instrumento de confrontaci脙鲁n pol脙颅tica dentro de la Uni脙鲁n Europea, como sucede y est脙隆 sucediendo y lo veremos en este Parlamento en la Resoluci脙鲁n, desgraciadamente.

Mientras un atentado terrorista en Jerusal脙漏n arrebat脙鲁 la vida a seis civiles 芒聙聰entre ellos un ciudadano espa脙卤ol芒聙聰, no solo se condenaba, sino que, adem脙隆s, en mi pa脙颅s se anunciaban medidas que, lejos de favorecer la paz, corren el riesgo de debilitar la lucha contra el terrorismo y de aumentar la inestabilidad de la regi脙鲁n.

Ya no puedo negar, como hac脙颅a anta脙卤o, pecando tal vez de cierta ingenuidad, que no hay antisemitismo en Europa, cuando veo a ciudadanos jud脙颅os que nada tienen que ver con las pol脙颅ticas del Gobierno de Israel perseguidos en la Vuelta ciclista de mi pa脙颅s, en bares, en restaurantes o en aviones. Se me hiela la sangre. Empiezo a ver que la historia se repite.

Nuestro deber aqu脙颅 no es defender a Gobiernos ni legitimar narrativas unilaterales: nuestro deber es defender a las personas, a los rehenes que permanecen en manos de Ham脙隆s, a los ni脙卤os y las mujeres que sufren en Gaza y a quienes en Israel y Cisjordania viven bajo el miedo constante a la violencia.

La Uni脙鲁n Europea tiene una responsabilidad de actuar como mediador honesto, con coherencia, unidad y determinaci脙鲁n. La paz, la dignidad y la prosperidad para israel脙颅es y palestinos no ser脙隆n posibles si no dejamos atr脙隆s los discursos de odio y apostamos por la reconciliaci脙鲁n y la convivencia.

脦聭脦鲁脦卤脧聙脦路脧聞脦驴脦炉 脧聝脧聟脦陆脦卢脦麓脦碌脦禄脧聠脦驴脦鹿, 脧聞脦路脦陆 脦聽脦颅脦录脧聙脧聞脦路 脧聙脧聛脦颅脧聙脦碌脦鹿 脦陆脦卤 脦卤脧聙脦驴脦麓脦碌脦炉脦戮脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌 脧聦脧聞脦鹿 脧聣脧聜 脦聲脧聟脧聛脧聨脧聙脦路 脧聝脧聞脦路脧聛脦炉脦露脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌 脧聞脦驴 脦麓脦鹿脦碌脦赂脦陆脦颅脧聜 脦麓脦炉脦潞脦卤脦鹿脦驴 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脧聞脦卤 脦卤脦陆脦赂脧聛脧聨脧聙脦鹿脦陆脦卤 脦麓脦鹿脦潞脦卤脦鹿脧聨脦录脦卤脧聞脦卤 脧聙脦卤脦陆脧聞脦驴脧聧. 脦職脧聛脦炉脦陆脦碌脧聞脦卤脦鹿 脦路 脦卤脦戮脦鹿脦驴脧聙脦鹿脧聝脧聞脦炉脦卤 脦录脦卤脧聜, 脦潞脧聛脦炉脦陆脦碌脧聞脦卤脦鹿 脦路 脦卤脦陆脦赂脧聛脧聣脧聙脦鹿脦卢 脦录脦卤脧聜.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ant脙鲁nio T脙垄nger Corr脙陋a, on behalf of the PfE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Ms脗聽Kallas, while everybody speaks about Israel and the surrounding problems, today I'm going to speak about Hamas 芒聙聯 the other side 芒聙聯 because the conflict has two sides.

Hamas is a terrorist organisation recognised by all countries represented here. Hamas must be held accountable if it refuses to release hostages and shoot them at point blank in a cold way, refuses to disarm and deliberately steals or blocks food aid meant for civilians.

Hamas is not a partner for peace, it is an obstacle to peace. All countries, nations or institutions that provide Hamas with manpower, weapons and money should be considered as terrorism supporters.

And let's say one thing here: if they want the war to end tomorrow, they just release the hostages and put the weapons down, and in 24 hours, there will be no more war in Gaza, I can guarantee you.

The other thing is recognising the Palestinian states, very nice. What are you going to recognise? The Gaza Strip? The West Bank, where the most corrupt government takes over and they don't have geopolitical continuity? They hate each other. Abu Mazen is hated as Netanyahu by the Palestinians.

Now, one thing is that if you want to create a state, you must have elections, you must have a parliament and you must have a government. And who's going to be the government? Hamas or its proxies? I leave this thought with you.

The other thing is Jordan. 60-70脗聽% of the people living in Jordan are Palestinians. Remember the Black September in 1970? Once you give them the statehood, they will say, we national Palestinians, we want to be in this country because it's our country. It means to destroy Jordan.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bert-Jan Ruissen, namens de ECR-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, aan eenieder die vandaag ook alle schuld weer bij Isra脙芦l legt, zou ik willen vragen: bent u soms 7脗聽oktober vergeten? En wat eraan voorafging: de jarenlange regen aan raketten en brandballonnen vanuit Gaza op Isra脙芦l? En de missie van Hamas, nl. de totale vernietiging van Isra脙芦l?

Ik zeg niet dat Isra脙芦l geen fouten maakt, maar als we het zicht verliezen op het moorddadige karakter van Hamas, dan dreigen we verkeerde keuzes te maken. Dan dreigen we 芒聙聹oplossingen芒聙聺 aan te reiken die helemaal geen oplossingen blijken te zijn, zoals bijvoorbeeld een Palestijnse staat erkennen, terwijl dat Hamas alleen maar langer in het zadel houdt. Zo komt er echt nooit vrede.

Wie echt vrede dichterbij wil brengen, concentreert zich op drie dingen: ten eerste de onmiddellijke vrijlating van de gijzelaars, ten tweede de ontmanteling van Hamas en ten derde royale humanitaire hulp aan en bescherming van de burgerbevolking in Gaza. Isra脙芦l heeft daarbij een zeer grote verantwoordelijkheid.

Maar sancties tegen Isra脙芦l moeten we in mijn beleving echt niet willen. Daarmee geven we een verkeerd signaal af. Ik kan dat niet anders zien dan een indirecte steunbetuiging aan Hamas. Niet doen dus!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hilde Vautmans, namens de Renew-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, we hebben hier al wat partijen gehoord. Ik houd mijn hart vast voor de onderhandelingen deze namiddag als ik mijn voorganger hoor spreken. Als we alleen focussen op 7脗聽oktober, als we alleen focussen op wat de terroristische organisatie Hamas heeft gedaan, zonder te kijken naar de daden van Isra脙芦l, dan komen we er niet deze namiddag. Ik doe echt een oproep: ik zal straks de onderhandelingen leiden tussen alle partijen.

Het is de eerste keer dat de Verenigde Naties officieel een hongersnood heeft uitgesproken over Gaza, en niet zomaar een hongersnood, maar een door mensen veroorzaakte hongersnood. Meer dan 132脗聽000 kinderen dreigen te sterven, terwijl de voedseltrucks, en we zien de beelden, door mensen worden tegengehouden. Burgers worden doodgeschoten omdat ze aanschuiven voor een stuk brood. Dat is geen tragedie, maar dat is een misdaad. Ik wil dat Europa niet langer medeplichtig is door toe te kijken.

Mevrouw Kallas heeft al heel veel dingen gedaan, heeft meer gedaan dan al uw voorgangers. Toch som ik hier eens op wat er naar mijn mening dringend moet gebeuren: 1) Open alle grensovergangen nu en laat de hulp ongehinderd binnen. 2) Hamas moet vandaag dringend alle gijzelaars vrijlaten. 3) Leg sancties op 脙漏n aan de Isra脙芦li's die de wet overtreden 脙漏n aan de terroristische organisatie Hamas 脙漏n aan de Isra脙芦lische ministers die de wetten met voeten treden. 4) Stop alle wapenexporten die oorlogsmisdaden aanwakkeren. 5) Erken de Palestijnse staat. Dat is het enige wat mogelijk is om tot een tweestatenoplossing te komen. Ik reken op deze goodwill 芒聙娄脗聽

(De Voorzitter ontneemt de spreker het woord)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Villy S脙赂vndal, for Verts/ALE-Gruppen. 芒聙聯 Fru formand! Jeg er altid s脙楼 imponeret over de her debatter. Over at det ekstreme h脙赂jre ikke med et enkelt ord er i stand til at omtale den smerte, man uds脙娄tter det pal脙娄stinensiske folk for lige nu. Det, der sker, er, at Israel straffer et helt folk for terroristernes uhyrlige handlinger. De bruger sult som v脙楼ben, de t脙娄ppebomber hospitaler, teltlejre og skoler. De skyder b脙赂rn, de sulter b脙赂rn, de bomber b脙赂rn. Intet af det her kan undskyldes med hverken bibelcitater eller selvforsvar. Og derfor har EU-landene og EU et meget stort ansvar for at stoppe de krigsforbrydelser, der foreg脙楼r.

Det er pinligt, at vi ikke er i stand til at stille dem til ansvar. Vi har indflydelse, og jeg synes, vi skal bruge den langt bedre, end vi g脙赂r det nu, og derfor er det n脙赂dvendigt at sanktionere som det ene, men det er ogs脙楼 vigtigt som det andet at suspendere den handelsaftale, som er det virkeligt st脙娄rke middel, vi har til at l脙娄gge pres p脙楼 Israel. Der er jo ingen tvivl om, at Israel bryder menneskerettigheder og alle humanit脙娄re love. Vi har et utal af internationale retssystemers ord for, at det, der foreg脙楼r, er forbrydelser mod menneskeheden. Det er det ikke kun i Gaza, det er det ogs脙楼 p脙楼 Vestbredden. Jeg tror stadig p脙楼, at tostatsl脙赂sningen med internationalt anerkendte gr脙娄nser fra 1967 er den eneste holdbare l脙赂sning for begge folk. Men det kr脙娄ver, at vi f脙赂rst sikrer pal脙娄stinensernes ret til at overleve. Og hvis man g脙楼r ind for en tostatsl脙赂sning, er det i sagens natur, at man ogs脙楼 anerkender en pal脙娄stinensisk stat.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Manon Aubry, au nom du groupe The Left. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, 700脗聽jours, il vous aura donc fallu 700脗聽jours de g脙漏nocide 脙聽 Gaza pour qu'il y ait enfin une r脙漏solution au Parlement europ脙漏en.

Pendant脗聽700 jours, la plupart des groupes ici ont refus脙漏 chacune de nos demandes et ont pr脙漏f脙漏r脙漏 fermer les yeux sur le pire carnage humanitaire que notre si脙篓cle ait connu. Et ce n'est que gr脙垄ce 脙聽 l'abn脙漏gation de notre groupe de la gauche, qui n'a jamais rien l脙垄ch脙漏 pour le peuple palestinien, qu'enfin le Parlement se saisit de la question.

Il aura donc fallu attendre que l'ONU d脙漏clare l'脙漏tat de famine et d脙漏nonce la rh脙漏torique g脙漏nocidaire de Netanyahu, que des bombes isra脙漏liennes visent des distributions humanitaires, que plus de 60脗聽000脗聽civils, dont 30脗聽000脗聽enfants, soient l脙垄chement assassin脙漏s, que plus de 200脗聽journalistes soient tu脙漏s, que Benjamin脗聽Netanyahou 脙漏tende son projet g脙漏nocidaire 脙聽 la Cisjordanie pour que ce Parlement se saisisse, enfin, du g脙漏nocide 脙聽 Gaza.

Mais que les choses soient claires: nous ne nous contenterons pas de vagues condamnations. Nous voulons des actes. Madame脗聽Kallas, qu'attendez-vous pour d脙漏cr脙漏ter un embargo strict sur l'envoi d'armes, alors que des pays comme la France en ont livr脙漏 un nombre record? Qu'attendez-vous pour suspendre l'accord d'association entre l'Union europ脙漏enne et Isra脙芦l, alors que votre propre Commission a reconnu que les droits humains 脙漏taient viol脙漏s? Qu'attendez-vous aussi pour offrir la protection diplomatique 脙聽 la Flottille de la libert脙漏, attaqu脙漏e cette nuit par drone, alors qu'elle est en route pour briser l'embargo humanitaire 脙聽 Gaza? Je veux dire, ici, solennellement, que ces centaines de membres de l'脙漏quipage d脙漏montrent qu'ils ont 1脗聽000脗聽fois plus de courage que vous, Madame脗聽Kallas, et que vous, les dirigeants europ脙漏ens.

Les Palestiniens ne pourront pas attendre 700脗聽jours suppl脙漏mentaires de g脙漏nocide, tout simplement parce qu'ils ne seront plus l脙聽 pour 脙陋tre sauv脙漏s. Ils n'ont plus d'abris o脙鹿 se prot脙漏ger des bombes, plus d'h脙麓pitaux pour sauver leurs bless脙漏s, plus de pain pour se nourrir, plus de journalistes pour rapporter leur martyre, plus de terres pour inhumer leurs d脙漏pouilles et plus de larmes pour les pleurer. Ils n'ont plus rien. Mais vous, Madame Kallas, vous, dirigeants europ脙漏ens, vous qui avez tout 脙搂a, vous perdez une chose qu'ils ont encore, vous perdez votre morale et votre humanit脙漏. L'Histoire vous regarde. Agissez, sinon il sera trop tard.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alexander Sell, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Seit dem 脙聹berfall der Hamas auf Israel hat die Europ脙陇ische Union 脙录ber 1,6脗聽Milliarden脗聽Euro an Gaza gezahlt; Deutschland hat nochmal eine Milliarde draufgelegt. Ohne deutsche Steuergelder k脙露nnten sich die Pal脙陇stinenser nicht ern脙陇hren.

Das war aber schon vor dem Krieg so. In Gaza wird n脙陇mlich nichts produziert. Zwei Drittel der Lebensmittel m脙录ssen importiert werden. Die Hilfsleistungen von der EU haben in Gaza zu einer beispiellosen Bev脙露lkerungsexplosion gef脙录hrt: Im Jahr 2000 lebten dort knapp 脙录ber eine Million Menschen, heute sind es 2,3脗聽Millionen脗聽芒聙聯 eine Verdoppelung in nur 25 Jahren.

Nirgendwo sonst auf der Welt w脙陇chst die Bev脙露lkerung so rasant wie in Gaza脗聽芒聙聯 trotz Krieg. Jede Frau bekommt zwischen drei und vier Kinder. Ich muss sagen, ich bewundere die Pal脙陇stinenser f脙录r ihren Kinderreichtum, ich will aber, dass wir uns diesen Reichtum auch leisten k脙露nnen. Deshalb m脙录ssen wir die Hilfszahlungen stoppen, weil deutsche Steuergelder deutschen Familien dienen sollen. Ich will, dass junge Europ脙陇er sich wieder trauen, eine Familie zu gr脙录nden. Wir m脙录ssen in unsere Zukunft investieren und nicht in den Babyboom in Pal脙陇stina.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hildegard Bentele (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Vice芒聙聭President, dear colleagues, after more than 700脗聽days of war, Israel and Gaza may be entering the final phase: the complete defeat of Hamas's military wing, and hopefully the liberation of the last 20脗聽hostages, including at least 7脗聽Europeans.

The EU has no boots on the ground, no role in the ceasefire talks and no seat at the table for Gaza's political future, but we do have a responsibility where we can act, and this is on improving the humanitarian situation. More aid is needed. Humanitarian zones must be real and all actors must cooperate politically. We must set clear boundaries: no deportations, no annexations, no new settlements, but a chance for a terror芒聙聭free and prosperous Palestinian state.

And finally, we should help Israelis and Palestinians rebuild trust: something we as the EU know from our own history of peace after war.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Thank you, Ms脗聽Bentele, for accepting my question. I have been following your position on this situation quite closely, and I also saw that you visited the German芒聙聭Israeli summer festival in Berlin just a few days ago. I take that your support for Israel stems from the right of Israel to exist, which is enshrined in our German national ideals, and I'm just wondering, in your opinion, does this also include the necessity to protect Israel from its internal enemies, and not only its external enemies?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hildegard Bentele (PPE), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Ich w脙录rde darauf jetzt auf Deutsch antworten. Daf脙录r stehe ich: dass wir eben anerkennen, dass die Israelis eine Demokratie sind, in der sie ihre Regierung w脙陇hlen, in der sie ihre Entscheidungen treffen. Und insofern tue ich mich auch schwer脗聽芒聙聯 was ja hier viele im Raum tun脗聽芒聙聯, die Milit脙陇raktion in Gaza, in Gaza-Stadt zu kritisieren, denn wir haben keinen besseren Rat, milit脙陇risch, von keinem Verteidigungsminister.

Sie sagen, man kann die Krise nicht milit脙陇risch l脙露sen. Das steht im Raum, sozusagen. Die Israelis l脙露sen diese Krise jetzt milit脙陇risch, indem sie die letzten Zellen der Hamas beseitigen. Und ich glaube, das ist auch ein richtiger Ansatz, wenn wir sicherstellen wollen, dass wir wirklich eine Zukunft ohne den milit脙陇rischen Arm der Hamas haben. Insofern脗聽芒聙聯 sage ich nur脗聽芒聙聯 sind wir hoffentlich bald am Ende, wir sind schon bei 40/50脗聽% von Gaza-Stadt,脗聽und ich hoffe eben脗聽芒聙聯 ja, dann h脙露re ich eben auf.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nacho S脙隆nchez Amor (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, el aluvi脙鲁n de palabras sobre Gaza no es solo un ejemplo gr脙隆fico de impotencia pol脙颅tica, sino que se est脙隆 convirtiendo en un obst脙隆culo para la acci脙鲁n, en una coartada para la inacci脙鲁n.

Hay que romper este c脙颅rculo vicioso y pasar de la mera descripci脙鲁n del horror a su calificaci脙鲁n pol脙颅tica y jur脙颅dica y a la atribuci脙鲁n de responsabilidades, dentro y fuera de la Uni脙鲁n Europea. Hay que abandonar las frases hechas y hacer algo muy importante en pol脙颅tica: llamar a las cosas por su nombre, sin jerga diplom脙隆tica.

Intent脙漏moslo en la Resoluci脙鲁n de hoy, porque esta notoria falta de credibilidad ya no es solo algo que sufriremos por d脙漏cadas en nuestra relaci脙鲁n con el Sur Global, sino que ya est脙隆 afectando a los ciudadanos europeos; y no me refiero solo a los manifestantes, sino a cualquiera que mira un telediario, con esas im脙隆genes que no se emiten en la televisi脙鲁n p脙潞blica de Israel.

Nombrar al mal moral es necesario. Recordemos el aviso de la 脗芦banalidad del mal脗禄 de Hannah Arendt y llamemos las cosas por su nombre. Por eso nuestra propuesta incluye la expresi脙鲁n 脗芦genocidio脗禄, se se脙卤ala a las autoridades israel脙颅es como responsables de cr脙颅menes de guerra, se pide la suspensi脙鲁n del Acuerdo de Asociaci脙鲁n UE-Israel y se piden sanciones, embargos, el reconocimiento de Palestina y la liberaci脙鲁n de los rehenes.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fabrice Leggeri (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, depuis plus de脗聽700 jours, 48脗聽otages sont d脙漏tenus par le Hamas. 48脗聽vies suspendues, la moiti脙漏 pr脙漏sum脙漏e morte, l'autre toujours retenue. Des visages que l'on oublie trop vite. Des cris que personne ne doit ignorer.

Et que fait l'Europe? Elle parle. Elle parle beaucoup, mais dans le vide. 脙聙 l'image du roi脗聽Lear, de Shakespeare, elle manie les grands discours, mais n'agit jamais pour sauver ceux qui en ont besoin. L'Europe se veut puissante, mais elle est en r脙漏alit脙漏 fantomatique.

Pendant ce temps, la famine progresse 脙聽 Gaza. Oui, il faut venir en aide aux civils, mais sans jamais renforcer une organisation terroriste. Or, l'Union europ脙漏enne distribue des millions d'euros et vote des r脙漏solutions, sans contr脙麓le, sans r脙漏sultat. Tandis que d'autres prennent des initiatives concr脙篓tes, l'Europe se ridiculise par son impuissance.

On nous parle encore d'une solution 脙聽 deux 脙聣tats. C'est un horizon de long terme que nous ne rejetons pas. Mais aujourd'hui, la r脙漏alit脙漏, c'est qu'un tel projet conduirait 脙聽 un 脙聣tat Hamas, c'est-脙聽-dire un 脙聣tat terroriste. Tant que cela perdure, la paix restera une illusion. La politique, ce ne sont pas les mots, ce sont les actes: des actes pour lib脙漏rer les otages, des actes pour prot脙漏ger les innocents, des actes pour garantir la s脙漏curit脙漏 d'Isra脙芦l.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alberico Gambino (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signora Alta Rappresentante, onorevoli colleghi, a Gaza la sofferenza della popolazione civile e la prigionia degli ostaggi gridano al mondo che questa guerra deve trovare assolutamente fine.

Bisogna individuare al pi脙鹿 presto percorsi di pace, ma sappiamo anche che la pace non si costruisce con gli slogan. Occorre sicurezza per Israele, libert脙聽 e dignit脙聽 per i palestinesi e il coraggio di una nuova narrazione che non alimenti odio e disumanizzazione, come ha ricordato il patriarca latino di Gerusalemme.

Per Fratelli d'Italia la linea 脙篓 chiara: sostegno al diritto di Israele a difendersi dal terrorismo, ma richiesta altrettanto ferma di proporzionalit脙聽 e di tutela del popolo palestinese e di corridoi umanitari.

Inoltre, non possiamo tacere di fronte alla sofferenza delle comunit脙聽 cristiane, colpite anch'esse da questa spirale di violenza, eppure fondamentali nell'equilibrio della regione.

L'Europa non pu脙虏 e non deve limitarsi a fare proclami, si ha il dovere di agire. L'Italia ovviamente far脙聽 la sua parte, perch脙漏 solo due popoli e due Stati liberi e sicuri possono aprire la strada a una pace duratura.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Barry Andrews (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, High Representative and colleagues, last month, Major General Haliva of the IDF said that for every one person killed on 7脗聽October, 50 Palestinians must die. A leak from the IDF last month also revealed a civilian death rate of 83%. International humanitarian law is clear: civilians must never be a target of war. This is something that used to bother Israel.

Back in 2002, the head of the legal unit of the IDF said, and I quote, 'I was present when we diverted a missile because we saw that a target standing on a roof had picked up a child. We had one clear rule. We were all parents. We could never approve of killing children.' And now we have more than 50脗聽000 children killed or injured in Gaza.

This is something that Israel used to agonise about 芒聙聯 the legal and moral implications of civilian deaths. So I implore those who consider themselves true friends of Israel to recognise that this is no longer in any way defensible.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hannah Neumann (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, famine has reached Gaza City. People are told to leave but have nowhere to go. Children are starving. Families are bombed.

While Trump and Netanyahu dream of turning Gaza into the Riviera, the European Union is paralysed by shameful infighting. While we debate, they create facts on the ground. And yes, it's above all the German Government that blocks action in the Council. That is unacceptable.

Because if we are serious about ending the suffering, about offering a real alternative to the Trump-Netanyahu alliance, we must stand united, and with our regional partners, and with the hundreds of thousands in Israel demonstrating for an end to this brutal war.

The Israeli Government has promised again and again to let in more humanitarian aid. Before the summer, High Representative Kallas, you announced another such deal. But people in Gaza are still starving. Isn't it time to suspend the EU-Israel Association Agreement, then? For months, Israeli ministers have incited violence and pushed new settlements to bury a two-state solution. Isn't it time to sanction them, then?

So instead of shouting at each other in here, let's unite behind a simple truth. From the river to the sea, all people should be free. Free from hunger. Free from bombs and missiles. Free from hostage-taking and terror attacks. Free from repression, antisemitism and racism. Free to love, live and to raise their children in peace.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Botenga (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame la Haute repr脙漏sentante, chers coll脙篓gues, s'il y a une chose qui est claire, c'est que la boussole morale de l'Europe, aujourd'hui, ne se trouve pas ici. La boussole morale se trouve chez les centaines de milliers d'Europ脙漏ens qui se mobilisent contre l'inaction et la complicit脙漏 europ脙漏enne脗聽芒聙聯 certains d'entre vous, sans doute脗聽芒聙聯, avec ce qui est en cours 脙聽 Gaza et ce qui est un g脙漏nocide.

C'est un g脙漏nocide que tout le monde arrive 脙聽 voir, sauf certains d'entre vous qui continuent 脙聽 soutenir Isra脙芦l, et l'Union europ脙漏enne qui continue 脙聽 soutenir Isra脙芦l avec des centaines de millions d'euros, avec des armes qui passent encore, des massacres qu'on soutient. C'est un peu genre quelqu'un qui rentrerait dans une 脙漏cole, dans un immeuble, et qui dit: 脗芦Je vais buter tout le monde, et apr脙篓s cet immeuble je vais m'en prendre 脙聽 l'脙漏cole et apr脙篓s ce sera l'h脙麓pital qui y passe.脗禄 Et l'Union europ脙漏enne qui vient dire: 脗芦Voil脙聽, vous voulez un fusil encore? Vous avez besoin d'une balle pour qu'on vous aide?脗禄 C'est 脙搂a, aujourd'hui.

Madame脗聽Kallas, vous avez dit que vous avez beaucoup discut脙漏 avec le ministre isra脙漏lien et que vous avez trouv脙漏 un bon accord sur l'aide humanitaire avec des termes de r脙漏f脙漏rence. Vous aviez oubli脙漏 de mentionner qu'il ne fallait pas la famine, parce que, depuis que vous avez eu votre accord, il y a la famine 脙聽 Gaza. C'est 脙搂a votre responsabilit脙漏, Madame脗聽Kallas.

脗聽

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sander Smit (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, high representative, commissaris, collega芒聙聶s, oorlog is gruwelijk. Oorlogsmisdaden tegen journalisten, moskee脙芦n, kerken en hulpverleners zijn onacceptabel. Toch maakt een niets ontziende contraguerrilla tegen terroristen die burgers als menselijk schild misbruiken, nog geen genocide. We mogen het volkenrecht niet uithollen. Waar is uw bewijs van genocidale intentie? Isra脙芦l levert voedsel en laat zieke kinderen via Eilat evacueren. Wie dan toch genocide blijft schreeuwen, bezondigt zich aan bloedsmaad en smerig antisemitisme. Dergelijke retoriek, gevoed door de Moslimbroederschap, zet onze straten en samenleving in brand.

Hamas, dat op 7脗聽oktober een bloedige pogrom pleegde, heeft deze oorlog doelbewust over Gaza afgeroepen. Hun plan: burgers doden voor politieke winst. En u beloont hen met de voortijdige erkenning van Palestina.

De EU moet de vredesplannen van de Arabische Liga omarmen: Hamas moet worden uitgeschakeld en ontwapend, zich overgeven en alle gijzelaars vrijlaten. Wederopbouw van Gaza moet plaatsvinden zonder verdrijving van burgers. Laten we leren van de Emiraten en aan vrede bouwen.

(De spreker stemt ermee in om te antwoorden op verschillende 芒聙聹blauwe kaart芒聙聺-vragen)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Botenga (The Left), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 You have just accused us of the most horrible things. You should be ashamed. Let me ask you, what are your sources? The International Court of Justice has spoken about a plausible risk of genocide, the International Association of Genocide Scholars, Amnesty International, Human Rights Watch, Doctors Without Borders, Palestinians on the ground, Israeli NGOs and even Israeli genocide scholars and Holocaust experts. So what are your sources? Who are you to deny the genocide? What is it, like 'Trust me, bro'? That's not going to work here. Do better!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sander Smit (PPE), 芒聙聹blauwe kaart芒聙聺-antwoord. 芒聙聯 Dit is het typische links-extremistische discours. Als enkele wetenschappers een bewering lanceren, dan is dat nog geen wetenschappelijke consensus. Er is geen internationale consensus, er is geen uitspraak van het Internationaal Gerechtshof hierover.

En nogmaals, waarom spreekt u niet over Sudan? Ik hoor niemand spreken over de 150脗聽000 doden in Sudan, de 11脗聽miljoen ontheemden en de 26脗聽miljoen personen in acute voedselnood. Bij u lijkt het wel:脗聽No Jews, no news.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Cecilia Strada (S&D), domanda "cartellino blu". 芒聙聯 Volevo anch'io tornare sulla parola genocidio.

La Convenzione per la prevenzione e la punizione dei crimini di genocidio del 1948 脙篓 la base legale sulla quale noi osserviamo, leggiamo e confrontiamo quello che accade a Gaza e in Cisgiordania e concludiamo, come tutte le organizzazioni che citava il collega Botenga, che 脙篓 in corso un crimine.

Non sono pagine Facebook, 脙篓 una cosa molto, molto seria: sono descritti tutti gli atti e li confrontiamo con quello che sta succedendo a Gaza e in West Bank, e il risultato 脙篓: vengono commessi atti di genocidio. C'脙篓 anche l'intenzione di pulizia etnica, espressa da ministri israeliani in televisione.

Quindi la mia domanda per Lei 脙篓: ha mai letto la Convenzione per la prevenzione e la punizione dei crimini di genocidio?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sander Smit (PPE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Of course I have read the document, and we should not make international law into a political instrument, as you do, because, as you know, we have seen acts, as I have said: food deliveries, evacuation of Gazan children through Eilat to Italy. That's what Israel is doing as well. And genocide requires genocidal intent to be proven.

So you think that evacuating Palestinian children to Italy for treatment can be reconciled with having a genocidal intent? Then you make a laugh, then you are really insulting the victims of real genocides like the Armenian Genocide and the Shoah.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kathleen Van Brempt (S&D). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, ik moet eerlijk zeggen dat ik toch plaatsvervangende schaamte voel over deze tussenkomst van de Europese Volkspartij. Ik wens Hilde heel veel succes met de onderhandelingen straks; het zal niet gemakkelijk worden. Het is hoog tijd dat we in dit Parlement een resolutie neerleggen! De excuses zijn op. De Commissie verschuilt zich achter de Raad en de Raad achter de Commissie en achter de unanimiteit.

Maar wie zich niet kan verschuilen voor de genocide, dat zijn de Palestijnen in Gaza en de bezette gebieden. Neem actie, voer economische sancties in, schort het associatieverdrag op, stop de subsidies aan Horizon. Stop ook met het kopen van wapens. Vandaag las ik in de krant dat er op een groot wapenevenement in Londen talloze Isra脙芦lische bedrijven aanwezig zijn. We gaan ons geld in Europa toch niet besteden aan Isra脙芦lische wapens?

Europeanen eisen actie! En iets heel belangrijks: het internationaal recht verplicht ons te handelen. Wie wegkijkt bij genocide, schendt dat recht en is medeverantwoordelijk. Dat geldt zowel voor ons als voor de Commissie. De excuses zijn op.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anders Vistisen (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, a March 2024 poll relieved a shocking fact: 71脗聽% of Palestinians support Hamas's horrific terrorist attack on 7脗聽October, and 59脗聽% believe that armed struggle is the best way forward. These are not fringe views. They are a majority in a society based on fanatical Islamism and support for terrorism. Every Arab country knows this and recognises it. That is why neighbouring countries such as Egypt, Jordan and Lebanon, or even the rich Arab oil states of the Gulf, have not taken any of these Palestinian extremists.

But in Europe the naivety is without limits. Denmark has already seen the consequences. In 1992, 321脗聽stateless Palestinians were granted residence by special law. Today, the results are clear: 74脗聽% live on welfare; 57脗聽% have criminal convictions;脗聽and for the children, the stats are even worse.

This is not humanity. This is a generational betrayal. Europe must finally learn: importing radicalised people means exporting security and prosperity for the next generations.

(The speaker agreed to take several blue-card questions)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Catarina Vieira (Verts/ALE), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Dear colleague, you say this is not humanity.

You just called all Palestinians extremists: is it humanity to call the 55脗聽000 or more children who have been killed or injured in this horrible genocide extremists? Where is your humanity? How dare you call an entire nation extremist?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anders Vistisen (PfE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 The polls define it as extremist when you support horrific acts of terrorism, but I can't bear any more of your hypocrisy on the left. There are genocides committed in many places in the world, but it is only when it is the Jewish state, the only Jewish state in the world, the State of Israel, that you care. You even walk in demonstration, in capitals like my own in Copenhagen, with Palestinians using the Nazi salute. How can people from the S&D Group, from Renew and other places accept that kind of fanaticism in a cause you claim is carried out in the name of humanity? I am appalled.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 This is the last time that I am going to accept two blue cards for one person, because we are really running out of time.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Erik Marquardt (Verts/ALE), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 You were talking about hypocrisy. You were quoting polls which were done in Gaza, so do you believe that Hamas is an organisation which accepts scientific freedom, free press and polls? Because in the end you would rely on numbers which are based on Hamas propaganda, maybe.

So, do you believe that the polls are trustworthy if they are done in Gaza at the moment, which the Hamas government is governing?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anders Vistisen (PfE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 No, I generally don't tend to believe Hamas, not when they claim how many victims there are, or when they decide how many of them are combatant and non-combatants, because I do not in general put my trust in terrorist organisations. But I recognise, just like you can in an autocracy like Russia, you can have polling done in a population even though it lives in an unfree society, just as well as we can today see how Germans felt about the Nazi regime in the 1930s, if you look at the historical sources for that.

So, no, I don't believe that you can just pick and choose the facts, so believe Hamas when it comes to the number of their children, and not when it comes to the support in the general population. You have to pick a side.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Joachim Stanis脜聜aw Brudzi脜聞ski (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Pani Komisarz! W cieniu tego ogromnego dramatu, jakim jest wojna Izraela z Hamasem, w cieniu niewyobra脜录alnego cierpienia, jakim s脛聟 cierpi脛聟ce miliony cywil脙鲁w, 脜聸mier脛聡, wysiedlenia, g脜聜脙鲁d, brak dost脛聶pu do podstawowych 脜聸rodk脙鲁w do 脜录ycia dla mieszka脜聞c脙鲁w Gazy, zapowiedzi w脜聜adz Izraela o otwarciu bram piek脜聜a pokazuj脛聟, do czego prowadzi nienawi脜聸脛聡 i brak gotowo脜聸ci do pojednania ze strony tych, kt脙鲁rzy odwo脜聜uj脛聟 si脛聶 do tego samego ojca, biblijnego Abrahama.

W tej tragedii, jak脛聟 jest wojna wyznawc脙鲁w judaizmu i islamu, trwa olbrzymie cierpienie chrze脜聸cijan, kt脙鲁rzy od wiek脙鲁w 脜录yj脛聟 w Izraelu i Palestynie. Dzi脜聸 ich liczba gwa脜聜townie maleje. Barbarzy脜聞skie zbombardowanie w lipcu bie脜录脛聟cego roku przez armi脛聶 Izraela katolickiego ko脜聸cio脜聜a 脜職wi脛聶tej Rodziny w centrum Gazy, jak r脙鲁wnie脜录 ataki ze strony islamskich ekstremist脙鲁w wobec chrze脜聸cijan wpisuj脛聟 si脛聶 nie tylko w d脜聜ug脛聟 histori脛聶 cierpienia chrze脜聸cijan w Ziemi 脜職wi脛聶tej, ale pokazuj脛聟, 脜录e wielowyznaniowe dziedzictwo Izraela nieuchronnie przechodzi do historii. Warto przywo脜聜a脛聡 tutaj s脜聜owa chrze脜聸cija脜聞skich patriarch脙鲁w Jerozolimy, kt脙鲁rzy zaznaczaj脛聟, 脜录e nie b脛聶dzie pokoju bez prawdziwego odwo脜聜ania si脛聶 do 脜潞r脙鲁de脜聜 cywilizacji judeochrze脜聸cija脜聞skiej.

脗聽脗聽Bernard Guetta (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, que voulaient-ils, ces assassins d'hier 脙聽 J脙漏rusalem? Ils voulaient, diraient-ils, venger les morts de Gaza, les bombardements, la faim et la destruction de toutes villes, villages, 脙漏coles et h脙麓pitaux. Mais quel est l'objet de ces bombardements qu'ils voulaient venger? On ne le sait que trop: venger la sanglante barbarie du 7脗聽octobre et l'intol脙漏rable d脙漏tention d'otages qui s'est ensuivie. La preuve nous est ainsi donn脙漏e de la totale absurdit脙漏, non, pardon, de la totale imb脙漏cilit脙漏 de cette odieuse trag脙漏die. La vengeance ne m脙篓ne qu'脙聽 la vengeance.

Notre r脙麓le, 脙聽 nous Europ脙漏ens, nous qui ne sommes ni pro-isra脙漏liens, ni pro-palestiniens, mais pro-paix, est d'inlassablement marteler qu'il n'est pas d'autre solution que la coexistence de deux 脙聣tats. C'est une utopie, diront beaucoup, mais non, non et non! C'est un imp脙漏ratif auquel tous les fanatiques devront se r脙漏soudre avant que l'un et l'autre de ces deux peuples n'aient tout perdu, m脙陋me l'espoir.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mounir Satouri (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, apr脙篓s 23脗聽mois de g脙漏nocide, hier, nous avons observ脙漏 une minute de silence pour Gaza. 脙聙 l'heure o脙鹿 je vous parle, 1脗聽million de Gazaouis ont re脙搂u l'ordre de quitter le nord de la bande. Isra脙芦l poursuit son nettoyage ethnique. Et nous, que faisons-nous? Notre premi脙篓re minute de silence.

Les Gazaouis n'ont qu'un choix, l'exil ou la mort. Et nous, Europ脙漏ens, avons contribu脙漏 脙聽 ce crime et sommes complices et coupables. Coupables de fournir des armes, coupables de refuser d'imposer l'entr脙漏e de l'aide humanitaire, coupables de ne pas sanctionner Isra脙芦l, coupables de d脙漏tourner les yeux de la Cisjordanie et de J脙漏rusalem-Est. Assez de silence! Suspendons l'accord d'association, passons aux sanctions, 脙聽 l'embargo sur les armes, isolons Isra脙芦l sur la sc脙篓ne internationale! Les Palestiniens n'ont pas besoin de notre minute de silence. Ils ont besoin de nos actes. Alors il est temps d'agir.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Catarina Martins (The Left). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, senhora Kallas, 脙漏 genoc脙颅dio, n脙拢o 脙漏 um conflito 芒聙聰 脙漏 genoc脙颅dio. Vinte e oito crian脙搂as morrem por dia na Faixa de Gaza, 脙聽s m脙拢os de Israel e 脙聽s m脙拢os de todos os que s脙拢o c脙潞mplices.

N脙拢o venha para aqui chorar os milh脙碌es de ajuda humanit脙隆ria que est脙拢o a apodrecer porque Israel decidiu matar 脙聽 fome em Gaza.

Na verdade, o que precisava de estar a fazer era apoiar quem est脙隆 a tentar que a ajuda humanit脙隆ria chegue a Gaza. N脙拢o lhe ouvi uma palavra sobre a Flotilha Global.脗聽Esta noite, um dos barcos com bandeira portuguesa onde viajam Greta Thunberg, Ada Colau, Mariana Mort脙隆gua e muitos outros cidad脙拢os europeus foi atingido por um drone. J脙隆 aconteceu antes. Sabemos que 脙漏 assim que Israel age.

E o que dizem? Roberta Metsola, Ursula von der Leyen, Ant脙鲁nio Costa, onde est脙隆 a vossa condena脙搂脙拢o a este ataque? Onde est脙隆 a vossa garantia de prote脙搂脙拢o 脙聽 Flotilha Liberdade? Onde est脙隆 a garantia de prote脙搂脙拢o a quem est脙隆 a fazer o que todos n脙鲁s dev脙颅amos estar a fazer, que 脙漏 fazer chegar ajuda humanit脙隆ria a Gaza?

O vosso sil脙陋ncio 脙漏 c脙潞mplice. Israel est脙隆 a matar com as armas e os meios europeus. Os c脙潞mplices do genoc脙颅dio s脙拢o tamb脙漏m genocidas.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alice Teodorescu M脙楼we (PPE). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Kommission脙陇r Kallas! Tv脙楼 palestinska terrorister genomf脙露rde under g脙楼rdagen 脙陇nnu ett kallblodigt attentat n脙陇r de m脙露rdade oskyldiga p脙楼 en buss i Jerusalem. Minst sex personer miste livet och ytterligare m脙楼nga, d脙陇ribland en h脙露ggravid kvinna p脙楼 v脙陇g till jobbet, 脙陇r allvarligt skadade. Hamas och palestinska islamistiska jihad firar terrorn och instruerar i ett f脙陇rskt klipp morgondagens terrorister i att d脙露da fler oskyldiga bussresen脙陇rer.

All den ondska som v脙陇stv脙陇rlden felaktigt anklagar Israel f脙露r, utf脙露r och firar islamisterna. 脙聞nd脙楼 脙陇r det Israel som ska pressas till underkastelse enligt naiva v脙陇sterl脙陇nningar, som inte f脙露rst脙楼r att vi 脙陇r n脙陇st p脙楼 tur. EU m脙楼ste klarg脙露ra att det inte finns n脙楼gon fred att bygga med en d脙露dskult till motpart. Ingen fred kan gro d脙陇r skolbarn indoktrineras till att dr脙露mma om martyrd脙露den, d脙陇r den palestinska administrationen bel脙露nar terroristers familjer ekonomiskt. Hamas m脙楼ste besegras en g脙楼ng f脙露r alla. Misslyckas Israel 脙楼terst脙楼r fler terrord脙楼d tills inget Israel finns kvar, tills alla judar f脙露rintats.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ren脙漏 Repasi (S&D). 芒聙聯 Sehr geehrte Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Mit 17脗聽Jahren nahm ich an einem Sch脙录leraustausch mit deutschen, j脙录dischen und arabischen Jugendlichen aus Israel teil. Meine Freunde sagten damals: Wir wollen so etwas wie eure EU脗聽芒聙聯 zwei Staaten, in Vielfalt geeint.

Fast 30脗聽Jahre sp脙陇ter ist der Traum zerplatzt. Die Terrororganisation Hamas ver脙录bt den gr脙露脙聼ten Massenmord an j脙录dischen Menschen seit dem Holocaust. In Gaza erleben wir menschengemachten Hunger, zerst脙露rte Familien, verlorene Leben. Die Zivilgesellschaft auf beiden Seiten blutet und leidet脗聽芒聙聯 wegen der Hamas und wegen der Regierung Netanjahu.

Auch der israelischen Zivilgesellschaft will ich hier zurufen: Wir sehen euch, und wir unterst脙录tzen euch! Euer Protest gegen den Krieg und gegen Netanjahu verhallt nicht. Auch f脙录r euch darf mein Europa, das f脙录r Frieden und V脙露lkerrecht steht, nicht schweigen脗聽芒聙聯 es muss handeln! Dabei darf es keine Tabus geben.

Darum fordere ich die sofortige Freilassung aller Geiseln, einen sofortigen Waffenstillstand und ein sofortiges Ende der humanit脙陇ren Blockade.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marco Squarta (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, a Gaza non assistiamo pi脙鹿 a un conflitto, ma al progressivo annientamento di una popolazione inerme: bambini uccisi, madri in lacrime, ospedali ridotti in macerie. Immagini che non possono essere giustificate in nome della difesa.

Hamas resta un'organizzazione terroristica, il 7 ottobre ha lasciato una ferita incancellabile. Gli ostaggi vanno liberati subito, ma non possiamo accettare che la risposta diventi punizione collettiva.

L'Italia con "food for Gaza" ha dimostrato che la solidariet脙聽 pu脙虏 tradursi in gesti concreti: tonnellate di aiuti inviati, bambini accolti e curati. 脙聢 un esempio che l'Europa deve raccogliere senza indugi.

Ora serve una sola voce, autorevole e decisa, che chieda il cessate il fuoco, la protezione dei civili e la liberazione di tutti gli ostaggi. Solo un'Europa forte potr脙聽 contribuire a una pace duratura, con due popoli che vivono in due Stati, liberi e sicuri nella dignit脙聽 e nella umanit脙聽.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ignazio Roberto Marino (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, I visited Birkenau and Auschwitz with Sami Modiano, who was deported there at the age of 13 and lost his father and young sister there.脗聽I visited Hiroshima, a museum that shows tragedy and destruction.

I will not live long enough to visit the Museum of the Destruction of Gaza and to hear the voices of my great-grandchildren asking 'but where was great-grandfather in those years? How could this have happened?'

Thanks to your young age, High Representative Kallas, you will still be there and you will need to change the subject or hide, because you will not be able to explain to your grandchildren how, in a position of power representing Europe, you did not do the impossible to stop those shooting children begging for water and bread. Nor did you raise your voice when your President called to express solidarity to a war criminal who, in your silence, carried out a genocide.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kathleen Funchion (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madam President, we are now more than 700脗聽days into Israel's genocide in Gaza, and the response from world leaders, including those here in Europe, continues to be one of shameful, resounding silence. More than 64脗聽000 people murdered was not enough for President von der Leyen to say 'Stop', and it seems that the images of enforced starvation of children is not enough for the Commission and the Council to take real action against Israel.

The EU and all Member States must suspend the EU芒聙聭Israel Association Agreement, ban trade with illegal settlements and finally work towards the delivery of a two芒聙聭state solution where all children can live without hunger and without fear of Israeli bombs falling from the sky.

I do want to comment on some of the comments we have heard here today from people in this Chamber who are denying the genocide. I ask, how can you watch children being shredded to pieces by Israeli weapons and do nothing, and deny the genocide?

History will not forgive silence. 'Never again' means 'never again for everyone'.

Saoirse don Phalaist脙颅n!

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomislav Sokol (PPE), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 You speak about genocide all the time 芒聙聯 you and the other colleagues from the left. My question is: are you aware of a statement signed by almost 500 legal and history scholars which, with various strong arguments, says that there is no genocide in Gaza?

So,脗聽are you also aware of that, or do you just plan to not hear this because it doesn't suit your narrative?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kathleen Funchion (The Left), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 The first thing I want to say is, I will call out a genocide wherever it happens. Wherever there is a genocide, it will be called out by me.

We know very well in Ireland what it's like to be under occupation. So that is why we have such empathy, rightly so, with the Palestinian people.

It has already been stated by my colleague, Marc Botenga. All of the various organisations 芒聙聯 the International Court of Justice, Amnesty, all of the organisations 芒聙聯 the list is as long as your arm 芒聙聯 that know that this is a genocide.

You are the one that is denying a genocide, and history will not look well on you at all, and rightly so.

Shame on you to look at kids starving 芒聙聯 enforced starvation 芒聙聯 trucks and boats trying to get in with food and aid, and you sit there and you laugh. Shame on you!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Colleagues, you have 30脗聽seconds for an answer.

As we are already late, I have to close the catch芒聙聭the芒聙聭eye requests now. We have already given blue cards, and we already have a lot of requests for catch芒聙聭the芒聙聭eye. So the blue card is closed, and now the catch芒聙聭the芒聙聭eye requests are closed.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Se脙隆n Kelly (PPE). 芒聙聯 A Uachtar脙隆in, here we are again debating the undebatable. Colleagues will defend what is indefensible and we will be here until Netanyahu achieves his goal: total destruction of Gaza.

Words cannot do justice to the human indignity that Netanyahu's enforced famine imposes on innocent Gazans. It is just heartbreaking to see young children with a bowl in their hands begging for food every day.

Netanyahu has also created the deadliest period for journalists in contemporary history. A generation of accountability has been wiped out, granting Netanyahu carte blanche to starve, kill and destroy.

As we speak, Gaza is on its knees and Israel bombards Gaza City. Meanwhile, activists like Kitty O'Brien face police brutality for standing against these atrocities.

We cannot give up on a two芒聙聭state solution, we cannot stand idly against annexation and we cannot stand by violence when peace is possible.脗聽Free the hostages and free Gaza.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lucia Annunziata (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, parler脙虏 intanto del perch脙漏 bisogna in questo momento difendere i palestinesi.

La prima ragione 脙篓 che sono un popolo vessato dal '48 e la loro sofferenza continua a riprodurre tragedie.

La seconda ragione 脙篓 che la guerra non si sta rivelando una soluzione nemmeno per Israele: dopo due anni di applicazione di tutto il suo potere militare in un piccolo territorio, il governo di Israele continua a sostenere di non essere riuscito a neutralizzare Hamas. Questa 脙篓 una sconfitta.

La pace, anche per Israele, deve prendere un'altra strada, che 脙篓 quella che ci indicano e ci suggeriscono i parenti degli ostaggi riservisti e le migliaia di cittadini che, in questi anni e tuttora, sfilano contro Netanyahu.

Infine, la pace si fa facendo chiarezza, anche tra noi: il punto che ci divide qui dentro, che ha reso impossibile fare niente o fare anche poche cose, 脙篓 quello del genocidio. Ma se noi anche non usassimo questa parola, non cambierebbero i fatti, come hanno detto molti prima di me: il modo, il luogo e i tempi dell'uccisione di decine di migliaia di persone.

Come gruppo S&D abbiamo deciso di pronunciare questa parola, e se per uscire dall'impasse in cui dobbiamo oggi dividerci sul genocidio...

(La Presidente toglie la parola all'oratrice)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Carola Rackete (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madam President, as of now, at least 2脗聽361脗聽Palestinians have died of malnutrition, and more than 2脗聽000 were murdered trying to reach food distribution.

Everyone in the world with a smartphone can see images of starving people, starving children on their phones. It is heartbreaking and disgusting to watch, and it is impossible to talk about this famine without talking about the intent of Israel: it's genocide.

It is disgusting to watch because the European Commission, European governments like the German one, and also many people here in the room are still choosing to be complicit instead of doing the obvious thing: impose sanctions, suspend the Association Agreement, and stop selling weapons to kill defenceless people who have lived in an open-air prison for decades.

We are living and shaping a historic moment when international cooperation falls apart on so many levels, but everyone here has a choice, and the Commission has a choice: to fulfil your obligation to prevent genocide.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Wouter Beke (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, mevrouw Kallas, collega芒聙聶s, 6脗聽000 doden geleden 芒聙聰 dat is vijf weken geleden 芒聙聰 stonden wij hier te discussi脙芦ren over de oorlog in Gaza. Toen vroegen we: wanneer gaat dat stoppen? 6脗聽000 doden geleden. Wanneer schiet Europa eindelijk in actie? Wanneer gaan we dat handelsakkoord, dat associatieakkoord opschorten? Wanneer zetten we Isra脙芦l onder druk? Wanneer voeren we dat importverbod uit de bezette gebieden door? Wanneer voeren we een Europees inreisverbod door? Dit moet stoppen!

Sommige lidstaten, waaronder Belgi脙芦, nemen initiatieven. Maar Europa blijft doof. Wel, Europa kan niet doof blijven. Als Europa enige morele verantwoordelijkheid heeft, dan moet het nu ageren. Dat doen we ten aanzien van Oekra脙炉ne. Dat moeten we ook doen ten aanzien van de gruwelen die in Gaza plaatsvinden. Voorzitter, mevrouw Kallas, neem uw verantwoordelijkheid, neem actie en zorg ervoor dat we volgende maand hier niet opnieuw moeten discussi脙芦ren en zeggen: er zijn opnieuw 5脗聽000 doden bijgekomen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Evin Incir (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, refusing to acknowledge a genocide doesn't make it less of a genocide. Refusing to call a starvation a starvation doesn't make it less of a starvation. Refusing to name an occupation an occupation doesn't make it less of an occupation. International law is not a matter of left and right 芒聙聯 it's a matter of principle, but the reality is undeniable.

For over a year in this European Parliament, there has been a right-wing majority that has refused to name atrocities for what they are and has been even less willing to act. You have the possibility now to show that you can spell 'humanity' through joining us in our demands for action, and to call a spade a spade. For how many more Palestinians must die before international law is enforced? How many more children must cease to see the daylight? Tell me how many!

Freeze the EU-Israel Association Agreement, adopt an EU-wide weapon embargo, sanction the criminal government of Israel, implement ICC and ICJ decisions, recognise Palestine. Shame on this current silence. Shame on hypocrisy. Shame on the inactions. Shame, shame, shame.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomislav Sokol (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, islamski fundamentalizam je najve脛聡e zlo dana脜隆njice. Od sjeverne Afrike, Bliskog istoka, Indijskog potkontinenta do Europe, kr脜隆脛聡ani, 脜陆idovi i pripadnici drugih vjera 脜戮rtve su ove totalitarne zlo脛聧ina脛聧ke ideologije.

Primjer toga upravo je Izrael, dr脜戮ava koja se od svog osnutka 1948. bori za opstanak. Nemojmo imati iluzije. Da Hamas pobijedi u ovome ratu, izraelska dr脜戮ava bi nestala, a njezin narod bio zbrisan.

Za ono 脜隆to se doga脛聭a u Gazi isklju脛聧ivo je kriv Hamas i dok on ne bude uklonjen, ne mo脜戮e biti mira. Islamisti脛聧ka ideologija koju predstavlja Hamas genocidna je i predstavlja rizik i za Europu.

Meni, gospodo s ljevice, nije normalno da za Bo脜戮i脛聡 duge cijevi moraju 脛聧uvati crkve u Njema脛聧koj ili da se blagdanski sajmovi moraju 脜隆tititi betonskim blokovima. Nemojte imati iluzije. Da oni s kojima zajedno demonstrirate protiv Izraela do脛聭u na vlast u Europi, vi progresivci bili biste im prve mete.

Zato se jasno odredimo protiv islamizma i spasimo Europu dok jo脜隆 nije kasno.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Temido (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, senhora Alta Representante, caros colegas, Gaza est脙隆 a morrer de fome diante dos nossos olhos e com ela a credibilidade das institui脙搂脙碌es europeias, individual e coletiva.

N脙鲁s falamos de valores, mas aceitamos a viola脙搂脙拢o sistem脙隆tica do direito internacional, com as crian脙搂as de Gaza condenadas 脙聽 fome infligida, o disparo contra civis nas filas de ajuda alimentar e o bombardeamento repetido de servi脙搂os de sa脙潞de.

N脙鲁s aplicamos san脙搂脙碌es financeiras contra a R脙潞ssia, mas adiamos a suspens脙拢o do acordo comercial com Israel, a interrup脙搂脙拢o das trocas comerciais com os colonatos e o congelamento da compra e venda de armas.

Reconhecemos todos os ind脙颅cios e sinais de que um genoc脙颅dio se desenrola em Gaza, mas n脙拢o queremos usar a palavra.脗聽Como 脙漏 poss脙颅vel que a Europa, que jurou 脗芦nunca mais脗禄, esteja aqui hoje outra vez? Perdemos a autoridade moral perante terceiros pa脙颅ses, perante os nossos concidad脙拢os.

Como fez na Ucr脙垄nia, Senhora Alta Representante, apelo para que a Uni脙拢o Europeia possa voltar a estar do lado certo da hist脙鲁ria em Gaza, na exig脙陋ncia de um cessar-fogo imediato, na entrada de ajuda alimentar e na concretiza脙搂脙拢o da solu脙搂脙拢o de dois Estados.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Mandl (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, first, I want to make the remark that also during this war and after this war, we in this Chamber have to talk to each other also on different matters. This is why I advocate for a bit of a de芒聙聭escalation in the language. I hear 'shame, shame, shame' all the time and things like that 芒聙聯 I do not think this is the way to deal with it.

Secondly, the whole mess has been started by the Iranian regime. I am very happy, Ms脗聽Kallas, that we have you here and not your predecessor during these times and generally, but I also want to remind you that the Iranian regime is very much aligned with the Russian regime at the moment. These are those who fight the political West, which we are supposed to defend, I think.

The Iranian regime might already regret this attack, but it is still there and Hamas is also still there. Usually, we are against political Islam, we are against Islamic terrorism. Usually, we are for democracy. Israel is a vibrant democracy.

Of course, I am very much in favour of supplying help to Gaza. I very much recognise that Israel itself is also supplying help to Gaza. I am not against the two芒聙聭state solution. I am for a two芒聙聭state solution, but after a long process of deweaponisation of the Palestinian territories.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽C脙漏line Imart (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame la Haute repr脙漏sentante, 703脗聽jours. 703脗聽jours depuis que le Hamas a arrach脙漏 des civils 脙聽 leur vie lors d'un attentat d'une brutalit脙漏 sans pr脙漏c脙漏dent. 703脗聽jours d'angoisse insoutenable pour les familles et 703脗聽jours que nous martelons, avec mes coll脙篓gues du groupe脗聽PPE, la m脙陋me exigence: la lib脙漏ration imm脙漏diate des otages, incontournable pour toute n脙漏gociation.

Pourtant, pendant ce temps, certains pays comme le mien ont choisi de reconna脙庐tre l'脙聣tat de Palestine, mais les conditions ne sont pas encore r脙漏unies, car ce sont, en reconnaissant l'脙聣tat de Palestine, les massacres du 7脗聽octobre qu'on valide.

La reconnaissance de l'脙聣tat de Palestine devrait 脙陋tre envisag脙漏e apr脙篓s la lib脙漏ration des otages isra脙漏liens, avec un apport effectif de l'aide humanitaire 脙聽 la population de Gaza et l'instauration d'un cessez-le-feu de part et d'autre.

L'Europe ne peut pas n脙漏gocier sous la menace des armes. La reconnaissance mutuelle des deux 脙聣tats est indispensable, mais vouloir b脙垄tir cela au milieu du chaos, c'est une folie qui compromettrait la cr脙漏dibilit脙漏 et l'unit脙漏 de l'Europe. L'heure n'est plus 脙聽 la diplomatie des bons sentiments. Nous devons mobiliser notre 脙漏nergie pour une seule priorit脙漏: la lib脙漏ration des otages et la fin de la souffrance de tous les civils.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

Spontane Wortmeldungen

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nina Carberry (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, genocide, famine, war: the EU was formed on the promise that we would stop such atrocities from happening again. Yet the people of Gaza are dying in their tens of thousands.

I have said it before in this Chamber: Israel must end its siege. Military operations must cease. Aid must flow freely.

It is unacceptable that the Commission and the European Council have failed to take action more than 18脗聽months on from Ireland and Spain calling for the review of the EU-Israel Association Agreement. That agreement must be suspended. Israel must be held accountable. We need a ceasefire, the release of all hostages and the unrestricted aid flow now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Daniel Attard (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, colleagues, after the horrors of the Second World War, our forefathers said 'never again'.

Yet today in Gaza, genocide is unfolding before our eyes. This is not anti-Semitism: this is the declared policy of Netanyahu, Ben-Gvir, Smotrich and their extremist government.

We present ourselves as the guardians of international law, yet when political conviction clashes with convenience, when human dignity clashes with commercial interests, we compromise. We look away.

So let us act. Because every day we allow the Israel Association Agreement, every day we allow trade and arms to flow, we betray the promise of 'never again'. Because, colleagues, 'never again' must mean something, or else it would mean nothing at all.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Oihane Agirregoitia Mart脙颅nez (Renew). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, mire, yo estoy harta de volver a Euskadi, a mi casa, semana tras semana y estar escuchando c脙鲁mo estamos viendo que asesinan y que matan de hambre a ni脙卤os y ni脙卤as y a civiles, y que desde la Uni脙鲁n Europea no estamos haciendo nada. No podemos seguir escuchando que no hay acuerdo, porque esto ya no nos vale. Debemos ejercer nuestra responsabilidad y hay que buscar v脙颅as para poder actuar.

脗驴Nos quieren decir que podemos firmar un acuerdo comercial que no gusta a nadie con los Estados Unidos y que no podemos avanzar en sanciones o en alg脙潞n tipo de presiones pol脙颅ticas y econ脙鲁micas hacia el Gobierno de Israel? Pues nos negamos a creer esto, y nos preguntamos tambi脙漏n si tienen ojos en la cara los l脙颅deres de las instituciones europeas y de las formaciones pol脙颅ticas europeas y no ven las crecientes quejas, demandas, manifestaciones, que tenemos en distintos puntos de Europa.

No es dif脙颅cil encontrar art脙颅culos que hablan sobre c脙鲁mo se deben respetar los derechos humanos y cumplir con el Derecho europeo internacional en el Convenio Europeo para la Protecci脙鲁n de los Derechos Humanos. La calle no entiende de tratados, no entiende que no seamos contundentes con este tema, y la paciencia de muchos eurodiputados y eurodiputadas se est脙隆 agotando, incluyendo la m脙颅a. Actuemos ya.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Miranda Paz (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora Kallas, recientemente usted hizo unas declaraciones, en las que dec脙颅a que sent脙颅a frustraci脙鲁n por el genocidio en Gaza. Usted no usa la palabra 脗芦genocidio脗禄, pero la usa mucha gente, harta de ver c脙鲁mo se mata a ni脙卤os.

Quiero desde aqu脙颅 transmitir toda la solidaridad con las personas que se manifiestan en toda Europa, incluido en mi pa脙颅s, en Galiza, donde recientemente, por manifestarse, han sido atacadas por la Polic脙颅a. Y quiero decirlo, adem脙隆s, alto y p脙潞blico. 脗隆Es el mundo al rev脙漏s, denunciar un genocidio y resulta que te pegan! 脗驴En qu脙漏 mundo vivimos, se脙卤ora Kallas? Yo tambi脙漏n me siento frustrada. Creo que Europa est脙隆 cometiendo un error muy grande, pero no parar脙漏 de pelear hasta que este genocidio acabe.

La Vuelta est脙隆 pasando hoy por el脗聽concello de Mos, un concello de Galiza, de mi tierra, que por cada kil脙鲁metro representa 6脗聽000 ni脙卤os muertos. Cada kil脙鲁metro de los 18脗聽000 ni脙卤os asesinados, de los 6脗聽000脗聽kil脙鲁metros de la Vuelta. No se puede amparar este tipo de actos. 脗驴Por qu脙漏? Porque el equipo de Israel est脙隆 participando. Y desde aqu脙颅, toda esa solidaridad, con toda la solidaridad palestina...

(la presidenta retira la palabra a la oradora)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脙聳zlem Demirel (The Left). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Die CDU-Abgeordnete Hildegard Bentele hat sich vorhin hier hingestellt und allen Ernstes behauptet, dass Israel gerade den Nahostkonflikt milit脙陇risch l脙露se. Ich will in aller Deutlichkeit sagen: Israel l脙露st gerade gar nichts. Israel begeht einen Genozid, einen Genozid im Livestream, und bekommt noch Applaus von der konservativen Ecke hier im Hause. Das ist unertr脙陇glich!

Doch Hildegard Bentele ist unwichtig. Frau Kallas, Sie und Frau von der Leyen und Herr Merz und alle anderen geben noch gr脙录nes Licht f脙录r diese menschenverachtende Politik.

Heute wurde die Freedom Flotilla angegriffen; das ist illegal. Die Blockade Gazas ist illegal. Warum schweigen Sie dazu? Warum verurteilen Sie das nicht? Es braucht Gerechtigkeit f脙录r die Pal脙陇stinenser, es braucht Frieden f脙录r die Pal脙陇stinenser脗聽芒聙聯 Sie reden nur.

Ihre Doppelmoral wird von niemandem in dieser Welt mehr akzeptiert, verstehen Sie es endlich. Sie m脙录ssen aufstehen! Die Geschichte wird 脙录ber Sie urteilen, Frau Kallas! Sie wird 脙录ber alle urteilen, die das doch unterst脙录tzt haben.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Ich m脙露chte hier nur einmal darauf hinweisen, dass jeder Abgeordnete in diesem Hause das gleiche Gewicht hat.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Hohes Haus! Lange bevor dieser Krieg, der schon so viele Jahrzehnte die V脙露lker der Israelis und der Pal脙陇stinenser beutelt, in die neueste Runde gegangen ist, gab es eine Reihe von Gerichtsverfahren und Demonstrationen innerhalb des israelischen Rechtsstaates. Diese richteten sich gegen die Korruption von Benjamin Netanjahu. Gl脙录cklicherweise f脙录r Benjamin Netanjahu konnte er sodann einen Krieg f脙录hren, der sein Volk und die Welt脙露ffentlichkeit von dieser offensichtlichen Korruption ablenkte.

Diesen Krieg, ausgel脙露st durch das furchtbare Verbrechen der Hamas am 7.脗聽Oktober, das Israel auf v脙露lkerrechtlicher Ebene durchaus das Recht zur Selbstverteidigung gab, f脙录hrt er seitdem unter eklatanter Missachtung des V脙露lkerrechts. Zusammen mit seinen rechtsextremen Ministern f脙陇hrt er eine Politik, die Israel unw脙录rdig ist und Israel den Hass und die Wut der arabischen V脙露lker auf Generationen sichern wird. Benjamin Netanjahu und seine Regierung sind innere Feinde Israels, gegen die wir uns stellen sollten.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Ende der spontanen Wortmeldungen)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kaja Kallas, Vice-President of the Commission / High Representative of the Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, thank you for this debate. I have a few remarks. First, we have not been silent. On famine we had a statement on 22脗聽August. We have been very clear about this.

When I say that I'm frustrated, I'm frustrated that we can't do more, and this is what I'm constantly working on. And that is why we are trying to improve the situation. If you think that I don't empathise with the people, that I don't feel the suffering of the people, this is not true. That is why I work hard to really improve the situation and help the people on the ground.

To the leader of The Left Group here, Ms脗聽Aubry, and Ms脗聽Martins 芒聙聯 of脗聽course, neither of them are here when I respond 芒聙聯 I understand that the people of Europe don't know how Europe functions. So you can point at one person and say, why don't you make this decision and everything works out well, whereas the MEPs, especially leaders of the groups, should know how Europe functions. We are not run by executive orders. We are run by collective decision芒聙聭making, which means that we have 27脗聽countries in the Council, and as we have proposed measures that require qualified majority, we don't have that. So work with the Member States of yours. I was hearing many representatives of Member States here. Work with them so that they also agree to these decisions.

Also in the Commission we are rendering decisions by consensus, and we also have those Member States represented in the Commission. So you can point your finger at me, but I really assure you, I try to do my best to improve the situation on the ground, and I will remain in contact with my counterpart in Israel, because I will also be raising all these difficult issues that you are mentioning here. And I also urge you to call your Israeli counterparts and say that you do not approve of this situation, also to address the difficult questions we have.

Of course, we must be calling for a permanent ceasefire and the release of the hostages. The EU is committed to supporting the two芒聙聭state solution. This is where we are united and we will continue the work. So I do not agree with Ms脗聽Demirel that I have given a green light for what Israel is doing. That is not true. I have raised all these issues with my Israeli counterpart every time.

But I urge you to also learn how the European Union functions. Here you could also see the division amongst the Member States. This is the same that I'm up against in the Council and in the College. I haven't been silent at all, but I'm not yelling, maybe, as you are yelling. So I haven't been silent at all. We have had strong statements and of course, like I said, we try to do more every day, and I urge you to support us in this endeavour.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 I have received seven motions for a resolution to wind up this debate.

This debate is now closed.

The vote will be held on Thursday.

Schriftliche Erkl脙陇rungen (Artikel 178)

脗聽脗聽Matja脜戮 Nemec (S&D), pisno. 芒聙聯 Spo脜隆tovani, V Gazi ljudje 脜戮ivijo v peklu. Vsak dan 脜隆e naprej umirajo v vrstah za hrano. Izrael Gazo sistemati脛聧no bri脜隆e z zemljevida zgodovine, strada prebivalstvo in izvaja genocid. To potrjujejo tako Zdru脜戮eni narodi, najuglednej脜隆i pravni strokovnjaki kot tudi 脜隆tevilne izraelske organizacije. Ravnanje Izraela mora jasno obsoditi tudi Evropski parlament, ki od marca 2024 mol脛聧i. Resolucija mora biti zato sprejeta! Evropska unija mora kon脛聧no zavzeti nedvoumno stali脜隆脛聧e do vojnih zlo脛聧inov in uvesti sankcije proti storilcem. Potrebujemo premirje in odpravo blokade humanitarne pomo脛聧i. Jasneje moramo podpreti tudi glasove miru in razuma, ki opravljajo svoje delo kljub sankcijam Zdru脜戮enih dr脜戮av proti sodnikom in to脜戮ilcem Mednarodnega kazenskega sodi脜隆脛聧a 芒聙聯 med njimi tudi slovenski sodnici Beti Hohler. Pozivam Komisijo, da 脛聧im prej aktivira akt EU o blokiranju, kot sta to zahtevali Belgija in Slovenija, in se odzove na nedavne sankcije ZDA proti sodnikoma Mednarodnega kazenskega sodi脜隆脛聧a. Podpreti moramo tudi Francesco Albanese, kandidatko za Nobelovo nagrado za mir. Ta teden imamo vsi prilo脜戮nost, da se postavimo na pravo stran zgodovine. Ne zamudimo je! Hvala.
