5. Formal sitting 芒聙聯 Address by Maia Sandu, President of the Republic of Moldova
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next item is the address by Maia Sandu, President of the Republic of Moldova.

Dear colleagues, it is my pleasure to introduce the President of the Republic of Moldova, Maia Sandu, who is with us here once again today.

Dear Maia, you have long been a valued friend to this Parliament and it is truly an honour to welcome you back to Europe's House of Democracy. We are all deeply impressed by your determination, your courage, and your steadfast commitment to the Moldovan people. Your tireless work steering Moldova along the path of democracy and integration with our Union is an inspiration to us all, and a testament to your leadership.

As Moldova takes its next steps on its European journey, know, dear Maia, that this Parliament stands firmly with you and all Moldovans in upholding our shared European values of democracy, peace and the rule of law. We are ramping up investment in Moldova's future via the EU-Moldova Facility, and we look forward to opening Moldova's accession negotiations.

These are challenging times for Europe. Freedoms that we thought were permanent are being tested, disinformation is weakening public trust in democracy, and Russia's brutal war continues in Ukraine. But Europe is resilient, and I know that together we will emerge stronger and more united than before.

Just weeks ago 芒聙聯 we were just talking about this 芒聙聯 European leaders gathered in Chi脠聶in脛聝u in just such a display of unity, reaffirming Europe's unwavering support for Moldova's European future. And today, in this Hemicycle, this House stands with you once again, dear President Sandu, dear Maia, united in our support for the Republic of Moldova and its democratic path to Union membership.

So, President, the floor is yours.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maia Sandu, President of the Republic of Moldova. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, thank you for this invitation. It is a great honour to speak on behalf of Moldova's people in this House of Democracy.

Today I would like to focus on the growing threats to our democracies and how we defend them together, because no one can be defended alone. I will do so through the story of Moldova, because our stability is your stability and our peace is Europe's peace.

On 28脗聽September脗聽2025, Moldova will hold the most consequential election in its history. Its outcome will decide whether we consolidate as a stable democracy on the path to EU membership, a safe neighbour to Ukraine and a security provider to the Union, or whether Russia destabilises us, pulls us away from Europe and turns us into a threat on Ukraine's southwestern border and Europe's eastern frontier.

Ladies and gentlemen, before I begin, let us recall one truth: most of your democracies were not complete when you joined this Union. You fought dictatorships, overcame economic hardships, and not one of you did it alone. Every democracy in this Chamber was built, supported and protected with the help of others. Germany and France, devastated by war, chose reconciliation. With Italy, Belgium, the Netherlands and Luxembourg, they laid Europe's foundation: fragile at first, but together strong.脗聽When Ireland joined, solidarity transformed it. Denmark showed that even established democracies chose the Union to secure their future. Then came southern Europe: Greece, Spain, Portugal, less than a decade after dictatorship. Their democracies were sheltered and consolidated inside the Union. Austria, Finland and Sweden followed 芒聙聯 neutral countries, they reinforced their own security and Europe's. Then came the great enlargement of central and eastern Europe: Czechia, Estonia, Hungary, Latvia, Lithuania, Poland, Slovakia, Slovenia. Barely 15脗聽years after communism, they grew stronger and more prosperous inside the EU. Cyprus and Malta also entered 芒聙聯 proof that no democracy is too small to matter. Romania and Bulgaria followed, less than two decades after shaking off their socialist past. Still with much to build at the time, accession gave them stability and the space to grow. Croatia, scarred by war, found peace and security in this family.

Today, Moldova, Ukraine and the Western Balkans stand at the gates, living proof that the European project is thriving 芒聙聯 still expanding, still protecting. Both Moldova and Ukraine are ready to advance to the next stage of negotiations in a merit-based process that reflects our progress, and both our democracies will be safer once we are inside the Union. That is why I say: this Union was never about perfection. It was about the protection of fragile democracies until they grew strong. And it should remain so today, because democracy does not come automatically. It comes after hardship, after sacrifice and above all, with support from friends. That is Moldova's lesson. And it is also Moldova's warning. If our democracy cannot be protected, then no democracy in Europe is safe.

Honourable Members, last month we celebrated 34脗聽years of independence. In the past three decades, Moldova built and preserved a living democracy. Fragile, yes, but enduring. We survived a Russian-backed separatist conflict, an economic collapse, energy blackmail, trade embargoes, even a banking fraud that shook the very foundations of our state. Each crisis could have broken us, but democracy resisted. When democracy was in danger, people protested. When oligarchs tried to capture the state, we resisted. And when power changed, it changed peacefully at the ballot box. Unlike in some other places across the former Soviet Union, Moldova did not become an autocracy. We preserved pluralism. We protected free elections. We remained faithful to who we are: Europeans. For the past two decades, the European Union, together with the United States, has been our steadfast partner 芒聙聯 helping us reform, modernise and build institutions strong enough to resist pressure, giving Moldovans hope that our place was and is in Europe.

Then came 24脗聽February脗聽2022; when Russia launched a full-scale invasion of Ukraine, we knew we could not wait. We needed safety and we understood that the sooner we are inside the European Union 芒聙聯 a peace project that has never seen war between its members 芒聙聯 the safer our democracy and our people will be. That is why we applied for accession just days after Russian tanks rolled into our neighbourhood. We have obtained candidate status and opened accession talks. We are not asking for shortcuts, we are doing our homework diligently. But for us, accession is not just a technocratic process. It is a race against time to anchor our democracy inside the Union, where it will be protected from the greatest threat we face: Russia.

Ladies and gentlemen, Moldova cannot escape its geography. We feel the long arm of Russian aggression. We share a 1脗聽200-kilometre border with Ukraine 芒聙聯 longer than any of your countries. And the reason Moldova still enjoys peace today is because Ukraine stands. Ukrainians are fighting to defend their country and their freedom, and they also shield Moldova. We owe our peace to them.

But their sacrifice is also a reminder: peace is never guaranteed. That is why our European path is not just a matter of values 芒聙聯 it is a matter of survival. And precisely because we have advanced greatly on this path, Russia has unleashed its full arsenal of hybrid attacks against us. The battlefield is our elections. Last year, during the presidential poll and the referendum to anchor EU accession into our constitution, interference was unprecedented. But democracy prevailed: Moldovans stood their ground and, through their authentic votes, they defeated the Kremlin's political fronts. Our institutions stood with them: alert, determined, resilient. Now the next battle is upon us: the parliamentary elections at the end of this month.

Ladies and gentlemen, we have resisted many pressures from Moscow in the past. Our independence was hard-won through economic and political hardships and the Russian-backed conflict on our soil. And whenever we exercised our free choice for real, Moscow struck back, cutting gas, banning our wine and fruit, stirring the Transnistrian region. But today we face an unlimited hybrid war on a scale unseen before the full invasion of Ukraine. The Kremlin's goal is clear: to capture Moldova through the ballot box, to use it against Ukraine, and to turn us into a launchpad for hybrid attacks in the European Union. That is why this election is very important: by defending it, we protect not only Moldova, but also regional security and stability.

Ladies and gentlemen, interference does not begin and end on election day; it starts months before and lingers long after. Like a virus, it finds the cracks and attacks. We had barely recovered from Russia's interference last year when, in January, we were plunged into a manufactured energy crisis designed to raise prices, leave the Transnistrian region in cold and darkness, and divide citizens on both banks of the Nistru River. Then came the money: illicit financing poured in through crypto, shell companies and prepaid cards. Last year, in a single day, over EUR脗聽1脗聽million in cash was intercepted at Chi脠聶in脛聝u airport and our institutions estimated that, over the course of the year, Russia spent the equivalent of 1脗聽%脗聽of脗聽Moldova's GDP to influence last year's elections.脗聽Today, new money bankrolls Kremlin-backed parties, buys influence, poisons democracy.

Last year, it went further into something our democracy had never faced before: a vote-buying scheme. A sanctioned Russian bank opened 138脗聽000脗聽accounts to sway results with direct payments. It is not something we are proud of, but it shows how Russia identifies vulnerabilities and exploits them. This is democracy itself turned into a target.

Russian proxies also fund protests orchestrated on Telegram, with transport arranged, people lured by promises of thousands of euros, lies spread, fake emails in the names of state institutions, deepfakes of politicians and fabricated international sites posing as impartial news but serving the Kremlin's propaganda. And on social media, a Romanian think-tank found that just 100脗聽coordinated accounts pushed videos with 13脗聽million views in a single month. Their comments 芒聙聯 thousands of copy-pasted lines 芒聙聯 reveal manipulation, not genuine debate. Cyberattacks hit government services. Last year, the postal service was struck because it delivers pensions where banks cannot. Phishing campaigns target officials, religion is weaponised, criminal groups are recruited for sabotage and intimidation. A judge ruling recently on political corruption received death threats. Our diaspora is targeted with online campaigns to divide families abroad and at home. Last year, 14脗聽polling stations across EU countries received false bomb threats to disrupt the vote. In Gagauzia, false claims spread that autonomy is under threat. In the Transnistrian region, calls for more polling stations are nothing more than a tactic to fabricate turnout well beyond real voter levels.

And this is happening now. It is coordinated so as to overwhelm our institutions and stretch limited resources. It is financed with hundreds of millions of euros 芒聙聯 dirty money 芒聙聯 and amplified by propaganda. It is made more dangerous by three features. It constantly changes: new money channels, new tricks, new narratives of disinformation. It is digital: payments on telegram, lies on TikTok, deepfakes on Facebook and Instagram 芒聙聯 more than 80脗聽% of toxic content on TikTok is AI-generated. And it exploits democracy itself: freedom of religion turned into propaganda, freedom of assembly into paid protests, freedom of association into instant Kremlin-backed parties and free capital flows into illicit money into politics. This is not only Moldova's story: crypto schemes tested in Chi脠聶in脛聝u脗聽now help evade EU sanctions and fund Russia's war machine. Vote-buying schemes tried in Moldova have surfaced elsewhere. False bomb threats, first seen in our elections, have already disrupted elections in other countries. Moldova may be the testing ground, but Europe is the target.

Honourable Members, this brings me to the heart of today's challenge: defending our democracies with teeth, and together.脗聽The threats we face are faster, sharper, less visible. If the threats are new, our responses must be new. Too often we face 21st脗聽century dangers with tools designed for peacetime. Yet war has returned to Europe, and we, Europe, must rethink how we defend our democracies. The Cold War offers lessons. Fragile democracies were shielded by economic resilience 芒聙聯 the Marshall Plan rebuilt shattered economies. They were defended in an ideological fight: Radio Free Europe exposed dictatorships, poverty and repression. And they survived because citizens knew freedom was not given, it was safeguarded. That is the spirit we need today, and Moldova knows this not from theory, but from experience. On the front line of democracy, we have learned what works and what fails.

These are our lessons. First, cut off illicit financing and protect elections. Dirty money is the lifeblood of foreign malign influence. Unless we cut it, every other defence is undermined. Elections especially must be shielded from it. Election observation must go beyond polling stations and follow the money, track information manipulation across platforms and expose covert influence. Second, build resilience. Resilience means energy security, interconnected infrastructure and market integration so that no democracy can be blackmailed. But resilience also means democracy that delivers: jobs, justice and opportunities. Third, engage citizens and win the ideological fight. Even when democracy delivers, we must involve people directly with clear information, free media and open debate while exposing autocracies for what they are: war, corruption and destruction. Fourth, strengthen deterrence and coordination. Aggression must be made too costly with stronger defences, intelligence and cyber capabilities, but also with stronger coordination. Hybrid threats exploit weak links. Europe must join forces and act faster. And fifth, integrate and innovate. Candidate countries must be part of the defence of democracy. No one is safe until everyone is safe. And, just as the Marshall Plan and Radio Free Europe were created then, today we need new tools: rapid digital forensics, AI labelling, joint cyber defence, new ways to organise citizens.

That is how Europe has endured: by adapting, rebuilding, turning fragility into strength. By protecting fragile democracies until they grow strong. The only way forward is to defend our democracies with teeth, and also defend them together.

Honourable Members, Moldova is not alone in protecting its democracy: the European Union has stood with us financially, technically and politically, and we are deeply grateful. This solidarity strengthens our ability to protect democracy at home while also allowing us to contribute to Europe's common security. At the same time, Moldova has gained hard experience in countering hybrid threats and we are ready to share it, because Moldova already thinks like a member and acts like a member, and that too is part of Europe's security.

Ladies and gentlemen, allow me now, from this House of European Democracy, to turn to my own people on the eve of a decisive choice.

Dragi cet脛聝脠聸eni, sa fim m脙垄ndri c脛聝 am construit o democra脠聸ie vie 脠聶i am p脛聝strat-o timp de trei decenii, chiar 脠聶i atunci c脙垄nd unii au vrut s脛聝 o distrug脛聝. Am trecut prin multe 脙庐ncerc脛聝ri 脠聶i am rezistat 脠聶i de fiecare dat脛聝 am ie脠聶it mai puternici. Ast脛聝zi 脙庐ns脛聝, independen脠聸a 脠聶i pacea noastr脛聝 sunt din nou puse la 脙庐ncercare: ingerin脠聸e 脙庐n alegerile noastre, finan脠聸脛聝ri ilegale din afar脛聝, campanii de minciuni, atacuri cibernetice, proteste pl脛聝tite, tactici de a sem脛聝na ur脛聝 脙庐ntre oameni. Acestea sunt metodele prin care se 脙庐ncearc脛聝 oprirea Moldovei din drumul ei european. Depinde de noi dac脛聝 aceste presiuni vor reu脠聶i s脛聝 ne opreasc脛聝 sau, dimpotriv脛聝, ne vor face mai uni脠聸i 脠聶i mai hot脛聝r脙垄脠聸i.

Anul trecut am ar脛聝tat lumii 脙庐ntregi c脛聝 putem sta drep脠聸i, prin vot, 脙庐n fa脠聸a unor for脠聸e mult mai mari. Acum trebuie s脛聝 facem pasul decisiv, s脛聝 alegem un Parlament care s脛聝 duc脛聝 Moldova 脙庐n Uniunea European脛聝. Aceasta este responsabilitatea noastr脛聝. Viitorul Moldovei depinde de curajul 脠聶i unitatea cu care vom merge la vot.

Honourable Members, I thank you for giving me the chance to speak to you today and for standing with Moldova on our European path.

(The House rose and accorded the speaker a standing ovation)

脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

7. Announcement by the President

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 We start with the extension of the term of office of the Special Committee on the European Democracy Shield and of the Special Committee on the Housing Crisis in the European Union. The Conference of Presidents, at its meeting of 3 September 2025, approved the requests by the Special Committee on the European Democracy Shield and the Special Committee on the Housing Crisis to extend their mandate by six months.

These extensions are necessary, dear colleagues, to enable the special committees to fulfil their mandates and tasks. If there are no objections 芒聙聯 and you will need to tell me if there are 芒聙聯 to the decision of the Conference of Presidents, these extensions will be deemed adopted.

8.2. Waste Framework Directive: textiles and food waste (A10-0144/2025 - Anna Zalewska) (vote)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next vote is on the recommendation for second reading by Anna Zalewska, on behalf of the Committee on the Environment, Climate and Food Safety, on the Waste Framework Directive: textiles and food waste脗聽(see minutes, item 8.2).

I give the floor to the rapporteur Anna Zalewska.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anna Zalewska, Sprawozdawczyni. 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Podczas negocjacji dyrektywy odpadowej przy脜聸wieca脜聜y mi dwa cele: zapewnienie realnego ograniczenia odpad脙鲁w poprzez zmiany w legislacji przy jednoczesnym zapewnieniu realistycznego i proporcjonalnego w swojej implementacji podej脜聸cia do potrzeby redukcji odpad脙鲁w 脜录ywno脜聸ciowych i tekstylnych.

Cieszy mnie podej脜聸cie wypracowane w kwestii obowi脛聟zku ograniczenia odpad脙鲁w spo脜录ywczych przez pa脜聞stwa cz脜聜onkowskie. Wprowadzone cele s脛聟 niskie i akceptowalne dla pa脜聞stw cz脜聜onkowskich. Nie s脛聟 one oparte na karach, ale na wspieraniu pa脜聞stw cz脜聜onkowskich w podejmowaniu dzia脜聜a脜聞 maj脛聟cych na celu ograniczenie wyrzucania i marnotrawienia 脜录ywno脜聸ci. W trakcie negocjacji uda脜聜o nam si脛聶 zapewni脛聡, i脜录 rolnictwo i rolnicy nie b脛聶d脛聟 obj脛聶ci celami dyrektywy, czyli obowi脛聟zkami redukcji odpad脙鲁w 脜录ywno脜聸ciowych. Wzmocnili脜聸my r脙鲁wnie脜录 pozycj脛聶 rolnik脙鲁w w stosunku do nieuczciwych praktyk prowadzonych przez operator脙鲁w supermarket脙鲁w, takich jak na przyk脜聜ad anulowanie na ostatni脛聟 chwil脛聶 zam脙鲁wie脜聞 脜聸wie脜录ych produkt脙鲁w b脛聟d脜潞 dyskryminacja tak zwanych brzydkich owoc脙鲁w i warzyw.

W ramach wynegocjowanej dyrektywy uda脜聜o nam si脛聶 r脙鲁wnie脜录 znacznie zmniejszy脛聡 nadmiern脛聟 biurokracj脛聶, mi脛聶dzy innymi przez wykluczenie z zakresu dyrektywy mikroprzedsi脛聶biorc脙鲁w i wprowadzenie uproszczonego raportowania dla pewnych podmiot脙鲁w. Ponadto ws脜聜uchali脜聸my si脛聶 w potrzeby pa脜聞stw cz脜聜onkowskich i znacznie wyd脜聜u脜录yli脜聸my terminy implementacyjne dla administracji. Dzi脛聶ki temu b脛聶dziemy mieli pewno脜聸脛聡, 脜录e planowane zmiany b脛聶d脛聟 dobre, realistyczne do spe脜聜nienia i zapewni脛聟 prawdziw脛聟 redukcj脛聶 odpad脙鲁w, czyli cel, kt脙鲁ry 脜聜脛聟czy nas wszystkich na tej sali.

Wierz脛聶, 脜录e porozumienie w sprawie dyrektywy odpadowej to 脜聸wietny przyk脜聜ad na to, jak m脛聟drze mo脜录na po脜聜脛聟czy脛聡 trosk脛聶 o 脜聸rodowisko naturalne z uproszczon脛聟 legislacj脛聟, kt脙鲁ra zmniejsza biurokracj脛聶. Prosz脛聶 Pa脜聞stwa o g脜聜osowanie za wynegocjowan脛聟 przeze mnie dyrektyw脛聟. Licz脛聶 na powt脙鲁rzenie wyniku w g脜聜osowaniu nad porozumieniem w komisji ENVI i dzi脛聶kuj脛聶 wsp脙鲁脜聜pracownikom i sprawozdawcom.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 I have received no amendments or motions to reject the Council position.

The Council decision is therefore deemed approved and the act is deemed adopted.

8.4. EU-Brazil Agreement: cooperation with and through Europol and the Federal Police of Brazil (A10-0142/2025 - Nikola Bart脜炉脜隆ek) (vote)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next vote is on the report by Nikola Bart脜炉脜隆ek, on behalf of the Committee on Civil Liberties, Justice and Home Affairs, on the EU Brazil-Agreement: cooperation with and through Europol and the Federal Police of Brazil (see minutes, item 8.4).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

芒聙聯 Before the vote:

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nikola Bart脜炉脜隆ek, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, ladies and gentlemen, dear colleagues, cooperation between Europol and the Brazilian police is the key to tackling crimes that affect both our regions.脗聽By sharing information, we can identify criminals, trafficking routes and illegal shipments much earlier. Drug trafficking into the EU remains a growing threat. Human trafficking and smuggling networks exploit migration routes. Money laundering of drug profits corrupts our financial systems. And firearms trafficking can escalate the violence on our streets.

These problems are infiltrating our cities and putting the lives of our citizens and children at risk. That is why it is essential for the authorities to exchange information and cooperate. All of this will be done with respect to personal data safeguards.

Thank you for supporting this report and the partnership between Europol and Brazil's Federal Police. It will help improve the safety of our citizens.

脗聽

8.5. EU-Kyrgyz Republic Enhanced Partnership and Cooperation Agreement (A10-0105/2025 - Nacho S脙隆nchez Amor) (vote)
Video of the speeches

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next vote is on the recommendation by Nacho S脙隆nchez Amor, on behalf of the Committee on Foreign Affairs, on the EU-Kyrgyz Republic Enhanced Partnership and Cooperation Agreement (see minutes, item 8.5).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

芒聙聯 Before the vote:

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nacho S脙隆nchez Amor, ponente. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, en primer lugar, quiero agradecer el trabajo de los ponentes alternativos, con los que he tenido una colaboraci脙鲁n excelente.

La Rep脙潞blica Kirguisa es uno de nuestros principales socios en una regi脙鲁n en la que tenemos intereses en materia de seguridad, conectividad, diversificaci脙鲁n estrat脙漏gica, resoluci脙鲁n de conflictos y defensa del multilateralismo.

El anterior Acuerdo ten脙颅a ya veinticinco a脙卤os y parece l脙鲁gico que se haya revisado a esta altura. Tiene un ambicioso cap脙颅tulo de comercio y de desarrollo sostenible, con una inclusi脙鲁n de derechos laborales bastante notable, pol脙颅ticas de cambio clim脙隆tico y apertura a empresas e inversores europeos y la importancia tambi脙漏n de las materias primas cr脙颅ticas. Tambi脙漏n refuerza la cooperaci脙鲁n en el 脙隆mbito de la pol脙颅tica exterior y de seguridad, en prevenci脙鲁n de conflictos y en terrorismo.

Sin embargo, seguimos con preocupaci脙鲁n la tendencia hacia un modelo pol脙颅tico securitario que est脙隆 erosionando r脙隆pidamente los est脙隆ndares democr脙隆ticos de lo que un d脙颅a fue considerada una isla de democracia. Y esperamos que este Acuerdo ofrezca a la Comisi脙鲁n nuevos instrumentos para invertir esta tendencia.

脗聽脗聽El presidente. 芒聙聯 Como bien saben, el acta de la sesi脙鲁n de ayer y los textos aprobados en ella est脙隆n a su plena disposici脙鲁n. Entiendo que nadie quiere intervenir al respecto, por lo que se aprueba el acta de la sesi脙鲁n.

脗聽

11. Devastating wildfires in Southern Europe: the need to strengthen EU aid to restore the massive loss of forests and enhancing EU preparedness (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽El presidente. 芒聙聯 El punto siguiente en el orden del d脙颅a es el debate a partir de las declaraciones del Consejo y la Comisi脙鲁n sobre los incendios forestales devastadores en el sur de Europa y la necesidad de reforzar la ayuda de la Uni脙鲁n para revertir la p脙漏rdida masiva de bosques y mejorar la preparaci脙鲁n de la Uni脙鲁n (2025/2854(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, Commissioner, this summer, wildfires caused major destruction in Europe. They severely damaged our environment, but also our towns and economies. Sadly, human lives were lost. Our thoughts are with the families of those lost.

The situation is tragic. During only 10 days in mid-August, the burned area in both Portugal and Spain was over 500脗聽000 hectares. This is the highest surface of burned areas ever recorded in mid-August in those two countries. In recent years, wildfires have also hit regions in Europe which were not so exposed earlier. They now go well beyond the Mediterranean region. This is the new normal.

Climate change is an underlying cause of wildfire. It is not a coincidence that 2025 was the warmest summer recorded in Europe. With climate change, wildfires have become more common and more severe. We talk less about climate change than we did just five years ago, but it is still happening and we still need to deal with it.

That is why the fight against climate change is a priority for us. The Danish Presidency aims to reach an agreement in Council this month on our 2040 climate target and the EU contribution to the COP30 in Brazil. It is ambitious and we don't have much time. We are working very hard to make it happen. That is why preventing wildfires starts with strong policies to mitigate and adapt to climate change.

On mitigation: the EU has been supporting global efforts with one of the most ambitious climate frameworks. The EU and its Member States are also committed to increasing the capacity to adapt, building resilience and reducing exposure to extreme events like wildfires. This is a key aspect of the EU adaptation strategy.

When the wildfires came this summer, Member States made huge efforts to respond as quickly as possible. The Union Civil Protection Mechanism was activated several times, again showing its worth. Yet, this summer proved that dealing with wildfires in the middle of heatwaves is very challenging. We need to be better prepared to respond to wildfires, just like other risks and threats that the Union faces.

Our leaders have stressed several times the importance of more coordination in the areas of preparedness and resilience. The presentation of the Preparedness Union strategy by the Commission and the High Representative in March this year must be seen in this context. Europe needs to move from a reactive approach to a preparedness-by-design approach 芒聙聯 one that anticipates, prevents and responds to a wide range of crises.

Madam脗聽President, honourable Members, Commissioner, preparedness, resilience and crisis management are a clear priority of the Danish Presidency. Our citizens expect us to do better. Some of the policy actions in the Preparedness Union strategy have been, or will be, presented by the Commission during our Presidency, and we will examine them closely in the Council.

In particular, we welcome the legislative proposal on the revision of the Union Civil Protection Mechanism presented in late July. We have started its examination as a priority and aim to get as far as possible. The mechanism is certainly an EU success story. It coordinates the support to a Member State that is overwhelmed. Since its start, the mechanism has helped many communities. This includes those hit by forest fires. This is why the revision of the mechanism is so important. It needs to keep delivering the successful cooperation it does today.

Once our respective institutions have agreed on our position on the proposal, we look forward to negotiating with the Parliament on this important matter.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hadja Lahbib, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, Minister脗聽Bjerre, honourable Members, this has been a record summer of wildfires across southern Europe, but this has also been a summer of action and a summer of solidarity. Year after year, we see how climate change makes disasters worse, including wildfires. The 2024 European climate risk assessment ranked them among the top climate risks.

Global warming makes extreme weather like this about 40 times more likely and not all regions are heating at the same pace. Europe is the fastest warming continent. Since the 1980s, Europe has warmed at twice the global average. That means we must be more ready than ever for heatwaves and wildfires.

Dear colleagues, this year's wildfires did not take us by surprise. The Commission took strong action to prepare for this summer. A team of wildfire experts were monitoring the situation hour by hour at our Emergency Response Coordination Centre. The Commission also prepositioned more than 670 firefighters from 14 European countries in Spain, Portugal, Greece, France and Italy, ready to support local teams on the ground. Our wildfire fleet was ready for action and our Copernicus Emergency Management Service stood ready to provide satellite images to Member States.

Despite all these preparations, more than one million hectares had burned in the EU by 3脗聽September. This is over three times the 2006-2024 average for this time of year and the most since 2006. That's an area, for instance, larger than Cyprus, or almost a third the size of my country, Belgium. Spain and Portugal alone account for more than half of the destruction.

Since the start of the summer, the Union's Civil Protection Mechanism has been activated 18 times to fight wildfires in Europe and beyond, including in Israel and in Syria. Albania, Greece and North Macedonia each called for help three times. Bosnia and Herzegovina, Cyprus, Bulgaria, Montenegro, Spain and Portugal also relied on this support. Yes, this has been a challenging summer for wildfires, but it has also been what I call the summer of solidarity. When one country calls, the European Union responds, united and in solidarity.

At the heart of this solidarity was the UCPM and its 24/7 Emergency Response Coordination Centre. Together they deployed 58 aircrafts, 38 planes and 20 helicopters, and more than 740 firefighters and rescuers. I want to pay tribute to them, particularly those who lost their lives while fighting the fires. My thoughts are with their families, their loved ones and their colleagues. Their courage and dedication save lives, homes and landscapes.

Our wildfire fleet showed once again that it is indispensable. Soon, the EU will be equipped with its own fleet of 12 new planes and several helicopters, and Copernicus provided its rapid mapping service 32 times to guide operations in real time.

Honourable Members, this year's wildfires send a clear message. We are improving, anticipating, preventing and responding better and quicker, but we must do much more on prevention and preparedness.

That is why this spring, the Commission adopted the preparedness Union strategy, a forward芒聙聭looking plan to make Europe better prepared for the crises of tomorrow. It takes an all芒聙聭hazards approach, embedding preparedness into every EU policy and strengthening cooperation across all levels of government and society. It will make our Emergency Response Coordination Centre more proactive and integrate Copernicus more closely into national early warning and planning systems.

We are also strengthening rescEU, our strategic reserve of disaster response capacities and stockpiles. Originally created for wildfires, rescEU now responds to earthquakes, storms, floods and supports Ukraine. But to stay effective, it needs investments, fresh capacity, replenished stockpiles, and long芒聙聭term readiness.

As part of the stockpiling strategy, the Commission proposes to bring existing reserves together and explore new capacities where Europe can add the most added value. These efforts are part of the ongoing revision of the Union Civil Protection Mechanism, including in the MFF package. Preparedness by design is at the heart of the preparedness Union strategy. It means building preparedness into every policy area, including wildfires of course.

Preparedness investments will also be central to the new national and regional partnership plans, part of the MFF proposal. Preparedness is now a core objective of the national and regional partnership plans, giving Member States a chance to design tailor芒聙聭made investments for their own needs.

Population preparedness is central to our preparedness Union strategy. It gives citizens, local communities and vulnerable groups the knowledge and skills to prevent response, but also to recover from wildfires. The citizens, local communities and vulnerable groups also bring their valuable expertise and skills to support societal preparedness and resilience.

Dear colleagues, the message from this summer is, I think, loud and clear: Europe must focus more on preventing wildfires before they start. We need more well芒聙聭targeted investment and smarter policies to build fire芒聙聭resilient landscapes.

This is not only about forests and farmland. Whole ecosystems have been destroyed, biodiversity is under threat and rural communities are left with deep and long芒聙聭lasting scars. The economic costs are also rising. Lost crops damage infrastructure and disrupt livelihoods, all adding to the human suffering.

That is why we are developing an ambitious European framework for climate resilience, to be adopted in the second half of next year. It will set out how the EU will tackle the climate challenges ahead, supporting Member States, candidate countries and our wider neighbourhoods.

Preventing catastrophic wildfires will take both traditional investment in infrastructure and ecosystem芒聙聭based strategies. Active management of high risk areas through measures like grazing or even controlled burning, can greatly reduce the risk of future fires. Nature restoration and wildfire prevention go hand in hand and we encourage Member States to integrate both in their landscape planning.

Of course, primary responsibility lies with Member States, but the EU supports prevention through several funds: the Agricultural Fund for Rural Development, the cohesion policy, and the Recovery and Resilience Facility. Together, these instruments help across the full disaster cycle: prevention, preparedness, response and recovery.

According to the European Court of Auditors, at least EUR脗聽3.5 billion in EU funding has been allocated to tackle forest fires between 2021-2027, reducing the vulnerability of more than 130脗聽million people. Our common agricultural policy, for instance, helps maintain landscapes that can resist and recover from wildfires, reducing losses.

The EU also funds forest restoration and fire prevention through direct grants like the LIFE programme, research under Horizon Europe and transition support via the Just Transition Mechanism. All of this is aligned with the European Green Deal and global biodiversity goals. And just as importantly, these efforts protect local economies and help revive rural areas.

The EU also shows solidarity after disasters by helping Member States and candidate countries recover. This is the role of the European Union's Solidarity Fund. It provides support when the total damage of a disaster exceeds the threshold set out in the regulation. Since the creation of the fund in 2022, four Member States have received assistance from the EUSF in relation to wildfires for a total amount of EUR脗聽151 million.

Reliable data on wildfire risk is essential for effective prevention and response. That is the main goal of the Commission's proposal for a forest monitoring regulation. So I urge you, honourable Members, to support this proposal in your plenary vote. They are vital for tracking and tackling wildfires.

Honourable Members, wildfires will remain a challenge, but we can still prepare better and reduce their impact to the minimum. With stronger prevention, smarter preparedness and deeper solidarity, Europe can protect its people, its forests and also its future.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ra脙潞l de la Hoz Quintano, en nombre del Grupo PPE. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, quiero comenzar agradeciendo las palabras de quienes me han precedido en el uso de la palabra. Lo digo especialmente yo, que vengo de Castilla y Le脙鲁n, de una de las regiones que, desgraciadamente, m脙隆s ha sufrido este verano los devastadores fuegos que han asolado el sur de Europa. Circunstancias meteorol脙鲁gicas extraordinarias que han provocado incendios extraordinarios en un n脙潞mero tambi脙漏n extraordinario.

Frente a ello, dos actitudes: la que acaban de mostrar la Comisi脙鲁n y el Consejo, que es la nuestra, la de la responsabilidad, la de la solidaridad, la unidad y el compromiso de cara al futuro, y la de aquellos que, una vez m脙隆s 芒聙聰lo vamos a ver despu脙漏s芒聙聰, intentan hacer una utilizaci脙鲁n partidista, intentando obtener ventaja pol脙颅tica de la desgracia, de la desolaci脙鲁n, hasta de la muerte.

No vamos a caer en la trampa, no vamos a utilizar ni esta intervenci脙鲁n ni este debate para evidenciar lo que el Gobierno de Espa脙卤a u otros Gobiernos han podido hacer mal, mejor o antes. Utilicemos este debate para que sirva de punto de inflexi脙鲁n para conseguir que, en el futuro, cuando vuelva a haber incendios 芒聙聰que los va a volver a haber芒聙聰, seamos capaces desde la Uni脙鲁n Europea 芒聙聰y tambi脙漏n desde los Estados芒聙聰 de dar una respuesta m脙隆s 脙隆gil, m脙隆s eficaz y m脙隆s r脙隆pida a los mismos.

Y eso no se consigue en absoluto con discursos demag脙鲁gicos. Se consigue, en primer lugar, implicando a los Estados en los planes de prevenci脙鲁n y tambi脙漏n en las estrategias forestales. Se consigue, igualmente, mejorando la coordinaci脙鲁n y la cooperaci脙鲁n transfronteriza. Y, por 脙潞ltimo, por supuesto, mejorando la financiaci脙鲁n de las pol脙颅ticas de prevenci脙鲁n, de extinci脙鲁n y tambi脙漏n de recuperaci脙鲁n de las zonas afectadas.

Se consigue, en definitiva, con ese compromiso que ha expresado la Comisi脙鲁n 芒聙聰y que nosotros compartimos plenamente芒聙聰 de que la pol脙颅tica forestal ha de convertirse, en el futuro, realmente en una pol脙颅tica de todos, en una pol脙颅tica comunitaria.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Leire Paj脙颅n, en nombre del Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora comisaria, se脙卤or脙颅as, este verano ha sido especialmente doloroso en Europa: Portugal, Grecia, Italia, Espa脙卤a, la monta脙卤a de Le脙鲁n, el Bierzo, Orense, C脙隆ceres, Asturias, Palencia han vivido los peores incendios en treinta脗聽a脙卤os, 130脗聽incendios en un solo mes, 330脗聽000脗聽hect脙隆reas quemadas por el fuego.

Hemos visto este verano, se脙卤or脙颅as, arder, con rabia y dolor, nuestro patrimonio cultural y natural, nuestros bosques, nuestra fauna, nuestros pueblos, nuestros recuerdos. Hemos perdido a vecinos que luchaban contra el fuego. Y, desde ese dolor, se脙卤or脙颅as, es donde estamos obligados a comprometernos m脙隆s contra estos incendios y, sobre todo, a ser conscientes de que vivimos en una emergencia clim脙隆tica 芒聙聰como ha dicho la comisaria芒聙聰 que multiplica hasta cuarenta veces los riesgos y las probabilidades de que estos incendios se vuelvan a producir. Tambi脙漏n las danas, que ocurrieron por ejemplo en Valencia.

Por eso, es importante una vez m脙隆s que entendamos que no basta con responder, debemos prevenir y prepararnos. Necesitamos planes de gesti脙鲁n forestal durante todo el a脙卤o. Necesitamos especies aut脙鲁ctonas e infraestructuras de 脙潞ltima generaci脙鲁n y, sobre todo, recursos humanos bien dotados y bien pagados. Necesitamos meter en la ecuaci脙鲁n la despoblaci脙鲁n, porque el abandono del pastoreo y el abandono de las tierras tienen mucho que ver. Necesitamos, por supuesto, un gran acuerdo pol脙颅tico, como plantea el presidente del Gobierno de Espa脙卤a. Necesitamos a todo el mundo y, sobre todo, necesitamos entender 芒聙聰de una vez por todas芒聙聰 que invertir no es despilfarrar, como han dicho algunos dirigentes, es prepararnos mejor para la respuesta.

Y necesitamos cumplir las leyes, esas que hemos aprobado aqu脙颅: la de la restauraci脙鲁n de la naturaleza, el Pacto Verde Europeo, todas las leyes que nos aprovechan para luchar mejor contra estos incendios.

Pero lo que no necesitamos, se脙卤or脙颅as, son la demagogia, pero sobre todo el negacionismo y la negligencia de quienes niegan el cambio clim脙隆tico, de quienes siembran la desinformaci脙鲁n y los bulos en las cat脙隆strofes, porque eso causa mucho m脙隆s dolor y retarda la respuesta.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ant脙鲁nio T脙垄nger Corr脙陋a, on behalf of the PfE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, Madam脗聽Commissioner, Madam脗聽Minister, first of all, I'd like to thank you very much for all the solidarity that was displayed by this House and by our friends in the European Union towards, not only this terrible happening that 3脗聽% of Portugal this year burned out and the loss of lives, but also for the solidarity vis-脙聽-vis the terrible accident with our elevator which killed 16 people in Lisbon.

Thank you for the solidarity. I'll speak Portuguese now.

Senhor Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, Senhora Ministra, eu devo dizer que todos os anos 脙漏 a mesma coisa, ou seja, todos os anos temos inc脙陋ndios. Este ano, arderam 3脗聽% da 脙隆rea de Portugal.脗聽E n脙拢o me venham com hist脙鲁rias que 脙漏 uma altera脙搂脙拢o do clima 芒聙聰 n脙拢o 脙漏. Grande parte s脙拢o fogos postos; h脙隆 m脙拢o humana nestes fogos. Portanto, n脙拢o vale a pena estarem com grandes teorias de altera脙搂脙碌es clim脙隆ticas se n脙鲁s n脙拢o prevenirmos que a m脙拢o humana fa脙搂a estragos nos nossos pa脙颅ses, por um lado.

Por outro lado, mais importante do que a solidariedade e os meios usados para combater os fogos: 脙漏 preciso que eles n脙拢o existam. 脙聣 preciso preven脙搂脙拢o para que n脙拢o haja fogos. Porque todos os anos 脙漏 a mesma coisa.

N脙鲁s dizemos isso em Portugal, mas agora estou aqui, perante a Europa, para dizer o seguinte: se nos querem ajudar, ajudem-nos a regulamentar o nosso territ脙鲁rio, ajudem a repopular as 脙隆rvores e a vegeta脙搂脙拢o do nosso pa脙颅s, ajudem com t脙漏cnicas que evitem fogos porque, uma vez que o fogo 脙漏 declarado, muito obrigado pela ajuda, mas j脙隆 脙漏 tarde.

N脙鲁s n脙拢o queremos palavras; n脙鲁s queremos a脙搂脙拢o. E 脙漏 isso que eu vos pe脙搂o, principalmente 脙聽 Comiss脙拢o Europeia e ao Conselho: sejam ativos, pr脙鲁-ativos, e tenham a脙搂脙拢o.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Diego Solier, en nombre del Grupo ECR. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora comisaria, ya lo dijimos en junio: los incendios se apagan en invierno, con prevenci脙鲁n, y ayudando al medio rural, a los campesinos, ganaderos y a quienes entienden y conocen los montes. Pero no se sab脙颅a... 脗驴No se pod脙颅a prevenir? Precipitaciones altas en primavera son gasolina para los montes en verano.

En lo que va de 2025 se han quemado cerca de 350脗聽000 hect脙隆reas en 223 incendios, bati脙漏ndose el r脙漏cord hist脙鲁rico del Sistema Europeo de Informaci脙鲁n sobre Incendios Forestales. Y mientras, las Administraciones regionales y nacionales se echan la culpa unas a otras, sin aportar soluciones efectivas. Y otra vez 芒聙聰una vez m脙隆s芒聙聰 la sociedad civil saliendo al rescate de nuevo.

Invertir en prevenci脙鲁n durante todo el a脙卤o es igual a menos gesti脙鲁n de crisis en verano. En la era de los datos tenemos informaci脙鲁n suficiente para actuar acorde y evitar p脙漏rdidas humanas, agr脙颅colas, medioambientales y patrimoniales.

Actuemos desde ya o el a脙卤o que viene estaremos en la misma situaci脙鲁n.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gr脙漏gory Allione, au nom du groupe Renew. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Madame la Commissaire, Madame la Ministre, chers coll脙篓gues, de toute ma carri脙篓re de sapeur-pompier, je n'ai jamais vu un 脙漏t脙漏 aussi d脙漏vastateur. J'ai 脙漏t脙漏 engag脙漏 dans l'Aude, et l'ampleur des feux de for脙陋t en Europe me laisse sans voix. Pour autant, nous n'avons pas le luxe de nous taire.

Inutile de vous dresser un bilan, les images ont parl脙漏 et chacun le sait: des milliers d'hectares partis en fum脙漏e, des personnes d脙漏c脙漏d脙漏es et des gens qui ont tout perdu. Une 脙漏vidence s'impose: ces feux hors norme sont une cons脙漏quence directe du d脙漏r脙篓glement climatique.

Pourtant, face 脙聽 cette r脙漏alit脙漏, on vient de l'entendre encore, l'extr脙陋me droite s'enferme dans le climato-scepticisme. Mais une certitude s'impose, le d脙漏ni ne nous prot脙漏gera jamais des flammes. Seules des actions concr脙篓tes le pourront.

Tout d'abord, face 脙聽 cette nouvelle g脙漏n脙漏ration de feux hors norme, la lutte ne suffit plus. Et oui, Madame la Commissaire, la pr脙漏vention doit devenir notre premier pare-feu. D脙漏velopper une v脙漏ritable culture du risque et de la pr脙漏paration n'est plus une option, c'est une n脙漏cessit脙漏 vitale.

Ensuite, vous le savez, il nous faut des bombardiers d'eau europ脙漏ens. Comme avec les masques venus de Chine, notre souverainet脙漏 ne peut plus d脙漏pendre d'avions venus du Canada. L'Europe a les savoir-faire. Il ne manque qu'une volont脙漏 politique pour b脙垄tir une production europ脙漏enne et durable.

Enfin, sans les femmes et les hommes, il n'y a pas de combat. Les sapeurs-pompiers, dont la majorit脙漏 sont volontaires, sont notre premier bouclier face aux flammes. Alors que tant de citoyens veulent s'engager, notre devoir est simple: il faut lever les obstacles 脙聽 leur mobilisation et reconna脙庐tre leur r脙麓le.

Les feux de for脙陋t ont chang脙漏 de nature, 脙聽 nous de changer notre r脙漏ponse.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Terry Reintke, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear colleagues, wildfires in Europe have reached a new terrible record this summer. More than one million hectares went up in flames in the European Union this year. One million hectares. Our thoughts are, of course, first and foremost with the people in Portugal, in Spain, in France, in Greece, Bulgaria and actually so many other countries across Europe, and a very special thanks goes out to all the firefighters who have risked their lives across Europe to help their neighbours, also from my home region in North Rhine-Westphalia. That is real European solidarity in action, and we must further step up exactly this solidarity in the future. But we must also finally tackle the problem at its root.

Last year, at this time, people's homes were drowning in massive floods. Now, they are burning in flames. Climate change is here. To mitigate its destructive consequences, we finally need an ambitious climate adaptation law. And what doesn't help in this crisis is an EPP that is trying to kill the forest-monitoring law while forests are burning all across Europe 芒聙聯 because nature is a powerful ally in the fight against these wildfires. We need to protect and restore it. Wetlands, healthy forests and soils will help us to reduce the risks in the future. And yes, colleagues, let's also talk about money, because we all know that disaster prevention costs much less than dealing with the consequences, and prevention means nature-based solutions and nature restoration.

But dear Commissioner, how is this reflected in the next European budget without a LIFE programme, for example? We need a dedicated and meaningful budget for this. And finally, and most importantly, after 2024, the summer of 2025 was again the hottest summer ever recorded. The time for inaction is over. What we don't need is an EPP gambling away our future. What we don't need is Paris killing the Paris Agreement. We need to cut emissions, colleagues. We need a strong EU climate law. Let's act now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Peter Liese (PPE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Zun脙陇chst einmal m脙露chte ich脗聽芒聙聯 auch im Namen der gesamten EVP-Fraktion脗聽芒聙聯 den Opfern in allen L脙陇ndern Mitgef脙录hl und Solidarit脙陇t ausdr脙录cken. Als Zweites ein gro脙聼es Dankesch脙露n an die Helferinnen und Helfer, auch und gerade die, die 脙录ber rescEU au脙聼erhalb ihres Heimatlandes geholfen haben. Als Drittes m脙露chte ich die Mitgliedstaaten auffordern, das Thema rescEU noch ernster zu nehmen, und zwar als Geber und als Empf脙陇nger.

Mein eigenes Mitgliedsland war immer kritisch, und ich bin sehr dankbar, dass im letzten Jahr, als in Sachsen-Anhalt ein Waldbrand war, Minister Sven Schulze Hilfe aus der EU angefordert hat und dass die auch kam, obwohl Deutschland lange skeptisch war. Ich glaube, auch in Spanien h脙陇tte die Hilfe schneller angefordert werden k脙露nnen in diesem Sommer.

Als Letztes: Klimawandel. Nat脙录rlich m脙录ssen wir 脙录ber den Klimawandel reden, aber das darf nicht unser erster Satz sein, wenn wir konkrete Opfer sehen. Ich kann auch nicht als Arzt zu einem Patienten mit Herzinfarkt kommen und sagen: Vor allen Dingen musst du deine Ern脙陇hrung umstellen. Konkrete Hilfe steht an allererster Stelle脗聽芒聙聯 das muss auch politisch klar sein.

Wenn wir 脙录ber Klimaschutz reden, dann sollten wir sehr viel st脙陇rker als bisher 脙录ber die internationale Dimension reden.脗聽Ich habe manchmal den Eindruck, als ob die Frage 芒聙聻Klimaziel f脙录r 2040: 90脗聽Prozent芒聙聹 das globale Klima alleine rettet. Wir reden viel zu wenig 脙录ber die Vorbereitung von Bel脙漏m, und, Frau Ratspr脙陇sidentin, wir reden zu wenig 脙录ber das Ziel f脙录r 2035. Das, was da diskutiert wird mit maximal 72,5脗聽Prozent, das ist mir zu wenig; darauf sollten wir uns st脙陇rker konzentrieren. Also erstens, globaler Klimaschutz, und zweitens, sehr schnelle, konkrete Hilfe.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Temido (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, caros colegas, os inc脙陋ndios rurais sempre estiveram presentes num pa脙颅s com floresta mediterr脙垄nica, como o meu, Portugal, mas as altera脙搂脙碌es clim脙隆ticas est脙拢o a acontecer cada vez mais depressa, com repeti脙搂脙拢o de fen脙鲁menos meteorol脙鲁gicos extremos e num quadro nacional do mais elevado n脙潞mero de igni脙搂脙碌es de entre todos os Estados-Membros.

Depois de 2021, pass脙隆mos a investir mais em preven脙搂脙拢o do que em combate e verific脙隆mos diminui脙搂脙拢o da 脙隆rea ardida at脙漏 2025.脗聽Este ano, Portugal voltou a viver o pesadelo dos inc脙陋ndios rurais. Mais de 274脗聽000 hectares de 脙隆rea ardida, no pior ano desde que, em 2017, n脙拢o s脙鲁 arderam 540脗聽000 hectares, como perderam a vida 116脗聽pessoas. Sabugal, Covilh脙拢, Trancoso, Ponte da Barca, o Norte e o Centro de Portugal foram mais uma vez devastados pelo fogo este ano.

O apoio da Uni脙拢o Europeia foi fundamental no combate 芒聙聰 e n脙拢o foi s脙鲁 o meu pa脙颅s que precisou da interven脙搂脙拢o dos meios do mecanismo europeu. Mas, por muito eficiente que seja um pa脙颅s ou a Uni脙拢o Europeia na resposta a inc脙陋ndios rurais, a outras amea脙搂as florestais transfronteiri脙搂as, a melhor chave est脙隆 na preven脙搂脙拢o.脗聽Para tal, 脙漏 preciso conhecer os territ脙鲁rios, as florestas, a sua cartografia, as suas caracter脙颅sticas.

N脙鲁s estamos a poucos dias da vota脙搂脙拢o nas Comiss脙碌es ENVI e AGRI da proposta de regulamento europeu que cria um quadro de monitoriza脙搂脙拢o das florestas, e aquilo que foi uma proposta ambientalmente progressista da Comiss脙拢o, baseada no conhecimento, corre o risco de ser esvaziada pelo Parlamento Europeu depois de o ter sido pelo Conselho.

A partilha de dados florestais para a gest脙拢o nacional 脙漏 essencial para proteger as florestas, para proteger as pessoas. Sem elas, as nossas l脙隆grimas ser脙拢o apenas l脙隆grimas de crocodilo sobre muito sofrimento.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jorge Buxad脙漏 Villalba (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, cientos de miles de hect脙隆reas de bosque, tierras de cultivo, pueblos enteros han ardido en Espa脙卤a. Decenas de miles de vidas afectadas o destruidas.

En Espa脙卤a, el Estado auton脙鲁mico ha provocado que hubiera equipos de bomberos dispuestos a ayudar, esperando que los pol脙颅ticos que gobiernan se pusieran de acuerdo con las competencias. Otra vez, los m脙隆s humildes han sufrido las consecuencias. A ese hombre de campo, los pol脙颅ticos que gobiernan 芒聙聰con expresi脙鲁n de psic脙鲁pata芒聙聰 le dicen que la culpa es del cambio clim脙隆tico, pero todo el mundo sabe que la culpa es de los pir脙鲁manos, los que encienden la mecha y los que impulsan leyes criminales. Mientras se baten r脙漏cords de recaudaci脙鲁n de impuestos, los servicios p脙潞blicos est脙隆n degradados y los espa脙卤oles abandonados en las cat脙隆strofes.

Socialistas y populares se echan la competencia, el reglamento y los presupuestos a la cabeza, intentando distraer a la gente, pero ni uno solo ha asumido su responsabilidad, ni los de aqu脙颅 ni los de aqu脙颅. En oto脙卤o, las riadas; en verano, los incendios. La Espa脙卤a que arde es la Espa脙卤a rural, abandonada, criminalizada durante d脙漏cadas por el bipartidismo y el fanatismo ideol脙鲁gico.

Soluciones: pues que los pol脙颅ticos que gobiernan pidan perd脙鲁n y se vayan a casa. Como no lo hacen, hemos tenido que acudir a los tribunales para que rindan cuentas de su negligencia grave y culpable. Recuperar las actividades tradicionales, el pastoreo de ganader脙颅a extensiva, los cortafuegos, la limpieza de montes, el desbroce, la recogida de le脙卤a y que la pol脙颅tica forestal no se haga por ecologistas de sal脙鲁n, que cuando llega el fuego no est脙隆n ni se les espera.

Y, por 脙潞ltimo, paguen las ayudas: en La Palma siguen esperando, en Valencia siguen esperando. A ver cu脙隆nto tardan en compensar a las v脙颅ctimas, porque para la emigraci脙鲁n masiva siempre tienen dinero.

(El orador acepta responder a una pregunta formulada con arreglo al procedimiento de la 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄)

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gr脙漏gory Allione (Renew), question 脗芦carton bleu脗禄. 芒聙聯 Cher coll脙篓gue, vous dites que ce n'est pas le changement climatique. Alors, moi, j'ai deux questions. Vous dites que ce sont des pyromanes qui allument les feux. Moi, je voudrais conna脙庐tre la part, justement, des feux li脙漏s aux pyromanes脗聽芒聙聯 premi脙篓re question.

Et, deuxi脙篓mement, comment expliquez-vous qu'en 20脗聽ans la vitesse de propagation des feux ait doubl脙漏, voire tripl脙漏?

脗聽脗聽Jorge Buxad脙漏 Villalba (PfE), respuesta de 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Pues mire, le voy a dar dos datos de Espa脙卤a: 220 incendios, 150 pir脙鲁manos detenidos o investigados ahora mismo por la polic脙颅a, teniendo en cuenta que en Galicia se detuvo a una persona que, al parecer, hab脙颅a provocado m脙隆s de diez incendios. Es decir, los porcentajes son alt脙颅simos.

Y en cuanto a la propagaci脙鲁n del fuego... porque no se limpia el bosque. Este a脙卤o ha llovido como nunca en Espa脙卤a, ha crecido toda la maleza, nadie hace labores de limpieza, se proh脙颅be a los ganaderos el pastoreo extensivo y, adem脙隆s, se exige a todo el mundo 芒聙聰hasta para recoger una pi脙卤a芒聙聰 una pu脙卤etera licencia administrativa.

脗聽脗聽Oihane Agirregoitia Mart脙颅nez (Renew). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, vemos que se repiten, en este Parlamento, debates y ataques que tuvimos cuando sucedi脙鲁 la dana. Se usan de nuevo desastres naturales y dramas para mentir sobre el cambio clim脙隆tico, para atacar al de enfrente o para enredar en 脙隆mbitos competenciales.

脗驴Saben ustedes que mientras suben las temperaturas, los montes se queman y algunos discuten, la extrema derecha sube?

Cada cual, desde su 脙隆mbito competencial: prevenci脙鲁n todo el a脙卤o, elecci脙鲁n de especies con criterio de riesgo, profesionales estables, restauraciones inteligentes tras los fuegos, protocolos 脙隆giles, no al negacionismo clim脙隆tico...

脗芦Este calor no es normal脗禄 es una de las frases que m脙隆s hemos escuchado este verano. Cuidemos, por tanto, nuestros montes, pero cuidemos tambi脙漏n nuestros servicios p脙潞blicos, que son necesarios para velar por ellos a lo largo de todo el a脙卤o, porque luego no vale echarse las manos a la cabeza.

Orgullosa de Euskadi, un pa脙颅s que tuvo la capacidad de ayudar a comunidades vecinas que lo estaban pasando mal.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Miranda Paz (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, como eurodeputada galega, promovi este debate porque tenho a responsabilidade de denunciar a solid脙拢o que sofreu Galiza, o meu pa脙颅s, nestes terr脙颅veis inc脙陋ndios de lume devastador. Casas queimadas, animais mortos, 脙隆rvores e terras agr脙隆rias, colheitas perdidas convertidas em cinzas, vidas destru脙颅das, imagens apocal脙颅pticas e paisagens devastadas.

O povo galego ficou s脙鲁. Unicamente gra脙搂as ao trabalho inesgot脙隆vel de bombeiros, vizinhan脙搂a e gente solid脙隆ria se apagaram os inc脙陋ndios. Fica o meu reconhecimento dentro do Parlamento Europeu.

Olhem os dados impactantes: arderam 145脗聽000 hectares, segundo o Copernicus, Comiss脙隆ria, como a cidade de Londres. Ourense tem 10脗聽% da superf脙颅cie queimada.

Comiss脙隆ria, o povo galego conheceu a magnitude real dos inc脙陋ndios pelo Copernicus, porque o governo galego ocultou dados nos meios p脙潞blicos. O meu pa脙颅s ardeu pela incompet脙陋ncia na preven脙搂脙拢o contra os fogos, por gastar mal os fundos europeus que, em vez de estarem destinados 脙聽 pol脙颅tica preventiva, n脙拢o s脙拢o gastos.

Toda a Europa deve saber que h脙隆 um respons脙隆vel: Alfonso Rueda Valenzuela, presidente do Governo galego. Um governo do Partido Popular que mostra, uma vez mais, a sua incompet脙陋ncia. Ocultaram a realidade e mentiram, faltou transpar脙陋ncia. O governo do PP minimizou a trag脙漏dia do meu pa脙颅s ardendo, dizendo que s脙鲁 arderam pedras e silvas.

Com os inc脙陋ndios, perdeu-se o modo de vida rural, o sustento das gentes, as explora脙搂脙碌es agropecu脙隆rias e a biodiversidade. Precisamos de medidas urgentes, como empregar o instrumento RESTORE, que poderia financiar as infraestruturas afetadas. Como queremos no Bloco passar da cinza 脙聽 vida com um plano de futuro para o rural?

脗聽脗聽Catarina Martins (The Left). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, senhora Comiss脙隆ria, em 2025, Portugal foi o Estado-Membro com mais 脙隆rea ardida em toda a Uni脙拢o Europeia. Centenas de bombeiros arriscaram a vida, e houve popula脙搂脙碌es que ficaram sozinhas a combater o fogo para salvar as suas aldeias.脗聽O Governo portugu脙陋s falhou, desvalorizou, n脙拢o preparou, pediu ajuda internacional tarde demais, n脙拢o foi capaz de uma resposta coordenada. Deixou as popula脙搂脙碌es, os bombeiros, GNR, autarcas, entregues 脙聽 sua sorte.

E agradecemos a solidariedade europeia, mas acho que tamb脙漏m devemos discutir se n脙拢o podemos fazer melhor. Na verdade, a Uni脙拢o Europeia tamb脙漏m n脙拢o esteve 脙聽 altura do que se esperava: o Mecanismo Europeu de Prote脙搂脙拢o Civil demorou cinco dias a enviar os meios a脙漏reos, depois de ser ativado. Cinco longos dias.

Enquanto continuam a exportar armas para Israel e a prometer a Trump mais neg脙鲁cio para o complexo industrial militar, os Estados-Membros e as institui脙搂脙碌es europeias est脙拢o a falhar no mais b脙隆sico para garantir a seguran脙搂a da sua popula脙搂脙拢o 芒聙聰 a prote脙搂脙拢o civil 芒聙聰, como se a seguran脙搂a dos cidad脙拢os europeus fosse secund脙隆ria.

As aldeias cercadas pelo fogo, a desesperar por ajuda, s脙拢o o tr脙隆gico resultado do desprezo pelas popula脙搂脙碌es e deviam ser a primeira raz脙拢o para arrepiar caminho e dar prioridade 脙聽 coes脙拢o e 脙聽 transi脙搂脙拢o clim脙隆tica.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Miguel Pedro (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, as minhas condol脙陋ncias 脙聽s v脙颅timas e a todos os afetados pelos inc脙陋ndios, com o reconhecimento 脙聽 coragem de quem combate estas trag脙漏dias.

Senhores deputados, quando falamos de inc脙陋ndios florestais n脙拢o falamos apenas de hectares de floresta ou de estat脙颅sticas 芒聙聰 falamos de pessoas.脗聽Aldeias inteiras evacuadas, fam脙颅lias que perdem casas, agricultores que veem o trabalho de uma vida reduzido a cinzas, ecossistemas e solos f脙漏rteis que levam d脙漏cadas a recuperar.

Este ver脙拢o, a Uni脙拢o Europeia ardeu como nunca: mais de 1 milh脙拢o de hectares destru脙颅dos, dois ter脙搂os s脙鲁 em Portugal e Espanha.脗聽Todos os anos sabemos que a pen脙颅nsula Ib脙漏rica vai arder. Sabemos que os ver脙碌es s脙拢o mais longos e mais quentes. Somos a linha da frente das altera脙搂脙碌es clim脙隆ticas. Somos o terreno onde a Europa mais arde e temos tamb脙漏m de ser a linha da frente da preven脙搂脙拢o europeia.

A resposta europeia j脙隆 n脙拢o pode ser a rotina da emerg脙陋ncia eterna; precisamos de preven脙搂脙拢o e prontid脙拢o. Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, o mecanismo rescEU tem de se tornar uma for脙搂a verdadeiramente permanente de combate a inc脙陋ndios, com meios a脙漏reos e brigadas multinacionais pr脙漏-posicionadas nas regi脙碌es de maior risco, como a pen脙颅nsula Ib脙漏rica, prontas a atuar em horas e n脙拢o em dias. Ao mesmo tempo, o pr脙鲁ximo Quadro Financeiro Plurianual tem de canalizar verbas significativas para a limpeza de combust脙颅veis, reconstru脙搂脙拢o de ecossistemas resilientes e apoio ao mundo rural.

Mas n脙拢o esque脙搂amos a outra frente: a justi脙搂a. Uma parte significativa das igni脙搂脙碌es 脙漏 crime de fogo posto. Este 脙漏 um crime grave que destr脙鲁i vidas, aldeias e patrim脙鲁nio natural. A lei existe 芒聙聰 o que falta 脙漏 justi脙搂a firme, sem complac脙陋ncia.

Senhoras e senhores deputados, isto 脙漏 um imperativo moral e geracional. Ou governamos o territ脙鲁rio no inverno, ou seremos governados pelas chamas no ver脙拢o.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Giuseppe Lupo (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, in Italia nel 2025 sono andati in fumo circa 35脗聽000 ettari; anche le aree protette sono state pesantemente colpite. Tra le regioni pi脙鹿 interessate la Sardegna e la Sicilia, dove si sono verificati centinaia di incendi.

L'aumento della temperatura, conseguenza del cambiamento climatico, 脙篓 indubbiamente un grave fattore di rischio.

Per fronteggiare queste catastrofi, che sono costate la vita a troppe persone, arrecando gravi danni all'ambiente e all'economia, serve una nuova strategia europea basata sulla prevenzione, che il meccanismo di protezione civile rappresenta certamente, e comunque svolge un ruolo cruciale.

脙聢 necessario un piano che preveda, tra l'altro, l'occupazione stabile e qualificata dei lavoratori forestali e dei vigili del fuoco, con l'utilizzo di nuove tecnologie come droni e sistemi satellitari.

I lavoratori forestali e i vigili del fuoco devono poter operare in assoluta sicurezza. Li ringrazio per il loro difficile lavoro contro gli incendi.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mathilde Androu脙芦t (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, cet 脙漏t脙漏, 16脗聽000脗聽hectares sont partis en fum脙漏e dans l'Aude, en France. Des agriculteurs, des viticulteurs ont tout perdu: leurs terres, leurs r脙漏coltes, leurs outils de travail. Le fruit d'une vie r脙漏duit en cendres. Pourtant, la vigne comme le d脙漏broussaillage sont les meilleurs Canadairs, n'en d脙漏plaise 脙聽 la gauche bobo, qui pense que la nature peut s'autor脙漏guler.

脙聙 cette anarchie dans l'entretien de la nature s'est ajout脙漏e une autre, tout aussi r脙漏voltante: plus de 2脗聽500脗聽f脙陋tards venus de toute l'Europe ont organis脙漏 une rave party en plein c脜聯ur des terres calcin脙漏es, en toute ill脙漏galit脙漏. Sur ces sols fragiles qui auraient pu rena脙庐tre, ils ont tout pi脙漏tin脙漏, aggravant encore la d脙漏tresse des exploitants. Voil脙聽 la r脙漏alit脙漏: quand nos paysans pleurent, l'脙聣tat les abandonne, seuls face au feu, puis seuls face 脙聽 l'anarchie, 脙聽 cette complaisance.

Nos campagnes ne sont pas des terrains vagues pour marginaux. Elles sont le c脜聯ur battant de la nation. Elles m脙漏ritent protection, respect et reconnaissance. Car derri脙篓re chaque champ, chaque vigne, chaque ferme, il y a des familles, des traditions, une histoire qui se transmet. Et quand on pi脙漏tine nos terres, ce ne sont pas seulement des hectares qu'on d脙漏truit, ce sont des vies enti脙篓res qu'on sacrifie.

脗聽脗聽Stine Bosse (Renew). 芒聙聯 Europa st脙楼r i flammer. 10.000 kvadratkilometer skov er i sommer br脙娄ndt v脙娄k i Sydeuropa. Svarer til hele Sj脙娄lland og Fyn. Tre gange s脙楼 meget som en normal sommer og det st脙赂rste areal, siden man begyndte at m脙楼le. Og det er ikke m脙娄rkeligt, for Europa er det kontinent i verden, der varmer hurtigst op. Men imens det sker, fors脙赂ger Dansk Folkeparti og de s脙楼kaldte patrioter helt at sabotere klimaloven, som skal komme til at bremse den globale opvarmning. Tillad mig at sp脙赂rge. Tillad mig at sp脙赂rge Dansk Folkeparti og de s脙楼kaldte patrioter: Hvor mange aktuarer fra forsikringsbranchen, hvor mange brandfolk, hvor mange meteorologer, landm脙娄nd med 脙赂delagt h脙赂st og ofre for flammerne skal vi samle her i salen, f脙赂r I ser, hvad der sker? Jeres provokationer i lovgivningsarbejdet er en sk脙娄ndsel. Sidder I virkelig og s脙赂ger svar i bunden af jeres kaffekop? Eller 脙赂nsker I bare ikke at vide, hvad der sker? Ja, det vil koste at 脙娄ndre vores vaner og forebygge katastrofer, men hvis vi ikke handler, vil prisen blive vores velstand og vores velf脙娄rd. Lad os handle sammen nu.

脗聽脗聽P脙陇r Holmgren (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Herr talman! D脙楼 st脙楼r vi h脙陇r nu igen, en septembersession i Strasbourg, och diskuterar konsekvenser av extrema v脙陇derh脙陇ndelser. F脙露r ett 脙楼r sedan var det 脙露versv脙陇mningar runtomkring i Europa. Den h脙陇r sommaren extrema skogsbr脙陇nder, framf脙露r allt i Spanien och Portugal. Jag 脙陇r s脙陇ker p脙楼 att vi kommer st脙楼 h脙陇r om ett 脙楼r igen och diskutera n脙楼gon annan form av extrem v脙陇derh脙陇ndelse.

Samtidigt har vi en h脙露gerhalva h脙陇r i parlamentet som konsekvent motarbetar ambiti脙露s klimatpolitik, och kopplat till just skogsbr脙陇nderna. 脙聞nnu allvarligare: just nu motarbetar man ocks脙楼 n脙楼gon form av skogs脙露vervakningslag 芒聙聯 Forest Monitoring Law 芒聙聯 fast脙陇n vi vet att just data om hur v脙楼r skog m脙楼r, runtomkring i Europa, skulle kunna vara ett av de absolut viktigaste verktygen f脙露r att motarbeta och lindra konsekvenserna i framtiden. Det 脙陇r en fullst脙陇ndig skandal. Det 脙陇r ett svek mot de offer som vi tyv脙陇rr har haft den h脙陇r sommaren. Men det 脙陇r faktiskt ett 脙陇nnu st脙露rre svek mot framtida offer av katastrofer som vi skulle kunna undvika.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fidias Panayiotou (NI). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, my country, Cyprus, faced the biggest wildfire in the last 50 years, and the European Union did almost nothing to help us.

If you look at Cyprus on the map, you can very easily understand the main problem: we are too far away from continental Europe. This means all the help sent from the EU doesn't reach us on time. This is such a big issue.

With this wildfire, we actually received more help from third countries like Jordan, Egypt, Israel and Lebanon than from the EU countries. This is not a good look for the European Union, and us Cypriots feel abandoned by the EU.

Don't get me wrong; we are very grateful for all the help, but sadly it is not enough. We need more investment for prevention through the EU Cohesion Fund and more firefighting equipment in Cyprus through the rescEU programme.

European Union 芒聙聯 you were not in Cyprus when we needed you the most, and this needs to change. I love you all.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nikos Papandreou (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, Commissioner, I agree with much of what you said.

We have the 2022 Wildfire Prevention Plan, but we can do more. I'm not going to criticise. We have a new world. Mega fires 芒聙聯 and I went to the town of Patras 芒聙聯 double in size in 30脗聽seconds.

So the main concept is prevention 芒聙聯 and our firemen will tell you that; I'm a part of a volunteer firefighting group. And I want to propose, not a democracy shield, but a firefighting shield that would include the new脗聽technology: drones.

We have hired drones in our local neighbourhood that can see a fire from 15脗聽km away, even from a match. We are looking at little machines: AI robots that clear the forest floor and new condensers that take water and spread it around or can fill the container.

Basically, we need best practices from Europe to propose, for all countries, very practical 芒聙聯 not expensive 芒聙聯 solutions to prevent the fires. If we prevent the fires, we've won the game. The main problem is when the fire starts.

And my slogan in Greece is: 'patriotism is not a political decision, not a political ideology; we have environmental patriotism'. That should be our slogan. To protect the environment is being patriotic.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Juan Carlos Girauta Vidal (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, yo creo que en este asunto se mezclan al menos tres factores.

Por una parte, est脙隆 la negligencia de los que, desde sus puestos de gesti脙鲁n pol脙颅tica, deb脙颅an haber hecho m脙隆s de lo que han hecho. En ese sentido, mi formaci脙鲁n pol脙颅tica ha presentado las oportunas acciones judiciales penales por las responsabilidades que se pudieran derivar.

Por otro lado, tenemos una multiplicaci脙鲁n bastante inexplicable de incendiarios. Es decir, en el caso espa脙卤ol, la mayor脙颅a de los incendios han sido provocados. Se ha detenido a m脙隆s de cien personas por provocar incendios. Mientras el Gobierno, en sus comparecencias p脙潞blicas, hablaba del cambio clim脙隆tico, no se dejaba de detener a provocadores de incendios.

En tercer lugar, yo creo que hay una cierta arrogancia por parte del ecologismo urbanita que cree que tiene mejores soluciones que aquellas que, durante toda la vida, han aplicado los que desde el campo han procedido a protegerse de los fuegos creando cortafuegos, desbrozando o limpiando los montes, cosa que ahora pueden hacer con tantas dificultades burocr脙隆ticas que, de hecho, no pueden hacerlo. As脙颅 que aprendamos de ellos.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nora Junco Garc脙颅a (ECR). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, el sur de Europa ha vuelto a arder: miles de hect脙隆reas devastadas, familias evacuadas y la izquierda repitiendo el eslogan f脙隆cil de 脗芦es por el cambio clim脙隆tico脗禄.

No es negacionismo. Hemos tenido 芒聙聰por lo menos en Espa脙卤a芒聙聰 el invierno y la primavera m脙隆s lluviosos en a脙卤os. El problema no es el clima, es la incompetencia pol脙颅tica.

Con fondos europeos disponibles para prevenci脙鲁n seguimos viendo montes sin limpiar, cortafuegos inexistentes y brigadas mal organizadas. Mientras tanto, antes que asumir su responsabilidad, las Administraciones prefieren hablar de la Agenda 2030 y del cambio clim脙隆tico.

Solo un dato: en Espa脙卤a se destina un 48脗聽% menos a prevenci脙鲁n que hace siete a脙卤os. Esto pone de manifiesto una falta de visi脙鲁n estrat脙漏gica y una gesti脙鲁n ineficaz tanto del Gobierno central como de los Gobiernos auton脙鲁micos en materia forestal.

脗驴Hablamos de cambio clim脙隆tico o de cambio propagand脙颅stico? Se trata solo de destinar fondos con inteligencia: fortalecer la prevenci脙鲁n implica menos extensiones quemadas, menos costes en extinci脙鲁n y, sobre todo, menos tragedias.

Los incendios no se combaten con ideolog脙颅a, sino con gesti脙鲁n y prevenci脙鲁n. Necesitamos m脙隆s gesti脙鲁n y menos propaganda.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marjan 脜聽arec (Renew). 芒聙聯 Gospod predsednik. Spo脜隆tovani, julija smo imeli razpravo o temi po脜戮arov v naravi in danes na pragu jeseni ugotavljamo, da nam je narava spet pokazala zobe. Ne gre vedno samo za naravo, temve脛聧 se v vsaki dru脜戮bi najdejo posamezniki, ki po脜戮are tudi podtikajo.

A ne glede na izvor po脜戮arov so posledice enake. Uni脛聧eni hektarji povr脜隆in, premo脜戮enje ljudi in, kar je nepopravljivo, izgubljena 脜戮ivljenja. Vendarle je treba povedati, da evropski mehanizem dobro deluje, prav tako solidarnost med dr脜戮avami.

Po脜戮ari pa ne izbirajo med dr脜戮avami 脛聧lanicami in tistimi, ki to 脜隆e niso. Omenil bom 脛聦rno goro, ker sem tam po脜戮are spremljal od blizu, in moram povedati, da je bila pomo脛聧 EU zelo u脛聧inkovita. V prihodnje moramo tak脜隆nim dr脜戮avam pomagati pri izgradnji 脜隆e bolj u脛聧inkovitega sistema za脜隆脛聧ite in re脜隆evanja, kar pa ni povezano samo s finan脛聧nimi sredstvi, temve脛聧 vklju脛聧uje tudi na脜隆e znanje in izku脜隆nje.

Slovenija, ki ima sama dober sistem, to 脜戮e izvaja. Seveda je najbolj脜隆e zagotoviti preventivo, a ko do po脜戮arov pride, je treba imeti zagotovljen dobro delujo脛聧 sistem same dr脜戮ave, hkrati pa hitro pomo脛聧 Evropske unije na zaprosilo prizadete dr脜戮ave.

(Govornik se je odlo脛聧il odgovoriti na vpra脜隆anje, postavljeno z dvigom modrega karton脛聧ka)

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sun脛聧ana Glavak (PPE), pitanje koje je podizanjem plave kartice postavio. 芒聙聯 Kolega 脜聽arec, spomenuli ste Crnu Goru. Hvala lijepa 脜隆to ste bili tamo.

Ja bih 脜戮eljela podsjetiti da je Republika Hrvatska pomagala Crnoj Gori, da je Republika Hrvatska jedan od sjajnih primjera na razini Europske unije kako se ula脜戮e u prevenciju, kako se ula脜戮e u sustav za脜隆tite, prije svega civilne za脜隆tite. 634 milijuna eura je Republika Hrvatska ulo脜戮ila.

Dakle, ja mislim da bismo trebali poslati poruku i zemljama 脛聧lanicama jer postoji veliki nesrazmjer volje njihovih vlada na koji na脛聧in 脛聡e pomo脛聡i u sprje脛聧avanju 脜隆umskih po脜戮ara, jednako tako i poplava. Solidarnost nam je potrebna, ja脛聧anje rescEU flote, prevencija i ja脛聧anje otpornosti. Mislim da smo tu suglasni.

脗聽脗聽Marjan 脜聽arec (Renew), odgovor na vpra脜隆anje, postavljeno z dvigom modrega karton脛聧ka. 芒聙聯 Seveda se strinjam z vami. Jaz sem omenil svojo dr脜戮avo kot primer, ki pomaga dr脜戮avi pri izgradnji sistema, se pravi tudi pri formalnopravnih zadevah, in na脛聧ina organiziranja same za脜隆脛聧ite in re脜隆evanja kot sistema. Seveda se pa strinjam z vami, da je tudi Hrva脜隆ka pomagala in seveda tudi mnoge druge dr脜戮ave in zagotovo ni nikoli tako dobro, da ne bi moglo biti bolj脜隆e.

脗聽脗聽Estrella Gal脙隆n (The Left). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora comisaria, llegamos a este pleno con el coraz脙鲁n encogido, un mill脙鲁n de hect脙隆reas arrasadas por el fuego en toda Europa y en Espa脙卤a, en particular, pues ha sido el pa脙颅s m脙隆s afectado. Las tierras de Galicia, de Castilla y Le脙鲁n, de Extremadura, han vivido los peores momentos de la 脙潞ltima historia. Detr脙隆s de este desastre hay personas que lo han perdido todo y profesionales exhaustos, en condiciones precarias, jug脙隆ndose la vida para protegernos. Desde aqu脙颅 quiero dar un abrazo y todo nuestro reconocimiento a todos los bomberos y bomberas y tambi脙漏n a las familias afectadas.

Quiero aprovechar para preguntar a los diputados del Partido Popular, 脗驴hasta cu脙隆ndo van a seguir atados al negacionismo de la extrema derecha? Tienen el mayor poder territorial en nuestro pa脙颅s. Y con esto se puede proteger a la gente y, en lugar de ofrecer seguridad, est脙隆n desinvirtiendo en protecci脙鲁n, est脙隆n compitiendo con VOX en desacreditar el consenso cient脙颅fico. 脗驴Cu脙隆ntos desastres naturales m脙隆s hacen falta para que se tomen en serio el cambio clim脙隆tico?

La negligencia cuesta vidas: ya lo hemos visto en Valencia y ahora lo estamos viendo 芒聙聰lo hemos visto芒聙聰 en media Espa脙卤a. Dejen de recortar y privatizar servicios p脙潞blicos b脙隆sicos y fundamentales. Dignifiquen a los profesionales que se juegan la vida apagando incendios y, adem脙隆s, velan por nuestra seguridad, antes de que arda lo que nos queda de pa脙颅s.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脜陆eljana Zovko (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, I will just reply to the previous speaker. I'm very proud that, in 2017, the European People's Party was the leader in adopting the rescue mechanism when everybody else in this room was against it. I was fighting, together with my colleague Elisabetta Gardini, to get this budget that would be EUR 100 million at the EU level. Now Croatian firefighters are fighting so that all firefighters in the European Union are together. As an intergroup we are fighting for them.

They recently organised themselves into the European Federation of Firefighters. We have to help these people to prepare them for the future fires. So no more demagogic speeches 芒聙聯 we have to give firefighters the means, the money and the means for civil protection, for solidarity and the emergency aid reserve, for the early warning system and for the equipment.

So I'm saying this in English for everybody else to understand me: don't play with people's lives. We have to improve conditions for firefighters in the European Union. The European People's Party was very proud to have the rescue mechanism that helped, even in the Covid times, and that is helping to protect all European citizens to be saved wherever they are.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Claire Fita (S&D). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Madame la Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, en moins de 24脗聽heures, un mort, plus d'une cinquantaine de maisons d脙漏truites, 16脗聽000脗聽hectares de cultures, et notamment des vignes, ravag脙漏s, c'est une partie du lourd bilan qu'a subi le d脙漏partement de l'Aude, en Occitanie. Mais ce drame n'est pas isol脙漏. Le sud de l'Europe, et en particulier le pourtour m脙漏diterran脙漏en, de l'Espagne 脙聽 la Gr脙篓ce, fait face aux m脙陋mes d脙漏vastations, aux m脙陋mes angoisses des populations et aux m脙陋mes cons脙漏quences 脙漏conomiques.

Il est donc crucial que nos politiques europ脙漏ennes int脙篓grent absolument cette sp脙漏cificit脙漏 m脙漏diterran脙漏enne, tant en mati脙篓re de s脙漏curit脙漏 civile, pour faire face aux incendies, qu'en mati脙篓re de politique agricole. En clair, reconna脙庐tre la sp脙漏cificit脙漏 m脙漏diterran脙漏enne, c'est admettre que notre agriculture est aussi un v脙漏ritable bouclier contre les flammes. Il est temps de lui donner la place qu'elle m脙漏rite.

Quand les flammes avancent, ce sont nos choix politiques qui d脙漏terminent si la terre br脙禄le ou si elle r脙漏siste.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Georg Mayer (PfE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, gesch脙陇tzte Kollegen! Die Waldbr脙陇nde dieser Saison脗聽芒聙聯 ich glaube, die zeigen eines ganz deutlich: Katastrophenschutz ist nationale Aufgabe, und das muss er auch bleiben, denke ich. Mitgliedstaaten und die Menschen vor Ort kennen die Gegebenheiten weitaus besser und daher nat脙录rlich auch den Umgang mit der Gefahr.

Subsidiarit脙陇t wahren: Das ist einmal mehr die Devise, die wir uns auf unsere Fahnen schreiben sollten. Br脙录ssel nutzt Krisen ja ganz gerne f脙录r mehr Macht und teure 脙录berb脙录rokratisierte Programme. Milliarden verschwinden dann in dunklen Kan脙陇len, w脙陇hrend den Helfern vor Ort die Ausr脙录stung fehlt.

Solidarit脙陇t: Ja, freiwillig, ohne EU-Zwang. Klimamodelle sind unterschiedlich gro脙聼; lokale Pr脙陇ventionen verlangen verschiedene Zug脙陇nge. Mehr Mittel in die Mitgliedstaaten und nicht nach Br脙录ssel, das ist die Devise.

脗聽脗聽Anna Zalewska (ECR). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! W p脜聜on脛聟cych lasach gin脛聟 ludzie, gin脛聟 zwierz脛聶ta, niknie bior脙鲁脜录norodno脜聸脛聡, s脛聟 ogromne straty dla maj脛聟tk脙鲁w ludzkich i gospodarki. Ale r脙鲁wnie脜录 cierpi cel klimatyczny, o kt脙鲁rym Pa脜聞stwo chc脛聟 tutaj dyskutowa脛聡, to znaczy poch脜聜anianie dwutlenku w脛聶gla. I informacja dla tych, kt脙鲁rzy chc脛聟 zwi脛聶ksza脛聡 cel do 90% 芒聙聯 nie zrealizujemy celu do 2030脗聽r.

Czego potrzebuj脛聟 lasy? Lasy potrzebuj脛聟 cz脜聜owieka, gospodarki le脜聸nej, pieni脛聶dzy, monitoringu, dobrych system脙鲁w przeciwpo脜录arowych, wreszcie system脙鲁w hydrologicznych. Nie mo脜录na postawi脛聡, bez g脜聜脛聶bokiej analizy, na okre脜聸lone 脜聸rodki, wydatkowa脛聡 tych 脜聸rodk脙鲁w. Zreszt脛聟 Trybuna脜聜 Obrachunkowy upomina Komisj脛聶 Europejsk脛聟, 脜录e nie ma 脜录adnych dowod脙鲁w, 脜录e te 脜聸rodki s脛聟 w脜聜a脜聸ciwie wydatkowane. Potrzebne s脛聟 bardzo szczeg脙鲁脜聜owe analizy pa脜聞stw cz脜聜onkowskich, mi脛聶dzy innymi po to, 脜录eby pokaza脛聡, kto za te p脜聜on脛聟ce lasy odpowiada.
