13. Amending ERDF, Cohesion Fund and Just Transition Fund as regards specific measures to address strategic challenges in the context of the mid-term review (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Le Pr脙漏sident. 芒聙聯 L'ordre du jour appelle le d脙漏bat sur le rapport de Drago脜聼脗聽Benea, au nom de la commission du d脙漏veloppement r脙漏gional, sur la proposition de r脙篓glement du Parlement europ脙漏en et du Conseil modifiant les r脙篓glements (UE)脗聽2021/1058 et (UE)脗聽2021/1056 en ce qui concerne des mesures sp脙漏cifiques visant 脙聽 relever des d脙漏fis strat脙漏giques dans le cadre de l芒聙聶examen 脙聽 mi-parcours (COM(2025)0123脗聽芒聙聯 C10芒聙聭0063/2025脗聽芒聙聯 2025/0084(COD)) (A10-0129/2025).

J'attire votre attention sur le fait qu'il n'y aura qu'un tour de parole des orateurs des groupes politiques pour ce d脙漏bat. Il n'y aura donc pas d'intervention 脙聽 la demande ni de carton bleu.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Drago脜聼 Benea, Raportor. 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, domnule vicepre脠聶edinte executiv Raffaele Fitto, dragi colegi, politica de coeziune reprezint脛聝 pilonul de baz脛聝 al solidarit脛聝脠聸ii europene, fiind expresia concret脛聝 a angajamentului nostru comun de a reduce disparit脛聝脠聸ile 脠聶i de a promova dezvoltarea echilibrat脛聝 脙庐n toate regiunile Uniunii Europene.

Ast脛聝zi ne afl脛聝m 脙庐ntr-un moment important pentru politica de dezvoltare regional脛聝, iar evaluarea de la mijlocul perioadei ne ofer脛聝 o oportunitate unic脛聝, nu doar de a analiza progresul realizat 脙庐n actuala perioad脛聝 de programare, ci 脠聶i de a ajusta, unde este necesar, instrumentele 脠聶i interven脠聸iile, pentru a putea r脛聝spunde prompt 脠聶i ferm noilor provoc脛聝ri cu care se confrunt脛聝 regiunile noastre.

Discu脠聸iile din ultimele luni nu au fost 脙庐ntotdeauna u脠聶oare, 脠聶i pe bun脛聝 dreptate. Politica de coeziune atinge inima solidarit脛聝脠聸ii 脠聶i a investi脠聸iilor europene 脠聶i este firesc s脛聝 existe opinii ferme 脠聶i viziuni diverse cu privire la direc脠聸ia 脠聶i priorit脛聝脠聸ile acesteia.

脙聨n ciuda diferen脠聸elor de perspectiv脛聝, am reu脠聶it s脛聝 construim un teren comun 脙庐n care conteaz脛聝 asigurarea faptului c脛聝 politica de coeziune, politica de dezvoltare regional脛聝 continu脛聝 s脛聝 serveasc脛聝 cet脛聝脠聸enii 脠聶i regiunile noastre 脙庐n cel mai eficient 脠聶i echitabil mod posibil.

Acordul provizoriu prevede m脛聝suri semnificative pentru adaptarea instrumentelor noastre la realit脛聝脠聸ile actuale, prin introducerea a cinci priorit脛聝脠聸i noi, aliniate la noile provoc脛聝ri emergente, precum 脠聶i cre脠聶terea flexibilit脛聝脠聸ii 脠聶i a capacit脛聝脠聸ii de absorb脠聸ie a fondurilor. Aceast脛聝 evaluare intermediar脛聝 reflect脛聝 at脙垄t continuitatea, c脙垄t 脠聶i evolu脠聸ia politicii de coeziune. P脛聝strarea valorilor sale fundamentale 脙庐n paralel cu o ajustare a implement脛聝rii pentru a asigura un impact mai mare.

Permite脠聸i-mi s脛聝 rezum c脙垄teva dintre acordurile importante la care am ajuns 脙庐mpreun脛聝 cu pre脠聶edin脠聸ia danez脛聝, fiind sprijini脠聸i de Comisia European脛聝 脙庐n aceste negocieri.

Parlamentul a acceptat ap脛聝rarea ca fiind o nou脛聝 prioritate. Cu toate acestea, 脙庐n ceea ce prive脠聶te investi脠聸iile 脙庐n capacit脛聝脠聸i industriale sau 脙庐n infrastructuri trebuie acordat脛聝 脙庐ntotdeauna prioritate celor cu dubl脛聝 utilizare. 脙聨n viitor, vom solicita Comisiei s脛聝 verifice dac脛聝 aceast脛聝 prevedere a fost respectat脛聝 de c脛聝tre statele membre.

De asemenea, am reu脠聶it s脛聝 introducem preg脛聝tirea civil脛聝 脙庐n toate tipurile de teritorii, aspect deosebit de important pentru Parlamentul European.

Un alt element esen脠聸ial convenit este gestionarea integrat脛聝 a apei 脠聶i rezilien脠聸a sa, pe care am reu脠聶it s脛聝 le aliniem la Strategia european脛聝 pentru rezilien脠聸a apei.

Am reu脠聶it s脛聝 facem referire la locuin脠聸ele durabile, al脛聝turi de locuin脠聸ele la pre脠聸uri accesibile 脙庐n 脙庐ntregul text al regulamentului. 脙聨n aceast脛聝 dup脛聝-amiaz脛聝 lans脛聝m un mesaj clar miilor de familii europene care se confrunt脛聝 cu dificult脛聝脠聸i 脙庐n g脛聝sirea unei locuin脠聸e la pre脠聸uri accesibile, 脠聶i anume c脛聝 am extins domeniul de aplicare a acestei prevederi at脙垄t pentru familiile social vulnerabile, c脙垄t 脠聶i pentru cele din clasa de mijloc.

A脠聶 dori s脛聝 transmit o veste bun脛聝 regiunilor situate la frontiera estic脛聝 a Uniunii Europene, 脠聶i anume c脛聝 acestea vor beneficia de o prefinan脠聸are suplimentar脛聝 unic脛聝, 脙庐n valoare de 9,5脗聽% din totalul sprijinului acordat prin programele ESF+ 脠聶i FEDER.

脙聨n cele din urm脛聝, permite脠聸i-mi s脛聝 men脠聸ionez faptul c脛聝 am limitat sprijinul acordat 脙庐ntreprinderilor mari la cele din 脠聸脛聝rile beneficiare ale Fondului de Coeziune, men脠聸in脙垄nd 脙庐n acela脠聶i timp un accent principal pe 脙庐ntreprinderile mici 脠聶i mijlocii.

V脛聝 propun s脛聝 reafirm脛聝m, prin intermediul votului de m脙垄ine, angajamentul fa脠聸脛聝 de o politic脛聝 de coeziune care s脛聝 func脠聸ioneze pentru to脠聸i, pentru to脠聸i cet脛聝脠聸enii din zonele urbane 脠聶i rurale, din regiunile mai pu脠聸in dezvoltate 脠聶i cele mai dezvoltate, pentru tineri 脠聶i v脙垄rstnici, pentru comunit脛聝脠聸ile 脙庐n tranzi脠聸ie 脠聶i pentru cele care se confrunt脛聝 cu provoc脛聝rile venite 脙庐n urma r脛聝zboiului din Ucraina, astfel 脙庐nc脙垄t nimeni s脛聝 nu fie l脛聝sat 脙庐n urm脛聝 脠聶i fiecare regiune s脛聝 aib脛聝 脠聶ansa s脛聝 se dezvolte 脙庐n mod durabil 脠聶i echitabil. A脠聶tept cu interes interven脠聸iile dumneavoastr脛聝.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Raffaele Fitto, Executive Vice-President of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, thank you for the opportunity to address today's debate. Let me first thank the rapporteur, Mr Benea, the shadow rapporteurs and the Council for your work, for your spirit of cooperation and for reaching this important agreement.

The EU has faced significant challenges in recent years. Some are being addressed. Many are still ongoing, while new ones continue to emerge. In a context of uncertainty and rapid change, cohesion policy is one of our best instruments and resources to face these challenges and to enable Europe to act and react, to make Europe able to continue its mission of reducing disparities, promoting balanced territorial development, ensuring that every European, wherever they choose to live, shares in our success and prosperity, and to do it now.

The compromise package that you will vote tomorrow is a unique opportunity to modernise cohesion policy, to respond effectively to the challenges of competitiveness, defence, housing, water resilience and the energy transition, to enable regions to invest even more in these joint EU priorities areas and to support citizens with effective action.

I am glad to say that the mid-term review reflects the efforts of all of us. It is the result of our exchanges, the discussions in the REGI Committee, the debates in this chamber, the meetings with the rapporteurs, the meetings with the regions and the territories. Many times, we have agreed on the need for greater flexibility. The areas of intervention and the priorities for allocating resources are also widely shared. The mid-term review prioritises widening investment in affordable and sustainable housing. The package also reflects Parliament's support for a comprehensive approach to civilian preparedness, as well as the new priority areas, such as promoting energy flows across Europe.

To boost Europe's competitive edge and to close the innovation gap, the legislation includes measures to increase investment in strategic technologies, in particular those contributing to the Strategic Technologies for Europe Platform. We also focus our support for the decarbonisation of production capacities, the development of resilient defence infrastructure, prioritising TEN-T of a dual use in nature, including to foster military mobility in the Union and for eastern border regions. The package includes many other useful flexibilities as well as additional incentives, including higher pre- and co-financing rates. The compromise also strengthens the respect for the rule of law. As reiterated in our statement, the Commission remains committed to ensuring the rule of law is haphazard in the implementation of cohesion funds.

Dear Members, the mid-term review is very important and highly needed by the territories, which is why the Commission has already received many requests. A positive outcome will mean greater opportunities for the territories, as well as certainty in timing and implementation. Through these targeted amendments of the mid-term review, the cohesion policy will be more agile and responsive, aligned both with EU values and today's realities.

Many thanks again for your work and for this agreement. I look forward to our discussion.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Riho Terras, rapporteur for the opinion of the SEDE Committee. 芒聙聯 Mr President, Mr Vice-President, dear colleagues, our priorities have changed since the breakout of full-scale war in Europe, especially when it comes to the increase of defence spendings.

This is why we create the possibility of the Member States to use the money from the cohesion funds, the European Regional Development Fund and the Just Transition Fund for defence purposes. To be clear, we are not taking money away from cohesion, we are simply creating the possibilities for the Member States to strengthen their defence.

As the rapporteur for the SEDE opinion for this report, I urge you to confirm the trilogue outcome tomorrow. This is urgent, since Members who wish to reprogramme some of their funds need to do so before the end of this year.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Danu脜隆e Nerudov脙隆, rapporteur for the opinion of the BUDG Committee. 芒聙聯 Mr President, Mr Commissioner, dear colleagues, the major priority of the European Union must be security for our citizens. Without security, there is no growth, no social justice.脗聽All EU policies, including cohesion, must contribute to this goal.

Today we have achieved a good result: a tax that enables stronger investment in defence. I welcome the changes to national allocation rules, and I am glad that the rule of law has been safeguarded. But this question is not settled: some governments will try to lower our ambition.

Let me be clear: Parliament will remain a watchdog of the rule of law, especially in the next long-term budget.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Krzysztof Hetman, w imieniu grupy PPE. 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Panie Komisarzu! Mam takie g脜聜脛聶bokie przekonanie i poczucie, 脜录e po raz pierwszy od bardzo d脜聜ugiego czasu tutaj, w Unii Europejskiej, w instytucjach europejskich, odpowiedzieli脜聸my w prawdziwy spos脙鲁b na wyzwania i oczekiwania, i potrzeby, jakie s脜聜yszymy od poszczeg脙鲁lnych spo脜聜eczno脜聸ci, od obywateli i grup zawodowych na terenie naszych pa脜聞stw cz脜聜onkowskich.

Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo, przy okazji tego przegl脛聟du 脜聸r脙鲁dokresowego polityki sp脙鲁jno脜聸ci podj脛聶li脜聸my tu wsp脙鲁lnie bardzo dobre decyzje dotycz脛聟ce kwestii przesuni脛聶cia, przekazania tych pieni脛聶dzy na obronno脜聸脛聡, szczeg脙鲁lnie je脜聸li chodzi o produkty podw脙鲁jnego zastosowania. To s脛聟 bardzo wa脜录ne 脜聸rodki dla naszych przedsi脛聶biorstw, kt脙鲁re b脛聶d脛聟 podnosi脛聡 swoj脛聟 konkurencyjno脜聸脛聡, a pieni脛聟dze przekazane na obronno脜聸脛聡 mog脛聟 by脛聡 nowym ko脜聜em zamachowym dla europejskiej gospodarki.

Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo, drugim elementem, niezwykle istotnym, na kt脙鲁ry chcieliby脜聸my priorytetowo zwr脙鲁ci脛聡 uwag脛聶, jest kwestia transformacji energetycznej, czystej i taniej energii, bezpiecznej, dost脛聶pnej po cenach takich, kt脙鲁re b脛聶d脛聟 akceptowane przez przedsi脛聶biorc脙鲁w oraz gospodarstwa domowe na odpowiednim poziomie.

Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo, kolejna sprawa to dostrze脜录enie kwestii zwi脛聟zanej z regionami wschodnimi granicz脛聟cymi z Rosj脛聟, Bia脜聜orusi脛聟 i Ukrain脛聟, bo one wszystkie borykaj脛聟 si脛聶 z ogromnymi wyzwaniami, zagro脜录eniami oraz z niepewno脜聸ci脛聟. Chcia脜聜em za to bardzo serdecznie podzi脛聶kowa脛聡.

Kolejna kwestia to odporno脜聸脛聡 wodna i zr脙鲁wnowa脜录ona gospodarka wodna. Kolejny priorytet, niezwykle istotny, bior脛聟c pod uwag脛聶 wszystkie zdarzenia, kt脙鲁re mamy na terenie Unii Europejskiej, je脜聸li chodzi o powodzie i susze.

Wreszcie kwestia zwi脛聟zana z elastyczno脜聸ci脛聟 i uproszczeniami w ramach polityki sp脙鲁jno脜聸ci. Musimy jak najszybciej to prawo wprowadzi脛聡 w 脜录ycie. Jeszcze teraz musimy to przeg脜聜osowa脛聡 na sesji plenarnej, tak aby te wszystkie bardzo dobre zmiany wesz脜聜y w 脜录ycie od 1 stycznia 2026 roku.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marcos Ros Sempere, en nombre del Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n es la bandera social de Europa. Es la pol脙颅tica que crea empleo, que genera inversiones all脙颅 donde se necesitan. Es la pol脙颅tica que convierte la desafecci脙鲁n en europe脙颅smo, mejorando infraestructuras, construyendo centros de salud o escuelas. Es, adem脙隆s, un instrumento de solidaridad dentro de la Uni脙鲁n y ha dado respuesta a los desaf脙颅os que hemos afrontado en los 脙潞ltimos a脙卤os, que no han sido pocos.

Vivimos en un contexto lleno de retos y la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n ha respondido. Por eso, abrimos en esta revisi脙鲁n la puerta a que los Estados que lo necesiten reprogramen necesidades. Flexibilidad, s脙颅, pero no a costa de extender un cheque en blanco a los Estados ni de olvidarnos de las pol脙颅ticas comunes, como vemos en la propuesta del pr脙鲁ximo marco financiero. Porque ese camino desmonta la idea de una Europa que act脙潞a integrada y cohesionada y nos devuelve a ser una mera suma de pa脙颅ses con enfoques muy dispares.

Europa necesita garantizar su propia seguridad y defensa y, por eso, nosotros, desde el Grupo S&D, hemos trabajado para que cada euro de cohesi脙鲁n y defensa tenga un uso dual para los ciudadanos. Tambi脙漏n hemos reforzado la estrategia de resiliencia h脙颅drica, incorporando la desalinizaci脙鲁n para garantizar agua para regiones como la m脙颅a 芒聙聰la Regi脙鲁n de Murcia芒聙聰 que sufren sequ脙颅as cada vez m脙隆s duras e impredecibles a causa del cambio clim脙隆tico. Y hemos reforzado tambi脙漏n la necesidad de apoyar vivienda asequible para evitar los estragos de un mercado descontrolado. Y hemos a脙卤adido salvaguardas al Estado de Derecho, para que ni un euro de la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n vaya a quien no respete las reglas comunes.

Se脙卤or comisario, este Grupo va a dar la batalla por una pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n con financiaci脙鲁n suficiente, reglas claras y garant脙颅as. No podemos aceptar que se vuelvan a imponer recortes, la austeridad y los hombres de negro, porque, para los socialistas, la cohesi脙鲁n es un principio irrenunciable de nuestra idea de Europa.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽S脙漏verine Werbrouck, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, en fait, quel g脙垄chis! Cela ne commen脙搂ait pourtant pas si mal. 脙聙 notre grand 脙漏tonnement, vous 脙漏tiez enfin pr脙陋ts 脙聽 assouplir les r脙篓glements d'application des fonds de transition juste et de d脙漏veloppement r脙漏gional, notamment en ce qui concerne la d脙漏fense, l'脙漏nergie et le logement: l'augmentation du plafond de cofinancement par l'Union europ脙漏enne, la prolongation des proc脙漏dures de d脙漏penses, l'int脙漏gration des grandes entreprises de la d脙漏fense et surtout plus de libert脙漏 laiss脙漏e aux 脙聣tats pour la r脙漏affectation des fonds.

Mais nous voil脙聽 rassur脙漏s 脙聽 pr脙漏sent, comme 脙聽 votre habitude, votre id脙漏ologie vous a rattrap脙漏s: aucune mesure pour garantir que les fonds b脙漏n脙漏ficient aux seules entreprises europ脙漏ennes et n'aillent pas enrichir l'industrie militaire am脙漏ricaine, comme 脙聽 chaque fois. De plus, vous conditionnez l'acc脙篓s aux fonds sur la base arbitraire du pr脙漏tendu respect de l'脙漏tat de droit, argument moral dont vous vous servez en r脙漏alit脙漏 pour restreindre les politiques men脙漏es par les gouvernements d'opposition.

C'est pourquoi nous ne pouvons na脙炉vement cautionner ni la gabegie europ脙漏enne par le d脙漏caissement et la prolongation des fonds inutilis脙漏s, pay脙漏s en grande partie par la France, ni l'esprit de censure qui pr脙漏side 脙聽 ce texte脗聽par l'alignement du calendrier budg脙漏taire sur votre agenda f脙漏d脙漏raliste.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Denis Nesci, a nome del gruppo ECR. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, signor Vicepresidente esecutivo, onorevoli colleghi, oggi pi脙鹿 che mai la politica di coesione deve dimostrarsi un vero motore di sviluppo per i nostri territori.

Di fronte alle sfide sempre pi脙鹿 complesse che l'Europa si trova ad affrontare, 脙篓 fondamentale garantire maggiore flessibilit脙聽 e strumenti concreti agli enti locali, affinch脙漏 possano rispondere in modo efficace e tempestivo alle esigenze reali delle comunit脙聽.

Con la nuova revisione, ogni paese avr脙聽 la possibilit脙聽 芒聙聯 su base volontaria 芒聙聯 di adattare i propri programmi di coesione in base alle priorit脙聽 specifiche dei propri territori, per conseguire gli obiettivi stabiliti dall'articolo 174 del TFUE.

Questo significa riconoscere che non esiste un'unica soluzione, ma che 脙篓 necessario valorizzare le diversit脙聽 e le peculiarit脙聽 di ogni realt脙聽 locale. Un risultato che 脙篓 stato reso possibile anche grazie al lavoro prezioso del Vicepresidente Fitto, che ha saputo portare avanti con determinazione un dialogo costruttivo tra Istituzioni europee e Stati membri.

Tra le priorit脙聽, ritengo fondamentale sottolineare l'attenzione alla competitivit脙聽 e all'innovazione per sostenere il tessuto produttivo e occupazionale; il contrasto alla crisi abitativa, un problema che oggi colpisce sempre pi脙鹿 famiglie in molte regioni europee; e la gestione sostenibile delle risorse idriche, con un focus particolare sulla lotta alla siccit脙聽, emergenza che non possiamo pi脙鹿 ignorare.

脙聢 fondamentale sottolineare come gli Stati membri sollecitano una rapida adozione del regolamento per consentire agli enti locali di avviare immediatamente i progetti.

Si tratta di un segnale particolarmente rilevante per le aree rurali, che pi脙鹿 di altre scontano divari storici e che devono affrontare problemi che solo con la politica di coesione si possono superare, perch脙漏 la politica di coesione 脙篓 uno strumento di equit脙聽 e crescita, capace di garantire uno sviluppo solido e duraturo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脛陆ubica Karva脜隆ov脙隆, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, throughout negotiations, Renew had worked hard to improve the Commission's proposal to better serve Europe's regions and its citizens. Cohesion funds now do not cover only defence, but also other key investment pillars, from electricity grids and civil preparedness 芒聙聯 which has been mentioned 芒聙聯 to affordable but also sustainable housing.

We defended poorer regions over rich ones. We focussed on SMEs. We extended the Just Transition Fund, so important also for our Irish friends and, most importantly, we advanced the respect for the rule of law.

In relation to frozen funds, we know the outcome of the negotiations is a compromise between three institutions. Of course, we would have wished to go further, as reflected in our plenary amendment. But for Renew, the mid-term review is only the start of the discussions on the future of the MFF, where we really want to see much stronger safeguards for the for the rule of law and the role of the rule of law in EU funds. Let me now switch to Slovak as well.

Mid-term review priniesol aj rizik脙隆. Slovensk脙隆 vl脙隆da zmrazila 200 mili脙鲁nov eur samospr脙隆vam, mest脙隆m, obciam. Na 脜隆k脙麓lky, 脜隆koly aj zdravotn脙颅cke centr脙隆. Nie preto, 脜戮e by neboli pripraven脙漏 projekty, ale preto, 脜戮e vl脙隆da m脙隆 in脙漏 priority pre 脛戮ud脙颅 v regi脙鲁noch. 脛陆udia to nie s脙潞.

Ja budem v脜戮dy st脙隆脜楼 na strane regi脙鲁nov, tu v 脜聽trasburgu, v Bruseli aj na Slovensku. Ako tie脜聢ov脙隆 spravodajky脜聢a som preto presadila aj poistku, aby vl脙隆dy nemohli len tak svojvo脛戮ne rozhodova脜楼 o zdrojoch, ktor脙漏 patria samospr脙隆vam, bez konzult脙隆cie miest a obc脙颅, a som rada, 脜戮e je s脙潞脛聧as脜楼ou tohto n脙隆vrhu. Eur脙鲁pska 脙潞nia nie je zl脙陆 Brusel. Je to podan脙隆 ruka.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Cristina Guarda, a nome del gruppo Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, 脙篓 vero, la politica di coesione ha un problema di utilizzo dei fondi, ma invece di correggere gli errori fatti qui, all'interno di questa istituzione, la burocrazia l'ha trasformata in un bancomat per grandi imprese e per fare mega infrastrutture.

E con la scusa della difesa da domani potrete dirottare miliardi destinati alle regioni meno sviluppate, o avvelenate, per la costruzione, per esempio, di un ponte sullo Stretto: ottimo investimento, che classificate pure come climatico.

Oppure, dare una valanga di soldi pubblici a poche grandi imprese della difesa, che 脙篓 evidentemente un metodo pi脙鹿 semplice di utilizzare questi fondi immediatamente, piuttosto che darli a tante piccole e medie imprese locali, no?

Ecco il tutto, pensate un po', con meno trasparenza. Perch脙漏 sia mai che sappiamo dove e come utilizzano realmente i nostri fondi e questo, mi dispiace, non 脙篓 rispettare il fondamento della politica di coesione.

Cosa rester脙聽 per difenderci della crisi climatica, per difendere le nostre politiche sociali, le piccole e medie imprese, la sanit脙聽, i servizi e le comunit脙聽 rurali?

Ecco, ma tanto Orb脙隆n vi ringrazia, visto che con questo voto potr脙聽 continuare a usare i fondi pur violando, ancora, i diritti fondamentali. Un aspetto su cui noi Verdi continueremo a combattere.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Valentina Palmisano, a nome del gruppo The Left. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, la proposta di regolamento che oggi esaminiamo segna, a nostro avviso, una deviazione grave dalla missione originaria della politica di coesione.

Una politica, lo ricordiamo, nata per ridurre i divari territoriali e sostenere comunit脙聽 e cittadini in difficolt脙聽 rischia ora di trasformarsi in un bancomat per spese militari e per l'industria della difesa, perch脙漏 questo 脙篓 quello che avverr脙聽 inserendo tra le nuove priorit脙聽 di investimento proprio la difesa.

E noi non possiamo accettare che i fondi destinati a scuole, ospedali, trasporti sostenibili, lavoro, sostegno alle piccole e medie imprese vengano dirottate sugli armamenti. Innanzitutto, questo sarebbe un tradimento dei valori storici su cui 脙篓 fondata l'Unione europea, e poi sarebbe catastrofico per le nostre regioni, le regioni pi脙鹿 fragili che noi oggi, qui, rappresentiamo.

E lo voglio dire chiaramente: noi non saremo mai dalla parte di chi priva i nostri territori di risorse essenziali, perch脙漏 significa privare questi territori di qualcosa di essenziale, appunto, e significa condannarli a restare indietro ancora una volta.

Guardate, anche noi abbiamo a cuore la difesa e la sicurezza dei nostri cittadini, ma riteniamo che queste non si costruiscano con pi脙鹿 armi, ma con pi脙鹿 cooperazione, pi脙鹿 giustizia sociale e pi脙鹿 resilienza economica.

Per questo continueremo a lavorare affinch脙漏 l'Europa rimanga un progetto di pace e non diventi un cantiere di guerra.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Irmhild Bo脙聼dorf, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Fast 800脗聽Milliarden Euro, so Ursula von der Leyen, sollen f脙录r die Kriegst脙录chtigkeit der EU ausgegeben werden. Wer ihre Rede beim Besuch in Bulgarien letzte Woche verfolgt hat, wundert sich, mit welcher Hartn脙陇ckigkeit und Schnelligkeit die Hochr脙录stung auf den Weg gebracht werden soll.

Die bulgarische R脙录stungsindustrie sei nicht nur ein Grund f脙录r bulgarischen Nationalstolz, sondern auch f脙录r einen europ脙陇ischen Stolz. Fast 60 der angek脙录ndigten 800脗聽Milliarden sollen aus Koh脙陇sionsmitteln flie脙聼en. Statt Kinderg脙陇rten werden im l脙陇ndlichen Raum dann eben R脙录stungsfabriken gebaut; die Dorfsanierung muss dem Drohnentest weichen; Infrastruktur wird es allenfalls geben, um die noch zu bauenden Panzer in k脙录nftige Kriegsgebiete transportieren zu k脙露nnen.

Wir r脙录sten hier f脙录r einen Krieg, den nur die EU will, sonst niemand. Eine echte strategische Herausforderung w脙陇re es, die Regionen zu st脙陇rken und Europa damit wieder zu einem zukunftsf脙陇higen und lebenswerten Kontinent zu machen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Raffaele Fitto, Executive Vice-President of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, many thanks for this rich and interesting discussion, it will frame my thinking as we continue our work together in the coming months and years.

With this package, we are creating a unique opportunity. Now it is up to the Member States and regions to answer the call to make the necessary programme amendments. The European Commission is ready to support, because we must all continue our collective efforts, further adapting the cohesion policy to Europe's new challenges: modern, ready for the future and continuing its mission to leave no region behind.

I look forward to continuing our collaboration. Even during the preparation of this package, dialogue with the REGI Committee and Members of the European Parliament has been extremely valuable. We will maintain the same openness and willingness to work together on cohesion, particularly as we approach the next multiannual financial framework.

Regarding the rule of law that has been mentioned by several Members, I want to be clear: as I told you before, I sent a letter to Mr Benea that underlines that the Commission remains committed to ensuring that rule of law is upheld in the implementation of the funds, and will assess any request for programme amendments in line with the CPR, the conditionality and the Medium Term Review Regulation.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Drago脜聼 Benea, Raportor. 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, dragi colegi, mul脠聸umesc tuturor pentru interven脠聸iile dumneavoastr脛聝. Prin intermediul acordului provizoriu privind revizuirea la mijlocul perioadei a politicii de coeziune, transmitem un mesaj clar tuturor europenilor: Uniunea European脛聝 r脛聝m脙垄ne receptiv脛聝 脠聶i unit脛聝 脙庐n efortul de a combate disparit脛聝脠聸ile 脠聶i de a promova o dezvoltare echilibrat脛聝 脙庐n toate regiunile europene, iar politica de coeziune este flexibil脛聝 脠聶i capabil脛聝 s脛聝 se adapteze la noile realit脛聝脠聸i de pe teren.

Permite脠聸i-mi s脛聝 mul脠聸umesc tuturor colegilor, raportori alternativi, vicepre脠聶edintelui Comisiei, Raffaele Fitto, reprezentan脠聸ilor Comisiei Europene 脠聶i ai Consiliului, precum 脠聶i pre脠聶edin脠聸iei daneze pentru cooperarea excelent脛聝 脠聶i munca intens脛聝 depus脛聝 脙庐n regim de urgen脠聸脛聝 pe acest raport. M脙垄ine, Parlamentul European 脙庐脠聶i va exprima votul asupra acordului provizoriu, at脙垄t de mult a脠聶teptat de autorit脛聝脠聸ile de management din 脠聸脛聝rile noastre.

脙聨mi exprim speran脠聸a c脛聝 acordul provizoriu va fi sus脠聸inut de membrii Parlamentului European 脙庐n cadrul votului care se va desf脛聝脠聶ura m脙垄ine, iar ulterior adopt脛聝rii noului regulament, statele membre, regiunile 脠聶i autorit脛聝脠聸ile de management vor transforma acest acord 脙庐n proiecte 脠聶i beneficii concrete pentru comunit脛聝脠聸ile din toate regiunile Uniunii Europene. Vom continua s脛聝 monitoriz脛聝m 脠聶i s脛聝 sprijinim acest proces pentru ca beneficiile politicii de coeziune s脛聝 ajung脛聝 脙庐n fiecare col脠聸 al Europei 脠聶i s脛聝 se reflecte 脙庐n via脠聸a fiec脛聝ruia dintre cet脛聝脠聸enii europeni.

脗聽脗聽Mihai Tudose (S&D), 脙庐n scris. 芒聙聯 Am votat rezolu脠聸ia legislativ脛聝 ce aprob脛聝 revizuirea regulamentelor din domeniul politicii de coeziune, astfel 脙庐nc脙垄t aceasta s脛聝 fie aliniat脛聝 noilor priorit脛聝脠聸i strategice ale UE 脠聶i statelor membre.

Modific脛聝rile permit redirec脠聸ionarea de fonduri din bugetul pentru coeziune c脛聝tre o serie de sectoare nevralgice: ap脛聝rare, securitate, regiunile de la frontiera estic脛聝 脠聶i competitivitate. 脙聨n contextul evalu脛聝rii intermediare, permise de cadrul juridic al programelor politicii de coeziune, ne asigur脛聝m c脛聝 parte din bugetul acestora poate merge rapid spre investi脠聸iile 脙庐n capabilit脛聝脠聸i de ap脛聝rare, 脙庐n infrastructura cu dubl脛聝 utilizare - mai ales pentru a stimula mobilitatea militar脛聝 -, precum 脠聶i 脙庐n consolidarea rezilien脠聸ei comunit脛聝脠聸ilor din regiunile frontaliere estice ale Uniunii, care se 脙庐nvecineaz脛聝 cu Rusia, Belarus 脠聶i Ucraina.

Sus脠聸in prefinan脠聸area suplimentar脛聝 de 20% 脙庐n cazul sumelor realocate acestor priorit脛聝脠聸i specifice, majorarea ratelor de cofinan脠聸are din partea UE cu 10% 脠聶i sprijinul suplimentar de 9,5% pentru regiunile de la grani脠聸a estic脛聝 a Uniunii.

Am votat, de asemenea, 脙庐n favoarea m脛聝surilor pentru gestionarea durabil脛聝 a apelor, promovarea accesului la locuin脠聸e la pre脠聸uri accesibile, protec脠聸ia infrastructurii energetice critice 脠聶i instalarea infrastructurii de re脙庐nc脛聝rcare electric脛聝.

脗聽

14. Cohesion policy (joint debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Le Pr脙漏sident. 芒聙聯 L'ordre du jour appelle 脙聽 pr脙漏sent la discussion commune sur la politique de coh脙漏sion, qui porte sur trois rapports:

芒聙聯 le rapport de Ciaran脗聽Mullooly, au nom de la commission du d脙漏veloppement r脙漏gional, sur le r脙麓le de la politique de coh脙漏sion dans l'accompagnement de la transition juste (2024/2121(INI)) (A10芒聙聭0137/2025),

芒聙聯 le rapport de Marcos脗聽Ros脗聽Sempere, au nom de la commission du d脙漏veloppement r脙漏gional, sur le r脙麓le des investissements de la politique de coh脙漏sion dans la r脙漏solution de l芒聙聶actuelle crise du logement (2024/2120(INI)) (A10芒聙聭0139/2025),

芒聙聯 et le rapport de Vladimir脗聽Prebili脛聧, au nom de la commission du d脙漏veloppement r脙漏gional, sur les possibilit脙漏s de simplification des fonds de coh脙漏sion (2024/2106(INI)) (A10芒聙聭0138/2025).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ciaran Mullooly, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear Commissioner脗聽Fitto, colleagues, we all share the same ambition. We want to see a Europe that delivers for our citizens in the changing world in which we find ourselves.

However, we know that ambition alone will not really deliver results. That's why cohesion policy matters. That's why I'm proposing targeted measures to ensure it delivers for people whose lives have been upended by the transition away from carbon-intensive industry. It's about making sure we see social justice before transition. It's about ensuring that no region, no community and no worker is left behind.

Colleagues, this is the core message of my report before you today. In this report, we highlight what many regions are experiencing 芒聙聯 the green transition is not an abstract policy objective, but a daily reality for communities, families and businesses. The reality of this transition is clearly visible in my own region: in Lanesborough in County Longford, at the Bord Na M脙鲁na Mount Dillon works, hundreds of workers lost their jobs and their livelihoods when the local power station was shut down. This is very real.

The same story has been repeated from Shannonbridge in County Offaly and right across Europe to Silesia in Poland, where I visited this year, and countless regions in between. Workers who have given decades of service have found themselves facing unemployment. If we fail to address these issues adequately, we risk deepening regional inequalities, Commissioner.

That is why I am bringing forward these new policies to ensure that workers are treated fairly and properly supported in every step of the transition. No one should face redundancy without a clear plan for retraining and reskilling, because, colleagues, without their buy-in the transition will never succeed. That is why Just Transition as a fund plays an important role within cohesion. It is not about idealism, but about ensuring the plan and the change is achievable and fair.

The Commission's mid-term review earlier this year recognised this reality by expanding the scope of the fund and ensuring that, under certain conditions, just transition projects are entitled to another year to spend funds. This is good. The Regional Development Committee of this Parliament has gone further, proposing practical reforms to make just transition work even better.

So first and foremost, Commissioner, the Regional Development Committee has made a strong call for a Just Transition Fund II, with adequate resources and firmly based on shared management and partnership principles. As you will be aware, the proposal from the Commission on the future MFF did not contain any proposal for a second fund.

Colleagues, in order to bring citizens with us, in order to ensure no one is actually left behind, we must send a clear and unequivocal message today. We need a second fund for just transition. We need it. Anything less is unacceptable. We must continue the work we have started.

We have also put further forward a concrete proposal for the creation of special economic zones in the regions hardest hit by closures. Crucially, these zones will not be a quick fix. They will remain in place until such a time as the majority of jobs last locally are actually replaced in the local area. That is the key to this, Commissioner.

I have long championed education and training at the heart of transition, and therefore I am particularly pleased and happy to say that we have made bold proposals for investment in apprenticeships and training within local areas. However, crucially, this must be tailored to the needs of the local economy.

This report also advocates for multiple simplification methods, bringing forward the idea of social justice before transition and specifically acknowledging the plight of seasonal workers, such as peat harvesters, that have not had adequate alternative work options created; they have been left behind. We have called for the establishment of new social training programmes for older unemployed workers who are unable to retrain and acquire new jobs to ensure they are supported until retirement.

So, in conclusion, ladies and gentlemen, I would like to leave with one clear message: the future MFF proposal from the European Commission has been a wake-up call to us all. That future MFF proposal is no longer business as usual. Let's send a clear message from this House that we reject centralisation and stand up for our regions and communities by proposing a strengthened Just Transition Fund II, with simplified procedures, empowering local communities to chart a path not only towards a just transition, but towards a truly fair and just Europe.

Commissioner, I give you this letter as I leave. This is the worker who says he is being left behind. He feels no options were provided after he lost his job. I hope you read it and you take his message to heart. This is real. This is a real person.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marcos Ros Sempere, ponente. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or vicepresidente, el Estado del bienestar empieza en la puerta de casa. Si eso no est脙隆 asegurado, todo lo dem脙隆s se tambalea. Y, para demasiada gente, la vivienda se ha convertido en un motivo de angustia, de asfixia. Y es algo que se repite cada principio de mes. Cada d脙颅a脗聽1 llega el momento de pagar el 40脗聽%, el 50脗聽% o hasta el 60脗聽% de su renta solamente para pagar un techo. Cuando un derecho b脙隆sico se traga m脙隆s de la mitad de la renta disponible de la ciudadan脙颅a, eso tiene un nombre. Y ese nombre es emergencia social, porque la vivienda deber脙颅a ser el pilar sobre el que construir un proyecto de vida propio y el derecho a acceder a una vivienda digna no puede ser un privilegio reservado a una minor脙颅a ni un bien para especular.

El problema es bien claro: entre 2015 y 2023 el precio de la vivienda en la Uni脙鲁n Europea aument脙鲁 un 48脗聽%. En 2023, uno de cada diez hogares urbanos destin脙鲁 m脙隆s del 40脗聽% de su renta a vivienda y cuando el mero acceso a una casa ahoga, asfixia y determina de arriba abajo la vida de la mayor脙颅a, ha llegado la hora de que los poderes p脙潞blicos actuemos.

Por eso, en la elaboraci脙鲁n de este informe hemos escuchado a quienes el alquiler les asfixia cada mes. Hemos escuchado a j脙鲁venes que no pueden ni plantearse salir de casa de sus padres. Hemos escuchado a expertos y a instituciones que reclaman para sus ciudades una pol脙颅tica de vivienda nueva, una pol脙颅tica de vivienda digna. Y eso es, precisamente, lo que hacemos hoy: traemos propuestas concretas, traemos soluciones concretas para una nueva pol脙颅tica de vivienda a nivel comunitario. Porque la Uni脙鲁n Europea puede y debe hacer m脙隆s.

Lo he repetido muchas veces: la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n es la pol脙颅tica social de la Uni脙鲁n Europea. Es la herramienta que convierte las prioridades europeas en proyectos reales, en oportunidades all脙颅 donde m脙隆s falta hace. Y con la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n podemos aumentar la oferta de vivienda asequible ya, tanto en la revisi脙鲁n intermedia como en el marco financiero plurianual a partir de 2027.

En primer lugar, habilitando el FEDER, el Fondo de Cohesi脙鲁n y el Fondo de Transici脙鲁n Justa para dar apoyo espec脙颅fico a inversiones que resuelvan los problemas de la crisis de vivienda, incluyendo la vivienda asequible y sostenible dentro de estos objetivos. Solo as脙颅 aumentaremos la oferta y facilitaremos su acceso a parte de la ciudadan脙颅a.

En segundo lugar, financiaci脙鲁n estable y a largo plazo. La Comisi脙鲁n y el Banco Europeo de Inversiones deben crear nuevas inversiones en vivienda asequible y sostenible, vinculadas a los fondos de cohesi脙鲁n, y combinadas con presupuestos nacionales, regionales y locales. Y hay que hacerlo con una condici脙鲁n irrenunciable: las viviendas que se financien con dinero p脙潞blico deben seguir siendo asequibles y de uso social p脙潞blico durante a脙卤os, prohibiendo taxativamente la venta especulativa a largo plazo. El dinero p脙潞blico debe convertirse en vivienda para vivir y no para especular.

En tercer lugar, simplifiquemos, porque se trata de un problema acuciante. Menos tr脙隆mites y plazos m脙隆s cortos para licencias, ayudas, contrataci脙鲁n clara, colaboraci脙鲁n p脙潞blico-privada. Un enfoque territorial no es solo urbano: tambi脙漏n zonas rurales, islas, regiones remotas.

Y, en cuarto lugar, prioricemos a los colectivos m脙隆s vulnerables. Necesitamos proteger de alquileres abusivos a trabajadores y estudiantes que residen en zonas tur脙颅sticas, recordando la necesidad de garantizar viviendas sostenibles.

Quiero terminar agradeciendo el trabajo generoso y constructivo de mis colegas en la Comisi脙鲁n de Desarrollo Regional, as脙颅 como el de todos los diputados que han contribuido al mismo. Porque la vivienda es uno de los principales factores de desigualdad y es hora de pasar del diagn脙鲁stico a la acci脙鲁n. Es hora de ponerse manos a la obra.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Vladimir Prebili脛聧, Poro脛聧evalec. 芒聙聯 Spo脜隆tovani predsednik, spo脜隆tovani komisar, drage kolegice in kolegi.

Naj za脛聧nem s preprostim vpra脜隆anjem. Ali bomo dokon脛聧no pokopali kohezijo? 脛聦e je tako, potem je ogro脜戮ena Evropska unija kot projekt v celoti. Danes ne razpravljamo le o tehni脛聧nem poro脛聧ilu, ampak o prihodnosti evropske solidarnosti.

Moje poro脛聧ilo o poenostavitvi kohezijskih skladov prihaja v 脛聧asu, ko se Evropska unija pripravlja na naslednji ve脛聧letni finan脛聧ni okvir. In bodimo iskreni 芒聙聯 za vsemi razpravami o u脛聧inkovitosti, pogojevanju in prora脛聧unski disciplini se skriva ena kruta resnica, in to je nevarnost koheziji. Kohezijska politika ni le 脜隆e ena prora脛聧unska postavka. Je obljuba Evrope, da noben dr脜戮avljan, nobena regija, nobena skupnost ne bo ostala pozabljena. Je najjasnej脜隆i dokaz, da Unija ni le trg, ampak solidarna skupnost, kjer naj bi vsi imeli enake mo脜戮nosti za razvoj. A vendar, ko se pribli脜戮ujemo naslednjemu ve脛聧letnemu finan脛聧nemu okviru, sli脜隆im nevarno razmi脜隆ljanje. "Zmanj脜隆ajte sredstva za kohezijo. Zdru脜戮ite jih in centralizirajte, prerazporedite jih za oro脜戮je." Bodimo iskreni 芒聙聯 zmanj脜隆anje kohezijskih sredstev pomeni pove脛聧anje neenakosti, pomeni Evropo dveh hitrosti.

Moje poro脛聧ilo o poenostavitvi kohezijskih skladov zato ne obravnava le obrazcev, postopkov ali informacijskih sistemov. Je politi脛聧na izjava. Kohezijo je treba braniti, poenostaviti in okrepiti. Poenostavitev ni le zmanj脜隆anje administrativnih opravil, je vpra脜隆anje pre脜戮ivetja. 脜陆upani in podjetniki dvigujejo roke nad kohezijo in teh virov ne koristijo, saj je vlaganje zahtevkov za evropska sredstva postalo tekma z ovirami v obliki obrazcev, kontrol in zamud. Kohezija bi morala regije okrepiti, ne pa iz脛聧rpati. Poenostavitev pomeni odpravo dodatnih pravil nacionalnih ministrstev, ki se 脜隆e nalagajo na pravila Evropske unije. Poenostavitev pomeni na脛聧elo enotne revizije. En pregled, ne pet. Poenostavitev pomeni dejanske digitalizirane kontaktne to脛聧ke, ki tudi najmanj脜隆im vasem omogo脛聧ajo dostop do evropskih sredstev. Namre脛聧, 脛聧e kohezija izgubi verodostojnost na terenu, 脛聧e se zdi prepo脛聧asna in preve脛聧 zapletena, potem damo argumente tistim, ki jo 脜戮elijo popolnoma uni脛聧iti. Naj bom konkreten: Slovenija, moja dr脜戮ava, je 脜戮iv dokaz, zakaj je kohezija tako pomembna.

Pred dvema desetletjema se je regionalna neenakost med glavnim mestom in vzhodnim delom dr脜戮ave pove脛聧evala. Zahvaljujo脛聧 kohezijskim skladom je Slovenija vlagala v ceste, raziskovalna sredi脜隆脛聧a ter komunalno in vodno infrastrukturo. Zato je danes med najuspe脜隆nej脜隆imi dr脜戮avami pri zmanj脜隆evanju regionalnih razlik. Naj bom kristalno jasen, kohezija nikoli ne sme biti centralizirana na nacionalni ravni. Enoten nacionalni program, ki bi ga vodili iz prestolnic, bi pomenil konec lokalne politike. In ravno na tej ravni najbolj vedo, kje so potrebne nalo脜戮be. Poenostavitev nikakor ne pomeni bli脜戮njic do goljufij ali korupcije. Potrebujemo pameten, ne ve脛聧 nadzora.

Kolegice in kolegi, vpra脜隆anje je preprosto: ali ho脛聧emo unijo, ki pu脜隆脛聧a svoje regije ob strani, spodbuja neenakost in besedo solidarnost zreducira le na slogan? Ali pa ho脛聧emo unijo, ki vlaga dolgoro脛聧no v razvoj in skupnosti ter daje po脜隆teno prilo脜戮nost vsem? In zakaj mora biti Parlament tukaj jasen? Ker gre za prihodnost kohezije in prihodnost na脜隆ih regij, teh ne smemo izdati. Te so bistvene za stabilnost na脜隆e Evropske unije. 脛聦e oslabimo kohezijo, oslabimo Evropo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Raffaele Fitto, Executive Vice-President of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, let me start by thanking the REGI Committee for the great work, especially the rapporteurs and the shadow rapporteurs. The three reports we discuss today show your strong commitment to cohesion policy. In the coming months, we will work together to shape the future EU budget and the modernisation of cohesion policy. Today's reports show the way.

First, honourable Member Mullooly, my thanks for your report on cohesion policy and the just transition. Our shared goal is clear: a climate-neutral competitive economy that leaves no person and no region behind. The Just Transition Fund has already made a real difference, supporting the region's workers and communities that are most affected. Fourty-six per脗聽cent of just transition funding is now contracted, with over EUR脗聽12 billion worth of projects selected. This instrument will deliver on its objectives. The local plans are key. Stakeholders and decision-makers work together to define the way forward, which adds legitimacy and value. But the Member States and the regions are not alone in implementation. The Commission provides targeted support, for example, through the Just Transition Platform. In addition, the flexibilities introduced by the mid-term review will accelerate Just Transition Fund investment.

Second, honourable Member Ros Sempere, my thanks for your report on cohesion policy and the housing crisis. We all agree: this is one of the most pressing social challenges across Europe, and we are determined to meet this challenge with the new European affordable housing plan. We are enabling Member States to double cohesion investment in affordable housing. As you know, housing is one of the five new priorities of the mid-term review, and the proposal offers flexibilities and incentives.

But we do know that the housing crisis is a very complex phenomenon, and that cohesion policy alone cannot solve it. Complementary funding sources and measures, including revised state aid rules, are essential. That is why we will continue to work closely with the European Parliament, and we will continue to raise awareness and encourage Member States to use all possible sources of investment to support affordable housing. The New European Bauhaus prizes, which will be awarded in the plenary room in Brussels at the end of the month, shows that cohesion policy has a role to play to support affordable, sustainable and inclusive housing solutions for our citizens.

Finally, honourable Member Prebili脛聧, my thanks for your report on simplification and cohesion policy. We agree that the current period has already introduced significant simplification measures, and the mid-term review will take simplification a step further. We all agree that flexibility and simplification are key to making cohesion policy work. They were the main reason behind the mid-term review and the approach we used to shape the proposal for the next multiannual budget.

The Commission's proposal for the future financial framework already addressed many of your recommendations. Our proposal reaffirmed the fundamental principles of cohesion policy: shared management, partnership, multi-level governance and a place-based approach. The next budget also has a strong focus on less developed and disadvantaged regions. Regions will continue to play a crucial role in shaping the policy actions which benefit them. In terms of funding in the national and regional plans, EUR脗聽450 billion will be available for cohesion policy, rural development and fisheries communities. On this, at least EUR脗聽218 billion must be dedicated to the less developed regions.

The spirit of shared management and partnership is dear to me. I firmly believe that partnership is the key for cohesion policy success, and it is in this spirit of partnership that I am here today. I look forward to our discussion and to work together.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Isabel Benjumea Benjumea, ponente de opini脙鲁n de la Comisi脙鲁n BUDG. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or comisario, como usted sabe bien, este Parlamento es un firme defensor de la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n. Y lo es porque es una pol脙颅tica de 脙漏xito, y como espa脙卤ola s脙漏 muy bien de lo que hablo.

Desde la Comisi脙鲁n de Presupuestos damos la bienvenida a toda reforma que venga de la Comisi脙鲁n encaminada a que este pilar de la Uni脙鲁n gane en eficiencia. Por lo tanto, apoyamos esa necesaria simplificaci脙鲁n de programas, la eliminaci脙鲁n de trabas administrativas y duplicidades o la reducci脙鲁n de cargas burocr脙隆ticas innecesarias, todo ello pensando en los destinatarios finales: ciudadanos, familias y empresas.

Pero todo intento de simplificar programas y ganar eficiencia no puede ser a costa de diluir la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n, de centralizar la gesti脙鲁n y de sustituir la participaci脙鲁n activa de las autoridades regionales y locales. Por eso vemos con preocupaci脙鲁n la propuesta del marco financiero plurianual que presentaron ustedes en julio y les animamos a recapacitar.

Simplificar para ganar eficiencia, s脙颅; simplificar para recortar o recentralizar, no. Esta ha de ser la m脙隆xima que gu脙颅e el futuro de la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

PRESIDENZA: PINA PICIERNO
Vicepresidente

脗聽脗聽Jacek Protas, sprawozdawca komisji opiniodawczej 芒聙聯 komisji CONT. 芒聙聯 Szanowna Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Panie Komisarzu! Polityka sp脙鲁jno脜聸ci jest fundamentem, kr脛聶gos脜聜upem jedno脜聸ci i sp脙鲁jno脜聸ci Unii Europejskiej. Wyr脙鲁wnywanie szans rozwojowych, niwelowanie r脙鲁脜录nic w poziomie i warunkach 脜录ycia mieszka脜聞c脙鲁w europejskich region脙鲁w jest naszym podstawowym zadaniem. Je脜录eli o tym zapominamy, to utracimy spoiwo buduj脛聟ce wsp脙鲁lnot脛聶 europejsk脛聟.

M脙鲁wi脛聶 o tym dlatego, by przestrzec przed zakusami, 脜录eby wprowadza脛聡 radykalne zmiany w polityce sp脙鲁jno脜聸ci i wsp脙鲁lnej polityce rolnej po 2027 roku, kt脙鲁re zmierzaj脛聟 do jej nacjonalizacji i centralizacji. Nie da si脛聶 uruchomi脛聡 potencja脜聜脙鲁w rozwojowych region脙鲁w, reagowa脛聡 na ich najwa脜录niejsze potrzeby, przyk脜聜adaj脛聟c do wszystkich jednakow脛聟 miar脛聶. Regiony musz脛聟 mie脛聡 narz脛聶dzia do realizacji w脜聜asnej polityki rozwoju ze wsparciem europejskim. Nie wystarczy, 脜录e b脛聶d脛聟 po脜聸rednikami w rozdzielaniu funduszy zarz脛聟dzanych centralnie. Dekoncentracja to nie to samo co decentralizacja. Powinni脜聸my sobie wszyscy zdawa脛聡 z tego spraw脛聶, planuj脛聟c polityk脛聶 sp脙鲁jno脜聸ci i wsp脙鲁ln脛聟 polityk脛聶 roln脛聟 na kolejne lata.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Giusi Princi, relatrice per parere della commissione EMPL. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signor Commissario e Vicepresidente Fitto, onorevoli colleghi, vengo dalla Calabria, una regione che affronta lo spopolamento delle aree interne, aggravato dal declino demografico e dalla fuga dei nostri giovani.

Ma la Calabria ha tutto: sole per le rinnovabili, talenti per il digitale, bellezza per attrarre investimenti. La vera sfida 脙篓 per脙虏 fare delle nostre ricchezze opportunit脙聽 per il futuro di chi vuole restare.

Per questo, nel mio lavoro in commissione EMPL sulla politica di coesione, ho messo al centro il capitale umano, perch脙漏 ogni giovane che rimane 脙篓 fonte di crescita e sviluppo per i territori.

La nuova politica di coesione pu脙虏 essere ancora pi脙鹿 generativa se continuer脙聽 quindi a mettere al centro la dimensione sociale, al pari della ricerca, dell'industria e delle infrastrutture.

La Calabria e il Sud, nel nuovo quadro semplificato e pi脙鹿 flessibile, possono allora s脙卢 diventare un laboratorio di coesione e innovazione che d脙聽 voce ai territori e ai suoi bisogni; perch脙漏 la questione 脙篓 anche garantire ai giovani le condizioni per cui scelgano di restare.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Estrella Gal脙隆n, ponente de opini脙鲁n de la Comisi脙鲁n EMPL. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, en mi pa脙颅s tenemos un Gobierno de coalici脙鲁n empe脙卤ado en mejorar la vida de la gente. Sin embargo, siempre aparece el mismo problema: el de la vivienda.

Les voy a dar un clar脙颅simo ejemplo para que se pongan en situaci脙鲁n: desde 2018 el salario m脙颅nimo se ha incrementado en Espa脙卤a un 61脗聽%, pero las familias siguen sin poder llegar a fin de mes. Y 脗驴saben cu脙隆l es la raz脙鲁n? Pues es sencilla: en los 脙潞ltimos a脙卤os el alquiler medio ha subido un 78脗聽%, mucho m脙隆s que lo que van subiendo los salarios. La vivienda se ha convertido en una aspiradora de rentas, es el agujero negro adonde se va el dinero de las familias y que aumenta la desigualdad e impide que la gente viva dignamente.

No estamos hablando de estad脙颅sticas vac脙颅as, estamos hablando de nuestras hijas, de nuestros hijos y de nuestras vecinas expulsadas de nuestros barrios por los fondos buitre. Que el precio de la vivienda est脙漏 disparado no es fruto de la casualidad, es resultado de las pol脙颅ticas que han entregado nuestras ciudades y nuestros barrios a estos especuladores que han convertido un derecho en un lujo inaccesible.

La soluci脙鲁n es sencilla: intervenir el mercado de la vivienda. Y este Parlamento debe decidir si est脙隆 del lado de la gente o de la 脙漏lite que ha hecho de nuestra precariedad un negocio.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marit Maij, rapporteur voor advies van de commissie EMPL. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, het cohesiebeleid is een essentieel onderdeel van de Europese Unie en zorgt ervoor dat alle regio芒聙聶s zich economisch en sociaal kunnen blijven ontwikkelen en dat iedereen een voordeel heeft van die o zo belangrijke Europese interne markt. Het cohesiebeleid speelt een belangrijke rol bij het ontwikkelen van banen 芒聙聰 baancreatie 芒聙聰 脙漏n sociale inclusie. Dat moet het cohesiebeleid ook in de toekomst kunnen blijven doen.

Daarvoor moeten we ons in de toekomst ook inzetten voor een apart Europees Sociaal Fonds, met een eigen budget, in het volgende meerjarig financieel kader van de Europese Unie. Een volgend Europees Sociaal Fonds moet meer zijn dan een wassen neus; het moet een fonds zijn waar mensen gebruik van kunnen maken, of dat nu is om vaardigheden voor een volgende baan te ontwikkelen, of om sociaal mee te komen in de Europese Unie en in de samenleving. Want met een asociaal Europa zijn wij verloren.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nikolina Brnjac, u ime kluba PPE. 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, povjereni脛聧e, drage kolegice i kolege, danas mladi ostaju 脜戮ivjeti s roditeljima jer ne mogu priu脜隆titi najamninu, a obitelji u gradovima tro脜隆e prevelik dio prihoda na stanovanje. To je stvarnost milijuna na脜隆ih gra脛聭ana u Europskoj uniji. Kohezijska politika mora biti alat koji odgovara na te potrebe. Zato sam predlo脜戮ila i konkretne mjere.

Prvo, da se ulaganje u priu脜隆tivo stanovanje priznaje kao ulaganje u na脜隆e klimatske ciljeve, 脛聧ime 脛聡emo imati jednostavnije financiranje priu脜隆tivog stanovanja u dr脜戮avama 脛聧lanicama, 脜隆to do sada nije bio slu脛聧aj. Drugo, naglasila sam potrebu za boljom integracijom Socijalnog fonda za klimatsku politiku s kohezijskom politikom kako bi ulaganja u socijalno stanovanje i ciljana pomo脛聡 mladima za pristupa脛聧ne najamnine bila br脜戮a i u脛聧inkovitija. I tre脛聡e, kroz ESF+ predlo脜戮ila sam da se financiraju doplatci za stanovanje i mjere socijalne uklju脛聧enosti povezane s priu脜隆tivim stanovanjem.

U izvje脜隆脛聡u o kojem danas raspravljamo jasno se poziva na pro脜隆irenje djelokruga Kohezijskog fonda ne samo na energetsku u脛聧inkovitost i obnovljive izvore energije nego i na inovativne pristupe koji pove脛聡avaju upravo priu脜隆tivost stanovanja. Ovo izvje脜隆脛聡e daje sna脜戮an temelj Europske komisije za izradu europskog plana priu脜隆tivog stanovanja najavljenog za sljede脛聡u godinu.

Ako 脜戮elimo o脛聧uvati konkurentnost, demografsku ravnote脜戮u i socijalnu pravednost, moramo stambeno pitanje tretirati strate脜隆ki jer dom nije luksuz, dom je temeljna potreba i pravo svakog gra脛聭ana.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nora Mebarek, au nom du groupe S&D. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, Monsieur le Vice-pr脙漏sident, notre transformation 脙漏cologique sera 脙漏quitable ou elle 脙漏chouera, et le Fonds pour une transition juste a 脙漏t脙漏 obtenu, vous l'avez entendu, de haute lutte sous la pr脙漏c脙漏dente l脙漏gislature, pour 脙漏viter que la neutralit脙漏 carbone ne soit v脙漏cue comme une punition.

Ce fonds est vital pour accompagner les travailleurs, les familles et les territoires les plus expos脙漏s. Dans mon d脙漏partement des Bouches-du-Rh脙麓ne, o脙鹿 l'industrie p脙漏trochimique est un pilier de l'脙漏conomie, d脙漏carboner est un imp脙漏ratif. Mais, sans soutien, cela signifie des pertes d'emplois et le d脙漏sespoir social. Gr脙垄ce 脙聽 une contribution forte de l'Union europ脙漏enne, cette mutation peut devenir une chance, avec un financement sur le ch脙麓mage partiel, des reconversions aux formations professionnelles et surtout le maintien du droit de vivre dignement dans sa r脙漏gion.

Or, ce n'est pas du tout ce que nous propose la Commission lorsqu'elle fait dispara脙庐tre purement et simplement le Fonds pour une transition juste du prochain cadre budg脙漏taire 2028-2034. 脙聙 la place, elle lui pr脙漏f脙篓re une transition propre, concentr脙漏e sur des grands projets industriels au d脙漏triment des besoins sociaux et territoriaux. C'est un recul inacceptable, vous l'avez entendu sur tous les bancs de ce Parlement. Aussi, notre Parlement trace une autre voie, un nouveau fonds, une directive sur la transition juste et l'implication totale des r脙漏gions et des partenaires sociaux. Notre message est clair: sans solidarit脙漏 europ脙漏enne, pas de transition juste et durable sur notre continent.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Klara Dostalova, za skupinu PfE. 芒聙聯 Pan脙颅 p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, pane komisa脜聶i, kohezn脙颅 politika v脛聧etn脛聸 investic do bydlen脙颅 maj脙颅 slou脜戮it jednomu jasn脙漏mu 脙潞脛聧elu 芒聙聯 zlep脜隆it 脜戮ivot Evropan脜炉, maj脙颅 sni脜戮ovat rozd脙颅ly, rozv脙颅jet infrastrukturu a zajistit dostupn脙漏 bydlen脙颅. Jen脜戮e m脙颅sto praktick脙陆ch 脜聶e脜隆en脙颅 je Komise prom脛聸脜聢uje v n脙隆stroje politick脙漏ho n脙隆tlaku a ideologick脙陆ch experiment脜炉. Sly脜隆脙颅me sliby o zjednodu脜隆en脙颅 芒聙聯 m脙漏n脛聸 byrokracie, v脙颅ce digitalizace, siln脛聸j脜隆脙颅 podpora pro obce. To v脜隆echno zn脙颅 dob脜聶e, ale realita je bohu脜戮el jin脙隆. Fondy jsou st脙隆le 脛聧ast脛聸ji v脙隆z脙隆ny na podm脙颅nky, kter脙漏 s r脜炉stem nemaj脙颅 nic spole脛聧n脙漏ho 芒聙聯 na Zelenou dohodu, genderov脙漏 agendy nebo pr脙隆vn脙颅 st脙隆t.

Kohezn脙颅 politika se p脜聶itom nesm脙颅 st脙隆t rukojm脙颅m politick脙陆ch spor脜炉 ani laborato脜聶脙颅 nov脙陆ch ideologi脙颅. Pokud m脙隆 kohezn脙颅 politika m脙颅t smysl, mus脙颅 se vr脙隆tit ke sv脙漏mu posl脙隆n脙颅 podporovat ob脛聧any investicemi, sni脜戮ovat region脙隆ln脙颅 rozd脙颅ly, a ne je zat脛聸脜戮ovat centralizovanou kontrolou. Lid脙漏 venku nepot脜聶ebuj脙颅 dal脜隆脙颅 Green Deal, nepot脜聶ebuj脙颅 genderov脙漏 p脜聶脙颅ru脛聧ky ani nekone脛聧n脙漏 bruselsk脙漏 podm脙颅nky. Pot脜聶ebuj脙颅 pr脙隆ci, pot脜聶ebuj脙颅 bydlen脙颅 a pot脜聶ebuj脙颅 norm脙隆ln脙颅 脜戮ivot. Je pot脜聶eba si p脜聶ipom脙颅nat, 脜戮e kohezn脙颅 pen脙颅ze nejsou pen脙颅ze Komise. Jsou to pen脙颅ze ob脛聧an脜炉. A tyto pen脙颅ze maj脙颅 j脙颅t na inovace, silnice, nemocnice, 脜隆koly a bydlen脙颅. M脛聸jme na pam脛聸ti, 脜戮e kohezn脙颅 politika je jedn脙颅m z m脙隆la pozitivn脛聸 vn脙颅man脙陆ch n脙隆stroj脜炉 evropsk脙漏 politiky. A na to bychom nem脛聸li nikdy zapom脙颅nat.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Antonella Sberna, a nome del gruppo ECR. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signor Vicepresidente Fitto, onorevoli colleghi, la crisi abitativa oggi 脙篓 una delle pi脙鹿 grandi emergenze sociali in Europa. I numeri parlano chiaro: secondo Eurostat, oltre il 7脗聽% degli europei spende pi脙鹿 del 40脗聽% del proprio reddito per la casa e in alcuni paesi la percentuale 脙篓 molto pi脙鹿 alta.

Senza una casa accessibile e dignitosa i giovani non costruiscono famiglie, i lavoratori non mettono radici e i territori si svuotano. Ecco perch脙漏 la politica di coesione ha un ruolo decisivo in questo, e grazie al suo lavoro, Commissario Fitto, i fondi strutturali stanno diventando pi脙鹿 flessibili, meno burocratici e capaci di rispondere ai bisogni concreti delle persone.

Oggi abbiamo una grande opportunit脙聽, che 脙篓 quella di armonizzare tutti gli strumenti a disposizione, dalla revisione del PNRR alla revisione di medio termine della politica di coesione, fino alla pi脙鹿 ampia programmazione europea per la casa.

In Italia il governo guidato da Giorgia Meloni ha lanciato un piano casa nazionale, che vuole trasformare l'abitare in una leva di crescita e coesione.

Io credo che questa intuizione debba e possa diventare anche una sfida che tutti noi possiamo cogliere in Europa: un piano casa europeo, sostenuto dalla politica di coesione, capace di contrastare lo spopolamento delle aree interne e rafforzare la tenuta demografica, perch脙漏 la vera forza dell'Europa non sar脙聽 misurata dai palazzi e dalle istituzioni, ma dalle case in cui le famiglie potranno mettere radici.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Raquel Garc脙颅a Hermida-Van Der Walle, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Commissioner, colleagues, the cohesion policy is a foundational policy for our Union. We invest in our weakest link to create a stronger chain. All of our regions and all of our citizens should benefit.

As times change and new challenges arise, we want and need cohesion policy to adapt and modernise. We can simplify access to funds without lowering the standards. We can strengthen our European defence while investing in smaller companies and poorer regions. We can address one of the biggest concerns of the European citizens present here today, namely the housing crisis, and meet our green ambitions.

Cohesion policy is valuable, literally. It is the best ambassador of our values and the desire for an ever closer Union. Therefore, we have to be fierce and ruthless in defending its foundation. Centralisation, as proposed by the European Commission, undermines these fundaments.

And this Parliament, also today, has been very clear: regions should remain at the core of this policy and we need to ensure that not one single euro from European funds ends up in the hands of those who want to destroy the rule of law and our shared European values. No money to autocrats and their cronies ever.

Cohesion policy is worth improving and worth investing. It sends a message of an idea dear to all of us: a Europe that protects.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gordan Bosanac, u ime kluba Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, povjereni脛聧e Fitto, jako mi je drago da smo kroz ovo prvo izvje脜隆脛聡e koje 脛聡e Parlament donijeti o stanovanju jasno detektirali probleme stambene krize.

Prvo su spekulativna ulaganja i prepu脜隆tanje politike stanovanja privatnom kapitalu. Dalje to tako vi脜隆e ne mo脜戮e. Drugo je manjak investicija u priu脜隆tivo i socijalno stanovanje, mobilizaciju praznih stanova i energetsku obnovu. To je smjer u kojem moramo i脛聡i dalje. I tre脛聡i problem je rast kratkoro脛聧nog najma odnosno nekontroliranje kratkoro脛聧nog najma. Tomu isto tako trebamo stati na kraj.

Dobra vijest je da je u kohezijskoj politici sada prepoznato stanovanje i to 脛聡u pozdraviti, da je novac sada na raspolaganju dr脜戮avama 脛聧lanicama da se bave ovim temeljnim uzrocima. Ali lo脜隆a vijest je da je na neki na脛聧in Komisija kapitulirala pred dr脜戮avama 脛聧lanicama i u stvari pustila sve u ruke dr脜戮ava 脛聧lanica da same odlu脛聧e ho脛聡e li se ili ne脛聡e baviti ovom krizom ili 脛聡e se baviti nekim drugim prioritetom. I to je lo脜隆a vijest.

Isto tako, ho脛聡u naglasiti jo脜隆 jednom kako bez obzira na napade koje slu脜隆amo o zelenoj tranziciji u ovom Parlamentu, ona je oti脜隆la ve脛聡 predaleko i ne脛聡e je zaustaviti niti moje kolege iz ECR-a, niti nitko iz desnice.

I zbog toga mislim da uistinu moramo shvatiti da je pravedna tranzicija koja 脛聡e biti adekvatno financirana klju脛聧 daljnjeg napretka. Ona ne mo脜戮e biti prepu脜隆tena samo dr脜戮avama 脛聧lanicama i volio bih ja脛聧u ulogu i Parlamenta i Komisije, da ne radimo kapitulaciju pred dr脜戮avama 脛聧lanicama.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Irmhild Bo脙聼dorf, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Klimagerechtigkeit, sch脙露ner Wohnen, bl脙录hende Landschaften脗聽芒聙聯 was soll eigentlich noch alles aus den Koh脙陇sionsmitteln bezahlt werden? Machen wir uns ehrlich: Einen gro脙聼en Teil der Probleme, die wir nun m脙录hsam l脙露sen m脙录ssen, haben wir selbst verursacht. Niemand zwingt uns, Klimaneutralit脙陇t bis zum Jahr 2050 herzustellen; dieses Ziel wurde losgel脙露st von der Realit脙陇t festgelegt. Produktionsverlagerung, carbon leakage, Investitionsabwanderungen sind eine erste Folge; Hunderttausende von Arbeitspl脙陇tzen gehen verloren. Die Deindustrialisierung des Kontinents hat l脙陇ngst begonnen.

Auch die Wohnungskrise ist hausgemacht. Wer Millionen von Einwanderern, Asylsuchenden, Fremdlingen in den Kontinent l脙陇sst, darf sich am Ende nicht wundern, dass der Wohnraum nicht mehr ausreicht.

Koh脙陇sionsmittel sind nicht daf脙录r gedacht, eine schlechte Politik zu heilen; sie sollen den l脙陇ndlichen Raum wieder lebenswert machen. Die einfache und unkomplizierte Zuteilung der Koh脙陇sionsmittel ist der einzig vern脙录nftige Vorschlag, der heute hier gemacht worden ist.

脗聽脗聽Kl脙隆ra Dobrev (S&D). 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k Asszony! Hadd mondjak el 脙聳n脙露knek egy t脙露rt脙漏netet, k脙录l脙露n脙露sen az Eur脙鲁pai Parlament jobb oldal脙隆n 脙录l脜聭 jobboldali k脙漏pvisel脜聭t脙隆rsaimnak. Orb脙隆n Viktor 15 脙漏ve papol nek脙录nk versenyk脙漏pess脙漏gr脜聭l. Ennek olt脙隆r脙隆n fel脙隆ldozta a munka t脙露rv脙漏nyk脙露nyv脙漏t, l脙漏trehozta a "rabszolgat脙露rv脙漏nyt", 脙漏s Eur脙鲁pa legolcs脙鲁bb 脙漏s legkiszolg脙隆ltatottabb munkaerej脙漏vel l脙隆tja el els脜聭sorban a milli脙隆rdos nagyv脙隆llalatokat. Elt脙露r脙露lte gyakorlatilag a k脙露rnyezetv脙漏delmet. A vil脙隆g legmocskosabb akkumul脙隆torgy脙隆rai szennyezik szinte korl脙隆tok n脙漏lk脙录l a magyar mez脜聭gazdas脙隆gi f脙露ldeket, term脜聭f脙露ldeket 脙漏s vizeket, 脙漏s p脙漏ld脙隆ul nem vezeti be az eur脙鲁t, hogy a gyenge forinttal seg脙颅tse a multik versenyk脙漏pess脙漏g脙漏t 芒聙聯 akarom mondani a profitj脙隆t.

Sz脙鲁val 15 脙漏ve papol versenyk脙漏pess脙漏gr脜聭l. Megtette mindezeket a l脙漏p脙漏seket, amelyeken l脙隆tom, hogy n脙漏ha 脙聳n脙露k is k脙漏sz脙录lnek. 脙職gyhogy feltehetj脙录k a k脙漏rd脙漏st: de siker脙录lt? 脙聣s az a v脙隆laszom, hogy nem. Tot脙隆lis kudarc, tot脙隆lis kudarc. 15 脙漏vvel ezel脜聭tt Magyarorsz脙隆g Kelet-K脙露z脙漏p-Eur脙鲁pa egyik legmen脜聭bb orsz脙隆ga volt. Ma Eur脙鲁pa legszeg脙漏nyebb orsz脙隆g脙隆v脙隆 v脙隆ltunk, 脙漏s ennek mind ez a jobboldali gazdas脙隆g- 脙漏s t脙隆rsadalompolitika az oka. 脙聣ppen ez脙漏rt 脙漏n azt javasoln脙隆m az Eur脙鲁pai Parlamentnek, hogy a holnapi napon k脙露vess脙漏k a baloldal 脙潞tj脙隆t, a baloldal javaslatait: tisztess脙漏ges fizet脙漏sek, nyugd脙颅jak, lak脙隆s脙漏p脙颅t脙漏s, eg脙漏szs脙漏g脙录gy, oktat脙隆s. Ezek teszik versenyk脙漏pess脙漏 az eur脙鲁pai embereket. A nagyv脙隆llalatok helyett 脙隆lljunk az emberek oldal脙隆ra!

脗聽脗聽Julien Leonardelli (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, l'attribution des fonds europ脙漏ens est aujourd'hui un v脙漏ritable scandale. La France contribue massivement au budget de l'Union et pourtant nos territoires sont trop souvent oubli脙漏s.

Chez moi, en Occitanie, la r脙漏alit脙漏 est simple. Les infrastructures de transport se d脙漏gradent. Nos zones rurales restent enclav脙漏es. L'eau manque chaque ann脙漏e un peu plus et, chaque 脙漏t脙漏, nos campagnes br脙禄lent, tandis que nos pompiers manquent de moyens pour prot脙漏ger les habitants. Voil脙聽 la vraie vie de nos concitoyens.

Dans ce contexte, on nous pr脙漏sente le Fonds pour une transition juste comme un outil de justice sociale. Mais la r脙漏alit脙漏 est tout autre. Ce fonds, financ脙漏 par les Europ脙漏ens eux-m脙陋mes, est noy脙漏 sous la complexit脙漏 administrative. R脙漏sultat: les collectivit脙漏s locales, les agriculteurs et les associations se retrouvent face 脙聽 une jungle de proc脙漏dures. L'aide arrive trop tard ou n'arrive jamais.

Il y a plus grave encore. Ce fonds n'a pas pour objectif de r脙漏pondre aux besoins urgents des Europ脙漏ens. Il sert avant tout 脙聽 accompagner une transition 脙漏cologique impos脙漏e, qui d脙漏truit nos industries, qui sacrifie nos emplois et qui plonge nos territoires dans la pr脙漏carit脙漏.

Bruxelles trouve toujours des milliards pour financer des projets pharaoniques 脙聽 l'autre bout du monde, pendant que nos paysans, nos p脙陋cheurs, nos ouvriers luttent pour survivre. Notre position est claire: l'argent des Europ脙漏ens doit rester en Europe. Il doit servir nos nations, nos r脙漏gions, nos travailleurs, nos agriculteurs, et non financer une transition 脙漏cologique punitive ni arroser des projets ext脙漏rieurs. C'est une question de justice. C'est une question de bon sens. Il est temps de b脙垄tir une Europe au service de ses peuples et non une Europe au service du reste du monde.

脗聽脗聽Waldemar Buda (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Panie Komisarzu! Tutaj przed nami powinna sta脛聡 wielka czarna trumna. My dzisiaj dyskutujemy o propozycjach bud脜录etowych na nast脛聶pn脛聟 perspektyw脛聶 z polityk脛聟 sp脙鲁jno脜聸ci mniejsz脛聟 o po脜聜ow脛聶, o 50%. To jest 脜聸mier脛聡 kliniczna, to jest katastrofa. I ja nie mam tutaj 脜录adnych pretensji do pana komisarza Fitto, poniewa脜录, o zgrozo, ten bud脜录et przygotowa脜聜 Polak 芒聙聯 Piotr Serafin. Ale, Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo, my si脛聶 nie mo脜录emy na to zgodzi脛聡. Je脜录eli s脛聟 inne zadania, prosz脛聶 bardzo, ale nie kosztem polityki sp脙鲁jno脜聸ci i nie kosztem region脙鲁w. A ten projekt niestety tak wygl脛聟da.

I jeszcze mam uwag脛聶, 脜录eby te 脜聸rodki by脜聜y po prostu rzetelnie i przyzwoicie dysponowane. My w Polsce mamy rozkradzione 脜聸rodki KPO na jachty, na muzyk脛聶 techno na balkonie i tego typu podobne projekty. Wi脛聶c ja tutaj apeluj脛聶 r脙鲁wnie脜录 do Pana Komisarza o przyjrzenie si脛聶, jak s脛聟 脜聸rodki europejskie w Polsce wydawane, kiedy ja za 脜聸rodki europejskie wydatkowanie odpowiada脜聜em w Polsce, byli脜聸my liderami w Europie. Dzisiaj si脛聶 kupuje jachty, ponad sto jacht脙鲁w kupiono za 脜聸rodki z KPO i to jest skandal. Nie mo脜录e to si脛聶 prze脜聜o脜录y脛聡 na polityk脛聶 sp脙鲁jno脜聸ci.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Joachim Streit (Renew). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, sehr geehrter Herr Kommissar! Wenn Koh脙陇sionsmittel ein Drittel des Haushaltes der EU ausmachen, dann ist das ein ganz wichtiger Punkt, zu dem wir heute sprechen d脙录rfen, und es beeindruckt mich auch als Mitglied des Haushaltsausschusses.

Ganz, ganz wichtig ist es, dass wir die Vielzahl von Fonds zusammenfassen und es einfacher machen脗聽芒聙聯 einfacher f脙录r diejenigen, die vor Ort Verantwortung tragen. Deshalb bin ich froh, sehr geehrter Herr Kommissar Fitto, dass Sie hier mitverantwortlich sind, denn Sie sind von Geburt an Regionalpolitiker, und als Regionalpolitiker wei脙聼 man ganz genau, was Politiker vor Ort haben wollen: Sie wollen keinen Zentralismus, sie wollen keine komplizierten Verfahren, sie wollen nicht abh脙陇ngig sein von der Politik des Heimatlandes, sondern vor Ort mit den Menschen einen Plan aufstellen, wie ihre Region f脙录r die Zukunft aufgebaut werden soll.

Deshalb m脙录ssen wir uns h脙录ten, bei der Koh脙陇sionspolitik einen zentralistischen Ansatz zu w脙陇hlen, wie ihn Frau von der Leyen m脙露chte, sondern wir m脙录ssen im Grundsatz dort bleiben, wo Koh脙陇sionspolitik immer war, n脙陇mlich bei den Kommunen. Das hei脙聼t: Subsidiarit脙陇t; es wird entschieden vor Ort.

Und zweitens: Was ich auch verlange, ist eine Konnexit脙陇t. Wenn die EU etwas haben will, was vor Ort noch nicht stattfindet, dann muss man auch die Mittel daf脙录r geben, sowohl f脙录r das Personal als auch f脙录r die Dinge.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Katrin Langensiepen (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kommission! Klimawandel ist keine Phase, Hase. Das ist nicht irgendwas, was man sich mal ausgedacht hat, was gerade mal passt, und dann kickt infantiles Verhalten ein, und ich mache die Augen zu, und den Klimawandel gibt es gar nicht.

Wir haben Anfang der Woche, also gestern, dr脙录ber gesprochen, 脙录ber die Landstriche, die vertrocknen, wo es Waldbr脙陇nde gibt, wo Menschen ihr Heim verlieren; und dann hei脙聼t es, ja, der Klimawandel, aber eigentlich gibt es den ja gar nicht. Den Klimawandel werden wir nicht stoppen k脙露nnen, aber die Folgen, die k脙露nnen wir abfedern f脙录r die armen Menschen.

Wie ist die Wohnungssituation f脙录r obdachlose Menschen? Wir haben ja eine Beschlusslage, Obdachlosigkeit in der EU bis 2030 zu beenden. Da m脙录ssen wir die Kommunen unterst脙录tzen, denn die sagen: 芒聙聻Mach ich gerne, aber von welchem Geld? Und wer bekommt es?芒聙聹

Ich glaube, die Akzeptanz f脙录r die EU und f脙录r die Arbeit, die wir hier leisten, ist: Was macht die EU, dieses Br脙录ssel f脙录r mich? Kann ich von der Miete leben? Kann ich auch als armer Mensch Wohnraum bekommen? Wie kann eine Kommune von Br脙录ssel profitieren? Und das st脙陇rkt die Demokratie, und das ist unsere Aufgabe脗聽芒聙聯 K脙录rzen, Intransparenz ist da keine L脙露sung.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kathleen Funchion (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madam President, I was happy to contribute positively to a report on simplifying access to EU funds. As many of us know, it is the simple fact that many groups in communities are very frustrated at and by the maze of paperwork drawing down EU funds involves. So in my contribution to this report, I tackled things like streamlining the number of data requests and listening to the people on the ground who were actually applying for funds.

However, I cannot support a report which cannot talk about the simplification without pushing for the militarisation of cohesion funds. This should be completely out of bounds politically, yet other groups lined up to accept without any resistance.脗聽How can allowing funds for regional development for defence purposes even be countenanced? Changing the rules so that the ESF+ and the ERDF can be put to use for the military industry is a gross betrayal.

Growing up in Ireland, we learned the value of EU funds to our economy and to our infrastructure. They were a symbol of practical solidarity for economically lagging regions. Now they are just to be used as another fund for topping up arms and the war-making industry. It is a shameful move, showing how nothing is safe from this agenda but will be an excuse in the future to cut funds from vital social projects in order to pay for weapons.

脗聽脗聽Elena Nevado del Campo (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario y vicepresidente de la Comisi脙鲁n Europea. Durante d脙漏cadas, la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n se ha erigido como parte sustancial del acervo comunitario: el nexo de Europa con sus territorios, la herramienta de su propio crecimiento conforme a sus necesidades reales.

Queremos regiones m脙隆s competitivas y queremos hacer frente a las diferentes situaciones y retos: desde la transici脙鲁n justa hasta las infraestructuras o la vivienda. Pero, ciertamente, con el 0,3脗聽%脗聽del PIB europeo tampoco se pueden obrar milagros.

Y yo tambi脙漏n tengo una historia que contarles. En Espa脙卤a, el gobierno de izquierdas, progresista, de coalici脙鲁n, nos prometi脙鲁 la construcci脙鲁n de 184脗聽000 viviendas que ni est脙隆n ni se las espera. Planes estatales de vivienda que ni est脙隆n ni se los espera. Y un gran parque p脙潞blico de vivienda que ni est脙隆 ni se lo espera.

Por eso necesitamos tambi脙漏n los fondos de cohesi脙鲁n europeos. Y por eso tampoco queremos una pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n que elimine a las regiones y a los municipios de la ecuaci脙鲁n. Usted sabe lo importante que es para las regiones y los municipios la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n para su progreso y para su salida hacia delante.

Por eso tampoco queremos dejar la cohesi脙鲁n en manos de los Gobiernos centrales. Europa pasa por ser y estar cohesionada. La cohesi脙鲁n hace Europa y no al rev脙漏s.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matthias Ecke (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Morgen verabschieden wir drei Initiativberichte 脙录ber die Zukunft der Koh脙陇sionspolitik, und ich muss ehrlich sagen: Vielleicht h脙陇tten wir uns die ganze Arbeit sparen k脙露nnen, vielleicht war sie umsonst.

Was n脙录tzt denn ein Vorschlag f脙录r eine simplifizierte F脙露rderung, wenn die Kommission plant, die Regionalpolitik, wie wir sie kennen, ab 2028 quasi abzuschaffen? Was bringt denn ein Mechanismus f脙录r einen gerechten 脙聹bergang, wenn wir den einzigen Fonds f脙录r diesen Strukturwandel, der genau daf脙录r da ist脗聽芒聙聯 den脗聽JTF脗聽芒聙聯, im n脙陇chsten EU-Haushalt nicht wiederfinden?

Ich hatte gerade in meiner Region eine gro脙聼e Konferenz zum JTF mit dem Gru脙聼wort von Ihnen, Herr Fitto, wo Sie begeisternde Projekte vorgestellt haben: KMU, Transfer, Forschung. Und was kann ich denen sagen: 芒聙聻Toll, was ihr gemacht habt, aber es wird gestrichen!芒聙聹?

Die EU-F脙露rderpolitik war immer das sichtbarste Zeichen Europas: Wir lassen euch nicht allein beim Weg in die Klimaneutralit脙陇t, beim Zusammenwachsen der Regionen, bei einer Wirtschaftsentwicklung, die 脙录berall ankommt. Und jetzt scheint dieses Versprechen gebrochen.

Wir sind weiterhin f脙录r eine Koh脙陇sionspolitik, die die Regionen in den Mittelpunkt stellt. Wir sind gegen eine Zentralisierung mit den Hauptst脙陇dten. Wir wollen Wohlstand in allen Teilen Europas.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tam脙隆s Deutsch (PfE). 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k Asszony! Besz脙漏lj脙录nk a l脙漏nyegr脜聭l! Elfogadhatatlan, hogy a koh脙漏zi脙鲁s forr脙隆sok foly脙鲁s脙颅t脙隆s脙隆t Br脙录sszel az elm脙潞lt 脙漏vekben a politikai zsarol脙隆s eszk脙露zek脙漏nt haszn脙隆lta. Az Eur脙鲁pai Uni脙鲁 nem parancsuralmi rendszer. Az EU 脙潞gy nem m脜卤k脙露dhet, hogy a br脙录sszelit脙隆k hatalmi sz脙鲁val megmondj脙隆k, meghat脙隆rozz脙隆k, megparancsolj脙隆k, jog脙隆llamis脙隆gi elj脙隆r脙隆sokkal kik脙漏nyszer脙颅tik, hogy hogyan is 脙漏ljenek a magyarok. A tag脙隆llamok szuverenit脙隆s脙隆t, a nemzetek szabads脙隆g脙隆t nem lehet uni脙鲁s p脙漏nz脙录gyi eszk脙露z脙露kkel csorb脙颅tani. 脗聽脗聽Vicent Marz脙聽 Ib脙隆脙卤ez (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario, tan solo en los 脙潞ltimos dos a脙卤os, en Valencia ha aumentado el precio del alquiler un 40%. Solo en dos a脙卤os. En Elx, el 70% de las viviendas del mercado de alquiler de la ciudad est脙隆n secuestradas para alquiler tur脙颅stico, 脗隆el 70%!, y no van para los vecinos y para las vecinas.

Y por eso le voy a proponer dos cuestiones. La primera, que los fondos de cohesi脙鲁n que se dedican a vivienda solo se puedan dedicar a viviendas asequibles y que si se destinan a viviendas asequibles, que esas viviendas no puedan luego pasar al libre mercado, que no se pueda especular con ellas.

脗聽脗聽Dan-脜聻tefan Motreanu (PPE). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, domnule comisar, stima脠聸i colegi, 脙庐ncep prin a mul脠聸umi colegilor responsabili pentru rapoartele dezb脛聝tute ast脛聝zi 脠聶i doresc s脛聝 fac referire la necesitatea simplific脛聝rii politicii de coeziune.

Exist脛聝 脙庐nc脛聝 regiuni ale Uniunii Europene 脙庐n care autorit脛聝脠聸ile locale 脠聶i 脙庐ntreprinderile mici 脠聶i mijlocii prefer脛聝 s脛聝 apeleze la credite bancare sau s脛聝 a脠聶tepte dup脛聝 fonduri guvernamentale, 脙庐n loc s脛聝 acceseze fonduri europene, deoarece procedurile birocratice complexe, costurile ridicate 脠聶i condi脠聸ionalit脛聝脠聸ile stricte fac ca finan脠聸area european脛聝 s脛聝 fie dificil de ob脠聸inut 脠聶i de gestionat.

Aceast脛聝 situa脠聸ie este cauzat脛聝 at脙垄t de regulile stabilite de la nivel european, dar mai ales de regulile suplimentare impuse de la nivel na脠聸ional 脠聶i care adesea sunt inutile. Din acest motiv, am depus o serie de amendamente la Raportul privind simplificarea, prin care solicit un set unic de reguli pentru accesarea fondurilor europene, precum 脠聶i aplicarea auditului unic 脙庐n ceea ce prive脠聶te implementarea, monitorizarea 脠聶i controlul.

脗聽脗聽Irene Tinagli (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signor Vicepresidente Fitto, onorevoli colleghi, parlo italiano cos脙卢 ci capiamo subito.

Il tema della casa 脙篓 un tema ormai enorme e i fondi di coesione sono spesso le poche risorse e i pochi strumenti che hanno gli amministratori locali sui territori, nelle regioni, nelle citt脙聽, per dare un po' di sollievo e di risposte ai cittadini, e quindi sono importantissimi.

Per脙虏, come si direbbe, non basta la parola, non basta mettere i fondi sulla carta: bisogna capire come vengono spesi, con quali strategie, con quali tempistiche, con quali condizionalit脙聽.

Gli porto solo due o tre esempi.

Primo: a noi ha fatto molto piacere la revisione di medio termine dei fondi, con il raddoppio da 7 a 15 miliardi di euro sull'housing,脗聽per脙虏 molti sindaci e amministratori ci dicono che era tardi, che non ci sono le condizioni, che molti erano gi脙聽 stati spesi. Non so se Lei ha dei dati su questo, per脙虏 solo per dire che la capacit脙聽 di avere dei tempi lunghi di programmazione per questo tipo di investimenti 脙篓 fondamentale.

Secondo: le condizionalit脙聽. Essere sicuri che queste risorse vadano davvero a progetti che hanno un impatto sociale rilevante, perch脙漏 脙篓 l脙卢 dove noi abbiamo bisogno: il disagio sociale.

Terzo: riguardo alla nuova proposta per le politiche di coesione, la preoccupazione 脙篓 per la centralizzazione delle decisioni a livello nazionale. Non possiamo togliere dei margini di manovra laddove c'脙篓 pi脙鹿 bisogno a livello locale e territoriale.

Quindi, per favore, agiamo, perch脙漏 abbiamo milioni di cittadini e di amministratori che hanno bisogno del nostro sostegno.

脗聽脗聽Val脙漏rie Deloge (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, le rapport qui nous est pr脙漏sent脙漏 ici illustre une d脙漏rive inqui脙漏tante, toujours plus inqui脙漏tante. Cette d脙漏rive, c'est celle du pacte vert, qui occupe de plus en plus l'espace mental et politique des centristes du Parlement.

La politique de coh脙漏sion, dont la raison de vivre est de soutenir l'脙漏conomie de nos territoires, est d脙漏sormais transform脙漏e en instrument politique du pacte vert de la Commission europ脙漏enne. Le Fonds pour une transition juste devient conditionn脙漏 脙聽 des crit脙篓res id脙漏ologiques, et la vision de ce rapport n'est rien de plus que la transformation de nos r脙漏gions en bureaucratie bruxelloise centralis脙漏e.

La v脙漏ritable transition juste, c'est celle qui soutient nos agriculteurs et nos territoires ruraux, sans id脙漏ologie, en soutenant l'innovation de terrain, la souverainet脙漏 alimentaire et la baisse des normes, pas celle qui conditionne nos fonds 脙聽 des d脙漏rives inqui脙漏tantes, toujours plus inqui脙漏tantes.

脗聽脗聽S脙漏rgio Gon脙搂alves (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, senhor Vice-Presidente Raffaele Fitto, a um ano do fim do prazo de execu脙搂脙拢o do Plano de Recupera脙搂脙拢o e Resili脙陋ncia, 60脗聽% dos marcos e metas a n脙颅vel europeu continuam por cumprir.脗聽Estes n脙潞meros demonstram que os fundos centralizados em programas nacionais, que foram apresentados como uma solu脙搂脙拢o para melhorar a efici脙陋ncia dos mesmos, n脙拢o trouxeram melhores resultados.
