脗聽脗聽M脙漏lanie Disdier (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, la politique de coh脙漏sion est un 脙漏l脙漏ment central de la construction et de la solidarit脙漏 europ脙漏ennes. Que les plus riches aident les plus pauvres, c'est le ciment de l'Europe.

Malheureusement, ces beaux principes sont souvent ternis quand la Commission s'en m脙陋le. Aujourd'hui, l'argent collect脙漏 passe par une bureaucratie bruxelloise qui n'h脙漏site pas 脙聽 se servir de l'argent des 脙聣tats membres pour faire avancer son propre agenda, quitte 脙聽 d脙漏border de ses comp脙漏tences.

Faire transiter des fonds par la Commission, c'est lui donner du pouvoir dont on sait qu'elle se servira invariablement pour 脙漏craser les volont脙漏s nationales. Dernier d脙漏bordement en date: certains veulent d脙漏tourner l'usage de la politique de coh脙漏sion pour investir dans la d脙漏fense, comp脙漏tence qui, comme tout le monde le sait, ne rel脙篓ve en aucun cas des attributions de la Commission.

De plus, le principe de solidarit脙漏 est que les plus forts aident les plus faibles. Encore faut-il garantir la prosp脙漏rit脙漏 de ceux qui produisent l'effort financier. Or, le comportement de paillasson de Mme脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen face 脙聽 Donald脗聽Trump cet 脙漏t脙漏 d脙漏montre de mani脙篓re flagrante qu'elle est incapable de prot脙漏ger nos int脙漏r脙陋ts europ脙漏ens.

脗聽脗聽Nora Junco Garc脙颅a (ECR). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidente, se脙卤or comisario, este informe INI, aunque no es vinculante, incide en lo esencial: hace un llamamiento serio a adoptar estrategias nacionales de vivienda, a simplificar las normas, a usar bien los fondos europeos y a proteger la propiedad privada y el derecho a permanecer en comunidad.

脗聽脗聽Sofie Eriksson (S&D). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Jag ska ber脙陇tta om Kiruna i Sverige. Ovanf脙露r gruvan ligger hem och liv f脙露r m脙陇nniskor som tillsammans med Malmberget och Svappavaara st脙楼r f脙露r 80 procent av Europas j脙陇rnmalm. Men gruvbrytningen 脙陇r ett gemensamt intresse f脙露r hela unionen. Industripolitiken och regionalpolitiken m脙楼ste h脙陇nga ihop. N脙陇r Kiruna f脙露rs脙露rjer stora delar av EU med j脙陇rnmalm kan inte Kirunaborna l脙陇mnas ensamma med notan. Det 脙陇r dags f脙露r EU och den svenska regeringen att kliva fram.

脗聽脗聽Isabella Tovaglieri (PfE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, la sinistra ha un problema con la propriet脙聽 privata, lo ha dimostrato di fatto candidando e dando voce a figure come Ilaria Salis, che hanno fatto delle occupazioni abusive la loro bandiera politica e che difendono sempre e comunque l'illegalit脙聽, come nel caso dell'opposizione allo sgombero del Leoncavallo.

Noi invece stiamo dalla parte del buon senso. Grazie al nuovo decreto sicurezza 芒聙聯 osteggiato con tutte le forze dalla sinistra 芒聙聯 difendiamo la propriet脙聽 privata a spada tratta, prevedendo sgomberi immediati e pene severe per chi occupa abusivamente.

Purtroppo anche l'Europa 脙篓 succube di questa sinistra radicale e lo dimostra con provvedimenti assurdi, come le case green, che andranno ancora una volta a penalizzare i proprietari di casa.

L'Europa non ha capito che per gli italiani la casa non 脙篓 un capriccio o un bene qualunque, ma 脙篓 famiglia, 脙篓 tradizione ed 脙篓 cultura. La casa 脙篓 sacra.

E allora non basta stanziare qualche fondo, peraltro condizionato come sempre ad assurde norme green, ma dobbiamo proteggere la propriet脙聽, come diceva il politologo Walter Lippmann: senza propriet脙聽 non esiste libert脙聽.

(L'oratrice accetta di rispondere a una domanda "cartellino blu")

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Raquel Garc脙颅a Hermida-Van Der Walle (Renew), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 I will speak in English. I was wondering, given your your very fierce defence of private property, which I share with you 芒聙聯 and I wish that that we all shared those thoughts 芒聙聯 if you're willing to defend so fiercely the good use of European funds, because that is a huge problem in Italy. The amount of fraud that there is in Italy at this moment is gigantic, and I don't see your government doing much about that. So I would like to ask you to put as much energy into defending the Europeans' property, our funds, in your country.

脗聽脗聽Isabella Tovaglieri (PfE), risposta a una domanda "cartellino blu". 芒聙聯 La ringrazio per la domanda, perch脙漏 io faccio parte di un partito, la Lega, il cui ministro dell'Economia e delle finanze, il ministro Giorgetti, 脙篓 stato tra i principali osteggiatori di un provvedimento che era nato con buoni auspici, che era quello di tutelare la casa alla fine del COVID e di consentire agli italiani di ristrutturarla a spese dello Stato attraverso il bonus del 110脗聽%, il famigerato "superbonus", che si 脙篓 rivelato fallimentare e si 脙篓 rivelato un buco nero enorme per le casse dello Stato.

E solo grazie alla presenza della Lega al governo, con il ministro Giorgetti, che oggi quello scempio che ci rendeva ridicoli agli occhi degli altri cittadini europei e anche a livello internazionale 芒聙聯 perch脙漏 脙篓 servito per rifare le case a chi aveva la seconda casa al mare 芒聙聯 non c'脙篓 pi脙鹿 e noi oggi possiamo avere ancora credibilit脙聽 nell'utilizzo dei fondi.

脗聽脗聽Lara Magoni (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, domani voteremo in plenaria la riforma di medio termine della politica di coesione, fortemente voluta dal Vicepresidente Fitto, al quale va il mio plauso sincero per l'attenzione al tema dell'housing e per la capacit脙聽 di aggiornare la politica di coesione alle sfide attuali.

Ma ai fondi europei servir脙聽 affiancare anche i capitali privati per costruire alloggi dignitosi e accessibili e creare nuova occupazione; solo cos脙卢 l'Europa potr脙聽 incidere davvero sulla vita delle persone, invece di perdersi in politiche ideologiche come quelle della casa green.

脗聽脗聽Andreas Schieder (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Herr Kommissar! Gerade Wohnen ist eines der Probleme der europ脙陇ischen B脙录rgerinnen und B脙录rger, die in allen europ脙陇ischen Mitgliedstaaten gleich sind. In den letzten 15脗聽Jahren sind die Mieten um 26脗聽Prozent im Durchschnitt angestiegen, und die Menschen mit den niedrigsten Einkommen zahlen 40 oder mehr Prozent ihres verf脙录gbaren Einkommens bereits f脙录rs Wohnen.

Das ist ein unzumutbarer Zustand, denn Wohnen ist ein Grundrecht, das Dach 脙录ber dem Kopf ist ein Grundrecht. Deshalb m脙录ssen wir mehr tun: nicht nur Koh脙陇sionsmittel daf脙录r verwenden, sondern noch viel, viel mehr.

Wir brauchen europ脙陇ische Gelder in der H脙露he von 300脗聽Milliarden脗聽Euro. Wir m脙录ssen die Spekulation eind脙陇mmen, wir m脙录ssen die short-term rentals wie Airbnb und dergleichen regulieren und eind脙陇mmen. Wir m脙录ssen mehr gef脙露rderte Wohnungen, auch f脙录r die Mittelschicht, in unserem Wettbewerbsrecht erm脙露glichen. Wir m脙录ssen den Mieterschutz st脙陇rken, um die Leute vor ungerechtfertigten K脙录ndigungen und extremen Erh脙露hungen zu sch脙录tzen. Und wir d脙录rfen nicht auf den Klimaschutz vergessen, denn auch daf脙录r braucht es Gelder脗聽芒聙聯 f脙录r thermische Renovierung. Denn Wohnen ist ein Grundrecht, das sich jeder und jede leisten k脙露nnen soll in Europa.

脗聽脗聽Victor Negrescu (S&D). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, domnule comisar, politica de coeziune este de ne脙庐nlocuit. Ea nu poate s脛聝 fie comasat脛聝, diluat脛聝 sau condi脠聸ionat脛聝 de reforme sau criterii netransparente sau antisociale. Politica de coeziune trebuie r脛聝m脙垄n脛聝 a脠聶a cum e stipulat脛聝 脙庐n tratate. Procedura "catch-the-eye"

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nina Carberry (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, let me start by saying that the current MFF proposal undermines cohesion policy. Cohesion must remain properly funded and accessible, not merged with CAP infrastructure and other priorities. By centralising management through single national plans, this proposal sidelines regional programmes and risks silencing the voices closest to citizens.

In Ireland, I recently visited the Laois Public Participation Network. That partnership now has over 700脗聽registered groups, representing more than 51脗聽000 people. They've used cohesion funds to launch climate projects, help with Storm 脙聣owyn recovery and kick-start sports initiatives. These are precisely the kinds of initiatives that would be threatened by this merger.

That is why the Just Transition Fund and other mechanisms must remain dedicated instruments under the cohesion policy. Their scope must be broadened so that more regions in transition can benefit. Because cohesion is about people and solidarity, it must not be watered down.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Francisco Jos脙漏 Mill脙隆n Mon (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n constituye un pilar fundamental de la Uni脙鲁n para reducir las diferencias entre pa脙颅ses y entre regiones. Por ejemplo, en mi tierra, en Galicia, los fondos de cohesi脙鲁n han sido esenciales para la modernizaci脙鲁n econ脙鲁mica y el desarrollo de infraestructuras, y Galicia y su Gobierno son un modelo en la ejecuci脙鲁n de fondos europeos.

Quiero mencionar un par de elementos sobre la propuesta de la Comisi脙鲁n Europea para el pr脙鲁ximo marco financiero plurianual. En primer lugar, lamento la propuesta centralizadora de la Comisi脙鲁n, pues los Gobiernos regionales conocen de primera mano las necesidades de las subregiones, y este conocimiento debe aprovecharse: el modelo de los Next Generation EU no ha sido bueno.

En segundo lugar, lamento el recorte presupuestario a la cohesi脙鲁n. Los fondos de cohesi脙鲁n se reducen en cerca de un 15脗聽% respecto al marco financiero actual. Por supuesto, es importante el impulso a las pol脙颅ticas de innovaci脙鲁n y competitividad, pero no a cuenta de reducir el apoyo a la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n.

Y hablando de recortes, tambi脙漏n tengo que mencionar la reducci脙鲁n que ha sufrido la financiaci脙鲁n en agricultura y pesca. La pesca me preocupa mucho. La propuesta de la Comisi脙鲁n solo contempla una asignaci脙鲁n espec脙颅fica de 2脗聽000 millones para pesca frente a 6脗聽000 millones, un recorte de m脙隆s de un 60脗聽%. Trabajaremos para revertirlo, y espero que el Gobierno espa脙卤ol tambi脙漏n trabaje en la misma l脙颅nea.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maria Grapini (S&D). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, domnule comisar, stima脠聸i colegi, dezbatem politica de coeziune, poate cel mai important lucru pentru proiectul european. Cele trei rapoarte: tranzi脠聸ie just脛聝, criza locuin脠聸elor, simplificarea fondurilor de coeziune, toate, domnule vicepre脠聶edinte, pot duce la o coeziune real脛聝.

Problema pe care eu o pun aici 脠聶i v脛聝 rog s脛聝 r脛聝spunde脠聸i: Crede脠聸i c脛聝 lua脠聸i aceste rapoarte脗聽芒聙聭脗聽Comisia 芒聙聭 脠聶i ve脠聸i 脠聸ine cont de ceea ce noi spunem acum? Dac脛聝 ve脠聸i lua fonduri de la politica de coeziune pentru 脙庐narmare 芒聙聭 da, trebuie s脛聝 ne ap脛聝r脛聝m, dar nu lua脠聸i fonduri de la politica de coeziune. Ini脠聸ial, doamna pre脠聶edint脛聝 a spus c脛聝 vom g脛聝si resurse suplimentare, nu s脛聝 lu脛聝m de la Fondul de coeziune sau de la politica agricol脛聝 comun脛聝.

Locuin脠聸ele, criza de locuin脠聸e, p脛聝i dac脛聝 toate pre脠聸urile cresc, evident c脛聝 va cre脠聶te 脠聶i costul locuin脠聸ei, iar veniturile multor, multor cet脛聝脠聸eni europeni scad, nu cresc. 脠聵i mai e o problem脛聝, Uniunea European脛聝 are o problem脛聝 demografic脛聝 脠聶i acest lucru are leg脛聝tur脛聝 cu criza locuin脠聸elor. Nu putem s脛聝 avem 脙庐ntemeiere de familii, familii tinere, care s脛聝 fac脛聝 copii dac脛聝 nu 脙庐脠聶i pot permite o locuin脠聸脛聝.

Domnule comisar, pute脠聸i s脛聝 ne spune脠聸i la sf脙垄r脠聶it dac脛聝 ve脠聸i prezenta 脙庐n colegiul comisarilor cele trei rapoarte 脠聶i ve脠聸i 脠聸ine cont c脛聝 politica de coeziune este o necesitate, nu este un moft?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, the green transition will only succeed if it's just. We cannot reach climate neutrality by 2050 by sacrificing the livelihoods of miners in Sicily, young workers in Asturias or entire communities in Western Macedonia.

Cohesion policy is not just a funding tool. It's Europe's promise that no region and no citizen will be left behind. This report reminds us that the transition must be territory-based, socially inclusive and economically resilient. That means reskilling, local investment and active social policies, not just CO2 targets.

It also means that cohesion must work hand in hand with instruments like the Just Transition Fund, the European Social Fund+ and the Recovery and Resilience Facility, with less bureaucracy, more flexibility and real local ownership.

Let's be clear: this is not just about climate, it's about democracy, solidarity and the future of Europe's regions. Because if the Green Deal is our destination, cohesion is the road that gets us there.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Daniel Buda (PPE). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, domnule comisar Fitto, politica de coeziune este inima solidarit脛聝脠聸ii europene. Ea trebuie s脛聝 r脛聝m脙垄n脛聝 instrumentul prin care reducem decalajele 脠聶i sprijinim regiunile r脛聝mase 脙庐n urm脛聝. Tocmai de aceea, domnule comisar, a脠聶 dori s脛聝 transmite脠聸i mesajul nostru foarte ferm 脙庐n cadrul colegiului comisarilor.

Avem nevoie de o politic脛聝 de coeziune puternic脛聝. Avem nevoie de o politic脛聝 de coeziune independent脛聝 脠聶i avem nevoie de o politic脛聝 de coeziune finan脠聸at脛聝 脙庐n mod adecvat. Doar a脠聶a vom putea avea o Uniune European脛聝 puternic脛聝. Pentru c脛聝 tr脛聝im vremuri complicate, 脙庐ntr-un context geopolitic dificil, cu r脛聝zboi la grani脠聸脛聝, schimb脛聝ri climatice, presiuni sociale 脠聶i economice, cum ar fi criza locuin脠聸elor. Or, aceast脛聝 criz脛聝 a locuin脠聸elor trebuie s脛聝 cunoasc脛聝 un r脛聝spuns european, deoarece statele membre ast脛聝zi 脠聶i-au demonstrat, din nefericire, incapacitatea de a rezolva aceast脛聝 problem脛聝.

Dublarea investi脠聸iilor 脙庐n locuin脠聸e accesibile nu este doar un gest social, domnule comisar, ci un r脛聝spuns la speran脠聸a unui viitor sigur, pe care milioane de tineri 脠聶i familii 脙庐l a脠聶teapt脛聝 de la Europa.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alicia Homs Ginel (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or vicepresidente, ante nosotros tenemos una crisis habitacional sin precedentes. 脗驴Qu脙漏 hacemos cuando millones de j脙鲁venes en Europa no pueden iniciar sus proyectos vitales? 脗驴Cuando las familias m脙隆s vulnerables destinan m脙隆s de la mitad de su sueldo a un alquiler o cuando se expulsa a vecinas y vecinos de sus barrios? Llegados a este punto, la Uni脙鲁n no solo tiene una posibilidad, sino que tiene la obligaci脙鲁n de actuar.

脗驴Y c脙鲁mo? Lo explica muy bien el informe del compa脙卤ero Marcos Ros: modificando las reglas y permitiendo que los fondos de cohesi脙鲁n se puedan destinar a vivienda asequible y sostenible, facilitando a los Estados las herramientas y los recursos para construir, rehabilitar y garantizar una vivienda digna para todas y para todos, y asegurando que cada euro que Europa invierta en un parque de vivienda p脙潞blico quede siempre ah脙颅 y no acabe en manos de los especuladores.

Vicepresidente, lo que est脙隆 en juego no es una mercanc脙颅a m脙隆s, sino la vida, la dignidad y el futuro de generaciones enteras. La Uni脙鲁n Europea tiene que estar a la altura. No perdamos esta oportunidad.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, la pervivencia de un escal脙鲁n de gobernanza regional en la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n no es solamente un examen existencial para la raz脙鲁n de ser de Europa, sino para el sentimiento de pertenencia de la ciudadan脙颅a a la que este Parlamento representa, especialmente en las regiones insulares y alejadas, como las regiones ultraperif脙漏ricas.

Y no se puede hacer m脙隆s con menos. Por tanto, las prioridades para el Grupo Socialista est脙隆n se脙卤aladas en la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n que se identifica con la pol脙颅tica social: financiaci脙鲁n para las emergencias 芒聙聰que cada vez son m脙隆s frecuentes y devastadoras芒聙聰 pero tambi脙漏n financiaci脙鲁n para una nueva prioridad: la pol脙颅tica de vivienda.

Este nuevo pilar de cohesi脙鲁n resulta divisivo ideol脙鲁gicamente, porque hay quien se aproxima a la vivienda como un bien de mercado y de especulaci脙鲁n, pero los socialistas lo hacemos como un derecho que la Uni脙鲁n Europea tiene la obligaci脙鲁n de atender, con una financiaci脙鲁n suficiente, y tiene que estar incrustado en la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n con un escal脙鲁n de gobernanza regional.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Fine della procedura "catch the eye")

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Raffaele Fitto, Executive Vice-President of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, many thanks for this rich and useful discussion, and to the rapporteurs and the shadow rapporteurs for their work. I have taken good note of your views. You raised many valid and useful points, for example, on simplification. The new MFF proposal introduced a single set of rules for more funds. Concerning flexibility, dedicated financial reserves will allow Member States and regions to address emerging needs or unforeseen shocks. Regions will remain central 芒聙聯 they will be central in the design and implementation of the National and Regional Partnership Plans in the new MFF.

The other points you mentioned should form the basis of our continuing discussions on the work. There is a long road ahead of us. We are just at the beginning. Let us continue to work together for a modernised cohesion policy which delivers for all regions.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ciaran Mullooly, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Madam President, thank you to all the contributors this afternoon. I was delighted to hear, Commissioner, you talk about the 46脗聽% of the Just Transition Fund contractors and the EUR脗聽12脗聽billion gone out to projects.

But of course, as you have heard from our colleagues today, if we had greater simplification, a more straightforward system of applying and less red tape, I believe it would be much higher. And I think we need to focus on that in the future. Many of my colleagues have raised very fair points: there must be a social dimension to the heart of this policy, not just in housing but in other areas. We need that social dimension. We must, of course, include young people, and we must look out for them.

Just transition cannot be left to Member States. This is a crucial part, Commissioner, going forward: we cannot just say, 'here is the fund, do what you wish with it.' I have seen firsthand what they do with it, Commissioner, it is not pretty. In my region, they closed the peat-burning power station down, but they were not ready to transition to renewables, so in the car park of this power station today, they burn diesel oil in a peaker plant. This is crazy. It is nuts.

So we must ensure our Member States are ready for this. Similarly, they now propose to take down power stations in my community and they have no major renewable project lined up. Instead they say they will spend between EUR脗聽10 and脗聽EUR脗聽15脗聽million taking away these power stations, and the workers who have lost their jobs read reports in the newspapers that companies in India and elsewhere will buy some of the materials and the equipment from the old power stations and bring them to India. This type of report drives the people in the community absolutely berserk. They say 'where is the sense in this? Where is the organisation?'

That is why, Commissioner, we need the Commission to be essentially involved as we go forward, we need the Commission to take control of the Just Transition Fund and ensure it works for the people who lost their jobs.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marcos Ros Sempere, ponente. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or vicepresidente, vivimos una emergencia habitacional, y una situaci脙鲁n de emergencia merece una respuesta de los poderes p脙潞blicos que est脙漏 a la altura.

No votamos un texto m脙隆s: votamos el primer documento sobre la crisis de vivienda de esta legislatura. Votamos para que parte del presupuesto europeo se traduzca en bienestar para una mayor脙颅a social que est脙隆 agotada y ahogada por este problema.

Este informe fija otro rumbo. Este informe abre la puerta a una inversi脙鲁n en vivienda que no tiene precedentes en la historia de la Uni脙鲁n Europea. Y lo hace con un principio irrenunciable: cuando hay dinero p脙潞blico encima de la mesa, la vivienda permanecer脙隆 siempre asequible y de uso p脙潞blico, porque las casas son para vivir, no para especular con ellas.

Este no es un informe m脙隆s para guardar en un caj脙鲁n. Es una propuesta con un marcado perfil social y que pone soluciones claras encima de la mesa. Aprobemos este informe y con 脙漏l aumentemos la oferta p脙潞blica de vivienda, contribuyamos a trav脙漏s de la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n a crear un gran parque p脙潞blico que equilibre el efecto de un mercado privado que est脙隆 descontrolado.

Si aprobamos este texto, si aprobamos este mandato del Parlamento, estaremos poniendo la primera piedra de un gran parque p脙潞blico de vivienda europeo y estaremos devolviendo a las personas lo que nunca debi脙鲁 perderse: ver en su casa un hogar, un lugar que signifique tranquilidad y sosiego, un lugar para desarrollar su proyecto de vida y no un motivo de angustia cada primero de mes.

Este Parlamento votar脙隆 ma脙卤ana un cambio de rumbo decisivo. Votamos poner a la Uni脙鲁n Europea a la vanguardia, para ayudar a resolver una de las crisis m脙隆s graves que afronta nuestro continente.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Vladimir Prebili脛聧, Poro脛聧evalec. 芒聙聯 Gospa predsednica, spo脜隆tovani.

Naj zaklju脛聧im z zahvalo vsem 芒聙聯 poro脛聧evalcem v senci, kolegom iz odbora, sekretariatu in predvsem 脜隆tevilnim partnerjem iz regij, civilne dru脜戮be, ki so prispevali ideje in predloge sprememb. To je bil resni脛聧no skupen napor in skupaj smo opravili veliko delo.

A danes je 脜隆ele za脛聧etek, saj sta pred nami vsaj 脜隆e dve bitki. Prva je poenostavitev. Ne smemo pustiti, da se kohezija utopi v papirologiji. Ob脛聧ine, mala podjetja in srednja podjetja, organizacije civilne dru脜戮be, vsi morajo imeti mo脜戮nost dostopa do sredstev, brez strahu, da bodo ostali pokopani pod obrazci in nadzornimi postopki. Kohezija je namenjena opolnomo脛聧enju, ne iz脛聧rpavanju; in poenostavitev mora pomeniti predvsem partnerstvo, obvezno vklju脛聧evanje lokalne uprave, socialnih partnerjev in drugih lokalnih akterjev v vsako fazo na脛聧rtovanja in implementacije. Brez njih namre脛聧 kohezija izgubi svojo legitimnost.

Druga bitka pa je ve脛聧letni finan脛聧ni okvir. Odlo脛聧il bo, ali bo kohezija ostala hrbtenica solidarnosti Evropske unije ali pa jo bomo izvotlili z zmanj脜隆anji in preusmeritvami sredstev. Kolegi, to je bistvenega pomena. Brez mo脛聧nega namenskega prora脛聧una za kohezijo ne bo konvergence, ne bo uravnote脜戮ene rasti in na koncu ne bo zaupanja v Evropsko unijo.

Zatorej, ko glasujete o tem poro脛聧ilu, ne glasujete samo o poenostavitvah. Glasujete za ohranitev nedotakljivosti kohezijske politike kot take. S tem po脜隆iljate sporo脛聧ilo Svetu in Komisiji: v naslednjem finan脛聧nem okviru ne bomo dopustili oslabitve kohezijske politike. Kohezija je obljuba Evropske unije njenim dr脜戮avljankam in dr脜戮avljanom, zato moramo izpolniti obljubo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 Ringrazio tutte e tutti per il dibattito ricco e interessante.

La discussione congiunta 脙篓 chiusa.

La votazione si svolger脙聽 domani.

Dichiarazioni scritte (articolo 178)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gabriela Firea (S&D), in writing. 芒聙聯 Role of cohesion policy investment in resolving the current housing crisis

I supported this report because it brings concrete solutions, not abstract debates. With European cohesion funds, we can build homes for young people and families who today spend over 40% of their income only on rent or mortgages.

In the last few years, housing prices have gone up by almost 50% across Europe. For many young Romanians, buying a house has become nearly impossible: and two out of three say they postpone this step because of costs. And yet, cohesion policy currently dedicates only 2% of its budget (about 7.5 billion euro) to housing, when the real need is at least 270 billion per year.

This report asks for more ambition: doubling European funds for affordable housing, investing in social housing and energy-efficient renovations, creating a European platform together with the Investment Bank, and using public buildings that today stand empty to give people a decent home.

For Romania, this means real chances for our young people, security for families, and stronger communities. A home is not a luxury, but a right, and it is our duty to make it possible.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽P脙隆l Szekeres (PfE), 脙颅r脙隆sban. 芒聙聯 T脙露bb mint egymilli脙鲁 uni脙鲁s polg脙隆r k脙漏rte: az Eur脙鲁pai Uni脙鲁 koh脙漏zi脙鲁s politik脙隆ja kezelje kiemelt figyelemmel azokat a r脙漏gi脙鲁kat, amelyek nemzeti, nyelvi, kultur脙隆lis vagy etnikai saj脙隆toss脙隆gaik miatt k脙录l脙露n figyelmet 脙漏s anyagi t脙隆mogat脙隆st 脙漏rdemelnek. A nemzeti r脙漏gi脙鲁k v脙漏delm脙漏ben ind脙颅tott eur脙鲁pai polg脙隆ri kezdem脙漏nyez脙漏s nem egy tag脙隆llam bel脙录gye, hanem egy m脙漏lyen eur脙鲁pai 脙录gy: a soksz脙颅n脜卤s脙漏g meg脜聭rz脙漏s脙漏nek 脙录gye, a diszkrimin脙隆ci脙鲁mentes, harmonikus fejl脜聭d脙漏s 脙录gye. A polg脙隆ri kezdem脙漏nyez脙漏sre adott Eur脙鲁pai Bizotts脙隆g v脙隆lasza ki脙隆br脙隆nd脙颅t脙鲁: ink脙隆bb t脜卤nik jogi kifog脙隆sgy脜卤jtem脙漏nynek, mint val脙鲁di politikai v脙隆lasznak. A Bizotts脙隆g az uni脙鲁s hat脙隆sk脙露r脙露k sz脜卤k 脙漏rtelmez脙漏s脙漏re hivatkozva gyakorlatilag elutas脙颅tja a kezdem脙漏nyez脙漏s l脙漏nyeg脙漏t: a nemzeti 脙漏s etnikai r脙漏gi脙鲁k k脙录l脙露nleges figyelemben r脙漏szes脙颅t脙漏s脙漏t. Ez cinikus hozz脙隆脙隆ll脙隆s, hiszen mik脙露zben az EU a kultur脙隆lis 脙漏s nyelvi soksz脙颅n脜卤s脙漏get z脙隆szlaj脙隆ra t脜卤zi, a gyakorlatban nem hajland脙鲁 semmilyen 脙潞j garanci脙隆t adni azok meg脜聭rz脙漏s脙漏re. A t脙露bb mint egymilli脙鲁 al脙隆脙颅r脙鲁 akarat脙隆t 脙颅gy formai 脙漏rvekkel s脙露prik f脙漏lre, mik脙露zben hosszan sorolj脙隆k a m脙隆r megl脙漏v脜聭, sokszor csak pap脙颅ron l脙漏tez脜聭 programokat. Ez a dokumentum vil脙隆gosan mutatja, hogy a Bizotts脙隆g f脙漏l szemben脙漏zni a politikai realit脙隆ssal: a nemzeti r脙漏gi脙鲁k 脙漏s kisebbs脙漏gi k脙露z脙露ss脙漏gek jogai, fejl脜聭d脙漏se 脙漏s gyarapod脙隆sa tov脙隆bbra is kiszolg脙隆ltatottak a tag脙隆llamok k脙漏nye-kedv脙漏nek. Az ilyen elutas脙颅t脙鲁, b脙录rokratikus megk脙露zel脙颅t脙漏s al脙隆脙隆ssa a polg脙隆ri kezdem脙漏nyez脙漏s int脙漏zm脙漏ny脙漏nek hiteless脙漏g脙漏t, 脙漏s azt 脙录zeni az 脙隆llampolg脙隆roknak: az EU-ban hi脙隆ba 脙漏l a r脙漏szv脙漏teli demokr脙隆cia ill脙潞zi脙鲁ja, val脙鲁j脙隆ban a politikai akarat hi脙隆nyzik a v脙隆ltoztat脙隆shoz.

脗聽

15. Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism: simplification and strengthening (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 L'ordine del giorno reca la relazione di Antonio Decaro sul meccanismo di adeguamento del carbonio alle frontiere: semplificazione e rafforzamento (COM(2025)0087- C10-0035/2025 - 2025/0039(COD)) (A10-0085/2025)

Ricordo agli onorevoli deputati che per questa discussione si terr脙聽 un solo giro di interventi di oratori dei gruppi politici, pertanto non 脙篓 prevista la procedura "catch-the-eye" e non saranno accettati interventi "cartellino blu".

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Antonio Decaro, relatore. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, all'inizio di questa legislatura abbiamo preso un impegno chiaro con i cittadini europei: fare tutto quello che 脙篓 nelle nostre possibilit脙聽 per salvare il nostro pianeta.

In questo primo anno abbiamo lavorato per tutelare l'ambiente, contrastare i cambiamenti climatici, stimolare le nostre imprese a investire con coraggio sulle tecnologie verdi, fornendo loro gli strumenti per tornare finalmente ad essere competitive nel mondo.

Abbiamo ben chiare le difficolt脙聽 che oggi il settore industriale ed economico vive, sapendo che le conseguenze di una nuova crisi ricadrebbero in parte anche sui lavoratori. Per tale ragione, da subito ci siamo messi al lavoro sulle semplificazioni, che sono un pilastro importante della nuova politica europea, tenendo fissati per脙虏 gli obiettivi ambientali che sono sanciti nel脗聽green deal.

In questo scenario si inserisce la modifica al regolamento CBAM, che ci apprestiamo a votare domani: intendiamo mantenere gli obiettivi ambientali ambiziosi e, allo stesso tempo, ridurre la burocrazia per le piccole imprese.

Nello specifico, sostituiamo la soglia脗聽de minimis basata sul valore delle merci con una soglia di 50 tonnellate annue di importazioni per settori strategici come l'acciaio, il ferro, l'alluminio, il calcestruzzo e i fertilizzanti. Questo permetter脙聽 di esentare il 90脗聽% degli importatori responsabili solo dell'1脗聽% delle emissioni complessive, mantenendo alti quindi i livelli di attenzione sugli obiettivi climatici e sui grossi produttori di inquinamento.

Questa proposta prevede anche semplificazioni nelle procedure autorizzative, nella raccolta dei dati e nella verifica delle emissioni finali, fissando la data di avvio del CBAM a febbraio脗聽2027, concedendo quindi alle nostre imprese il tempo necessario per adeguarsi ai nuovi standard.

Inutile dirvi, colleghe e colleghi, che questa proposta mira a proteggere le imprese europee contro una politica economica nemica dei paesi inquinatori e, nel caso specifico, degli Stati Uniti, che hanno imposto anche dei dazi tra il 30 e il 50脗聽% proprio sui prodotti che sono coperti dal CBAM.

Inutile, quindi, che debba essere io a spiegarvi l'importanza di questa proposta in questo momento delicato e, a riprova di quello che sto dicendo, pongo alla vostra attenzione proprio il lavoro che 脙篓 stato fatto in commissione ENVI, dove ci siamo impegnati a lavorare solo sulle modifiche proposte dalla Commissione europea senza riaprire altre disposizioni, volendo dare un segnale chiaro di compattezza e di unit脙聽 di intenti rispetto al contesto che oggi ci circonda.

Confido in questo approccio pragmatico e responsabile, che domani possa trovare il sostegno necessario per confermare la proposta sul CBAM nella sua interezza e dando piena efficacia; spero che possa diventare un modello di lavoro che possa ispirare tutti noi nelle prossime azioni future dove affronteremo dei fascicoli complicati, come la nuova PAC, il quadro finanziario pluriennale e la gestione dei fondi di coesione.

Se vogliamo davvero costruire una cittadinanza europea forte e radicata, occorre che siamo innanzitutto noi che rappresentiamo le istituzioni europee a fare il primo passo, dimostrando cura del territorio, cura dei cittadini, attenzione alle imprese, attenzione al mondo del lavoro e soprattutto una visione strategica nelle politiche ambientali.

Solo cos脙卢 potremo dire di aver fatto un buon servizio per le nostre comunit脙聽, che oggi tutti noi, in quest'Aula, abbiamo l'onore di poter servire.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

IN THE CHAIR: SABINE VERHEYEN
Vice-President

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Wopke Hoekstra, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, and could I also start by thanking Mr脗聽Decaro very much for his great work.

Final doesn't exist in life, but I think we are as close to finalising as we could possibly be in terms of one hugely important part of CBAM, and that is simplification.

You know that I've always advocated for street smarts and ambitious climate policy. And, at the very heart of this endeavour 芒聙聯 and making sure that we bridge between climate and competitiveness 芒聙聯 in my view, is the ETS, which is a market-based system which functions very well and which has been at the heart of interest throughout the world at present day.

CBAM is a natural addition to our ETS system, but we do have the opportunity to further beef up, to further streamline and to further improve our CBAM system. And we do so through a four-step approach. One is simplification, which we're discussing today. The second one is exports, which we've already touched upon as well, and which I hope to also finalise by the end of this calendar year. The third is scope. And the fourth 芒聙聯 hugely important for our industry 芒聙聯 is making sure that we deal with circumvention, or attempts to circumvent, in a very thorough way.

Again, today is about simplification. Why is this important? Well, because we're taking 90脗聽% of companies out of scope while upholding 99脗聽% of emissions. So, a huge win for those who are in favour of, on the one hand, climate action and, at the same time, simplification. That is what we're doing today. And I think that is a huge win for both climate and industry, but, could I say, also for common sense.

There's one last thing, Madam脗聽President, that I wanted to mention, and that is that the legislators 芒聙聯 and certainly also this Parliament 芒聙聯 have delivered on this proposal with considerable speed, and that is hugely important. We're often stuck in bureaucratic processes. It often takes too long for a good idea to be put into practice. And yes, even here it might not be the speed of light, but it is as close as possible as we could get in political life. So I wanted to thank all co-legislators, but Parliament in particular, for their speedy delivery on this proposal.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Karin Karlsbro, f脙露redragande av INTA-utskottets yttrande. 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Kommissionen! Kollegor och 脙楼h脙露rare! EU forts脙陇tter att vara v脙陇rldens b脙陇sta milj脙露organisation, och idag visar vi att det g脙楼r att f脙露renkla men samtidigt h脙楼lla fast vid klimatambitionerna. I en global ekonomi m脙楼ste alla ta ansvar och minska utsl脙陇ppen, inte bara flytta dem n脙楼gon annanstans, till n脙楼gon annan del i v脙陇rlden.

EU:s klimattullar 脙陇r ett utm脙陇rkt exempel p脙楼 hur vi kan anv脙陇nda EU:s handelspolitik som verktyg f脙露r att n脙楼 v脙楼ra klimatm脙楼l. Det ska inte l脙露na sig att flytta sina utsl脙陇pp till n脙楼got annat st脙陇lle. Usel klimatpolitik ska inte vara en konkurrensf脙露rdel. I en tid n脙陇r USA l脙陇mnar Parisavtalet m脙楼ste vi forts脙陇tta visa att det 脙陇r m脙露jligt och koppla tillv脙陇xt till gr脙露n omst脙陇llning. V脙楼ra v脙陇ljare r脙露stade f脙露r det v脙楼r industri efterfr脙楼gade, och klimatet kr脙陇ver det.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 As Sandra G脙鲁mez L脙鲁pez for the Committee on Budgets is not there at the moment, I give the floor to Filip Turek on behalf of the ITRE Committee.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Filip Turek, rapporteur for the opinion of the ITRE Committee. 芒聙聯 Madam President, let me tell you a little story. The European Union puts billion-euro fines on its own factories for producing CO2. The EU produces only about 6脗聽%脗聽to 8脗聽% of global emissions, yet the world's emissions keep rising. To make this look fairer, the CBAM was introduced, but the reality is different.

In the end, we are punishing ourselves: production and investment just leave the European market. Only the biggest players with armies of officers can still afford to trade with the EU. And we believe this will change the climate. It will not. The planet is not cooling anyway.

European companies go bankrupt, consumers pay more and Europe is simply turning into a green open-air museum. If you are serious about your favourite world competitiveness, I have one solution for you: cancel all emission targets, taxes and tariffs. Cancel the Green Deal!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie-Luce Brasier-Clain, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, tout le monde sait que les mesures propos脙漏es ici sont clairement insuffisantes. Nous avons toujours d脙漏fendu l'id脙漏e d'une taxe carbone aux fronti脙篓res de l'Europe, mais jamais sous cette forme. Il fallait r脙漏ussir le tour de force de faire croire 脙聽 un outil protectionniste enfin, quand, par rejet de ce m脙陋me protectionnisme, on n'impose qu'une mesure environnementale.

La question du march脙漏 carbone est centrale. C'est elle qui rend ce m脙漏canisme nocif pour notre industrie, puisqu'en supprimant les quotas gratuits dont elle b脙漏n脙漏ficiait jusque-l脙聽 il lui cause un pr脙漏judice qui ne sera jamais compens脙漏.

Dans le contexte 脙漏conomique actuel脗聽芒聙聯 droits de douane am脙漏ricains, surcapacit脙漏 chinoise, co脙禄t de l'脙漏nergie脗聽芒聙聯, une disparition des quotas gratuits est un nouveau coup dur port脙漏 脙聽 notre comp脙漏titivit脙漏. La balle est dans le camp des 脙聣tats et de la Commission. C'est leur 脙漏cologisme politique qui nous a conduits 脙聽 cette situation. 脙聙 eux de sortir de leur logiciel d脙漏pass脙漏 et d'entrer de plain-pied dans le XXI谩碌聣脗聽si脙篓cle.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anna Zalewska, w imieniu grupy ECR. 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Panie Komisarzu! To krok w dobrym kierunku. Po raz pierwszy Komisja tak wyra脜潞nie pos脜聜ucha脜聜a pa脜聞stw cz脜聜onkowskich, kt脙鲁re wdra脜录aj脛聟 CBAM i przemys脜聜. Ale to jest niewystarczaj脛聟ce, bo wykluczenie ma脜聜ych przedsi脛聶biorstw to jeszcze nie uratowanie europejskiego przemys脜聜u, o czym przecie脜录 w sprawozdaniu pisze Draghi.

Czas na kolejny krok i wprowadzenie pilnych rozwi脛聟za脜聞, kt脙鲁re realnie obni脜录脛聟 ceny produkcji energii w Europie. Potrzebujemy realnych zmian w dyrektywie ETS, kt脙鲁ra jest w najwi脛聶kszym stopniu odpowiedzialna za wzrost koszt脙鲁w produkcji. Potrzebujemy konkretnych rozwi脛聟za脜聞 wspieraj脛聟cych europejski przemys脜聜, w tym powrotu do darmowych uprawnie脜聞. Przemys脜聜 europejski eksportuj脛聟cy swoje produkty poza granice Unii, do pa脜聞stw o znacznie mniej restrykcyjnej polityce klimatycznej, znajduje si脛聶 w coraz gorszej sytuacji, trac脛聟c swoj脛聟 pozycj脛聶 konkurencyjn脛聟 na globalnym rynku. Czyli robimy dobrze, ale za ma脜聜o. Czas podj脛聟脛聡 nast脛聶pne kroki.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sara Matthieu, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, well, I'm not sure where all the colleagues are, but I'm glad to be here. Commissioner, Parliament.

Voorzitter, de koolstofgrensheffing zorgt ervoor dat grote vervuilers betalen, en bevordert eerlijke concurrentie, want zij geldt ook voor niet-Europese bedrijven.

Het stapsgewijs verminderen van de gratis uitstootrechten zorgt voor miljarden aan inkomsten, die broodnodig zijn. Europa heeft momenteel immers een investeringskloof van 344脗聽miljard脗聽EUR om klimaatneutraal te worden. Deze rekening gaan we niet bij onze gezinnen leggen, maar wel bij de grote vervuilers.

Die inkomsten moeten we slim investeren om onze industrie te helpen vergroenen. We moeten echter ook de uitvoering goed aanpakken; daarom verminderen we nu de administratieve lasten, zonder de klimaatbescherming op te offeren.

Tot slot heb ik 脙漏脙漏n heldere boodschap voor de Amerikaanse president Trump: Europa bepaalt zijn eigen regels. Wij laten ons niet dicteren door bullebakken. Alle aanvallen op deze koolstofgrensheffing mogen dus stoppen. Wij gaan niet bougeren.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jonas Sj脙露stedt, f脙露r The Left gruppen. 芒聙聯 Fru talman! N脙陇r EU-kommissionen talar om f脙露renkling, d脙楼 ska man vara f脙露rsiktig. D脙楼 ska man l脙陇sa f脙露rslaget noga. F脙露r f脙露renkling betyder alltf脙露r ofta f脙露rsvagning av bra lagstiftning. Men s脙楼 脙陇r det inte den h脙陇r g脙楼ngen. Den h脙陇r g脙楼ngen 脙陇r f脙露renkling f脙露renkling. D脙陇rf脙露r st脙露der vi det h脙陇r f脙露rslaget till att f脙露renkla klimattullarna, CBAM. De kommer att bli enklare att genomf脙露ra. De kommer att bli enklare att hantera, men 脙露ver 99 procent av effekten kommer att finnas kvar. Det 脙陇r ett bra f脙露rslag.

Men klimattullarna kommer att m脙露ta motst脙楼nd i omv脙陇rlden. Inte minst tror jag att de kommer att m脙露ta motst脙楼nd fr脙楼n USA. Man m脙楼ste ta en konflikt f脙露r att s脙陇tta klimatet f脙露re kommersiella intressen, och d脙陇r m脙楼ste kommissionen st脙楼 p脙楼 sig. Men samtidigt som vi pratar klimattullar 脙楼ker Ursula von der Leyen till golfspelande president Trump i Skottland och sluter ett avtal om att k脙露pa amerikansk fossilgas och fossilolja f脙露r 750 miljarder dollar. Fossila br脙陇nslen som framst脙陇llts med fracking, den s脙陇msta metod man kan t脙陇nka sig b脙楼de ur klimat- och milj脙露synpunkt.

Vi beh脙露ver lite b脙陇ttre sammanh脙楼llning i kommissionens politik p脙楼 klimatomr脙楼det. Jag f脙露resl脙楼r att kommission脙陇r Hoekstra tar ett allvarligt samtal med Ursula von der Leyen om det h脙陇r med fossila br脙陇nslen.

脗聽脗聽Anja Arndt, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Der CO2-Grenzausgleichsmechanismus soll vereinfacht werden. Durch den neuen Vorschlag w脙录rden aber immer noch 99脗聽Prozent unserer Importe von Eisen, Stahl, Zement, D脙录ngemitteln und Aluminium mit untragbar hohen CO2-Abgaben belastet werden. Und wof脙录r? F脙录r ein v脙露llig unbedenkliches CO2-Edelgas und f脙录r Emissionen, die dar脙录ber hinaus im Ausland entstanden sind. Wie verr脙录ckt ist das eigentlich?

Es geht hier nicht um Peanuts, sondern um Milliarden Euro. Das Geld wandert 脙录brigens direkt zur Kommission. F脙录r B脙录rger und Unternehmen w脙录rde alles daraus Hergestellte ab 2027 sp脙录rbar teurer, wie zum Beispiel Autos, Waschmaschinen, D脙录nger und deshalb alle Lebensmittel und auch das Bauen.

Und noch etwas: Die Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit sollte durch diese Verordnung wiederhergestellt werden, aber dass unsere Exporte durch diese Preissteigerung noch st脙陇rker ihre Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit verlieren, das wurde ganz au脙聼er Acht gelassen. Die Vereinfachung war sicher gut gemeint, aber sie treibt die Deindustrialisierung Europas v脙露llig verantwortungslos weiter voran.

CBAM und der gesamte CO2-Zertifikatehandel geh脙露ren komplett abgeschafft. Sie zerst脙露ren, und retten gar nichts. Mit diesem Klimazoll will die Kommission Geld machen, darum geht's. Deutschland braucht die AfD 芒聙娄

(Die Pr脙陇sidentin entzieht der Rednerin das Wort.)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mohammed Chahim, on behalf of the S&D Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, first of all, I very much welcome our swift trilogue agreement between Parliament, the Commission and the Council. This is not a small achievement, and we should really be proud of what we have done. It shows that within this Parliament, pro-European parties can work together and we are capable of moving fast and delivering when climate and industry and our credibility is at stake.

CBAM matters. It's the backbone of our climate trade policy. It ensures that the polluter pays, that our European producers are not undercut by imports from producers that refuse to decarbonise. In short, and I've said it many times here: you are welcome to sell your products in Europe, but only if you play by the same rules 芒聙聯 either you reduce your emissions or you pay a CO2 tariff at the border.

But colleagues, my intervention today is not only one of praise; I also need to raise the alarm. The signals that the Commission has hinted at the extra flexibility for President Trump in CBAM deeply concern me. What do these flexibilities mean and why would we undermine our own instrument? No European industry has asked for this.

And let me remind you what we've always stood for: no exemptions, no carve-outs, no special deals for anybody. Not for our neighbours, and certainly not for a global superpower. Our Parliament's position is crystal clear. I don't see what has changed here.

If the Commission starts undermining CBAM by doling out perks before it has even fully entered into force, then the credibility of the entire system is at risk. This would not be a success story, but the beginning of the end of CBAM and maybe even the end of the beginning before it's fully in place in January.

Colleagues, we owe it to the climate and to our industry to defend CBAM as it has been agreed: firm, fair and future-proof.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Wopke Hoekstra, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, apologies 芒聙聯 I was just stuck in a Dutch t脙陋te-脙聽-t脙陋te with the architect of CBAM.

Madam President, it's one down, three to go. We have dealt with simplification 芒聙聯 or we have almost dealt with simplification 芒聙聯 and exports, scope and circumvention are very high on our list to deal with during this calendar year. Again, we have delivered on this with considerable speed, and that is hugely important in the type of times we are living in.

So again, let me thank the co-legislators, and the European Parliament in particular, for making sure that this was dealt with in this particular way and in this time frame. We will continue with the other three parts of the equation, and I hope to be back here again soon on exports, on scope and on circumvention.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Antonio Decaro, relatore. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, abbiamo detto pi脙鹿 volte che il脗聽green deal 脙篓 un grande piano di tutela dell'ambiente e di contrasto ai cambiamenti climatici, ma 脙篓 anche un piano per la competitivit脙聽 delle nostre aziende che, investendo nelle tecnologie verdi, diventano competitive nel mondo.

Credo che in questo momento, con queste modifiche del CBAM, stiamo dando dimostrazione di un impegno anche sulla semplificazione, perch脙漏 in fase di prima attuazione ci siamo resi conto che alla fine il 90脗聽% degli importatori sono responsabili dell'1脗聽% delle emissioni complessive e quindi abbiamo ritenuto giusto, tutti insieme, concentrarci su quel 10脗聽% di importatori che, invece, sono responsabili del 99脗聽% 芒聙聯 pensate 芒聙聯 delle emissioni complessive.

脙聢 un modo per ridurre la burocrazia, semplificare la vita delle aziende ma mantenere gli obiettivi strategici di tutela dell'ambiente e di contrasto ai cambiamenti climatici, che sono previsti all'interno del grande piano che abbiamo chiamato green deal.

Questa operazione del CBAM serve a evitare delocalizzazioni, serve ad evitare la concorrenza sleale, serve anche a proteggerci da azioni che arrivano da paesi esteri; ho citato prima nel mio intervento i dazi che arrivano tra il 30 e 50脗聽% proprio sui prodotti del CBAM o su quei prodotti che hanno al loro interno i prodotti del CBAM.

Credo che domani dovremmo dare una dimostrazione di unit脙聽, come abbiamo fatto nella prima approvazione in Parlamento e come abbiamo fatto nelle due approvazioni in seno alla commissione ENVI 芒聙聯 che mi onoro di rappresentare 芒聙聯 perch脙漏, come ho detto nel mio primo intervento, io credo che le condizioni di contorno, quello che sta accadendo nel resto del mondo, oggi ci portano a stare insieme, a difendere le nostre aziende e a difendere il futuro del nostro pianeta.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Die Aussprache ist geschlossen.

Die Abstimmung findet morgen, Mittwoch, den 10.脗聽September 2025, statt.

脗聽

16. Wave of violence and continuous use of force against protesters in Serbia (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache 脙录ber die Erkl脙陇rungen des Rates und der Kommission zu der Welle der Gewalt und der anhaltenden Gewaltanwendung gegen Demonstranten in Serbien (2025/2810(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Kos, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, the Commission appreciates the opportunity for another debate on the situation in Serbia, which the Commission has been following very closely for many months, and with increasing concern. After ten months of protests, Serbian society is left with deep divisions and feelings of hatred and frustration, and all of this is reflected on the streets of Serbian towns and cities.

We expect police action to be proportionate and respect fundamental rights. We condemn all acts of hate, vandalism and violence, and continue to call on all sides to de-escalate the tension. Respect for fundamental rights and values 芒聙聯 including freedom of peaceful assembly, and media and academic freedom 芒聙聯 are key elements of Serbia's EU path and need to be adhered to by everyone.

This also implies avoiding any kind of violence against representatives of government and their families, as well as to party premises as a means of political protest. The Commission has consistently emphasised the importance of respecting and proactively defending the independence of judicial institutions, and we condemn violence or threats against representatives of the judiciary, including the prosecution.

Media freedom is one of the essential foundations of a democratic society and is a fundamental right in the EU, and therefore is a crucial element of, and a prerequisite for, Serbia's EU accession process. In this context, political and economic influence on the media in Serbia remains a grave concern and needs to be addressed.

We expect Serbia to create an enabling environment for media freedom and ensure that freedom of expression can be exercised without hindrance. We are calling for the de-escalation of tensions and for the authorities to allow an increasing space for journalists to freely express their voices. The attacks that journalists have been facing in the context of the protests need to stop, including reported cases of police involvement and verbal attacks from high-level officials. It is crucial that journalists can do their job free from any threat of violence, harassment and intimidation to ensure that citizens have access to all information.

The Commission also stresses that spreading disinformation and hostile political rhetoric, including against the EU and its citizens, runs contrary to EU values and standards.

Honourable Members, Serbia is a candidate country to the EU. We want a truly democratic Serbia inside the EU. As Commissioner for Enlargement, I am truly devoted to this. We will continue with constructive engagement and will do everything possible to help Serbia move forward on its accession path.

But for this to happen, concrete steps on democratic principles and reforms are needed. The participation of President脗聽Vu脛聧i脛聡 in military parades in Moscow and in Beijing, accompanied with critical statements towards the EU and respectful Members of Parliament 芒聙聯 as of last time, towards the Members of the European Green Party delegation 芒聙聯 is not what is expected from a candidate country.

The sincerity of Serbia's commitment to the EU path and its political will to credibly implement the required reforms have to be strengthened via communication and concrete action. Serbia needs to step up efforts and overcome the current stagnation in the implementation of the fundamental reforms. It is time to deliver concrete results in fighting corruption and strengthening the rule of law, including the independence of the judiciary and media freedom, and on electoral reforms.

The Commission expects Serbia to create an enabling environment for civil society. Independent voices need to be heard and protected. Intimidation and pressure on civil society is not the way we operate in the Union, and must not be tolerated. All these reform steps are key to Serbia's progress on its European path, and they are imperative to exiting the current political impasse and divisions in Serbian society.

History has proven that democratic changes can only come from within the country through internal democratic processes, and this is what the Commission respects and abides by. We support ODIHR's recommendations on electoral legislation, especially the audit of voter registers. This would improve the political climate and could serve as an example that advancement is possible if there is the will and a constructive approach by all.

Again, it is imperative for Serbia to create the conditions for a renewed dialogue between stakeholders across the political spectrum and across society. In our view, this should be based on a renewed societal consensus on key reforms. We hope to see steps towards dialogue concerning the election of the new council of the media regulator, REM, and a successful completion of this procedure in the upcoming weeks. This is one of the commitments for opening cluster 3. EU accession is an all-of-society project and, at the same time, a joint responsibility of all political parties and other stakeholders.

Dear honourable Members, we will continue engaging with the authorities and all stakeholders, political actors and civil society, encouraging them to find a way out of the current political impasse and to establish a genuinely inclusive process for the benefit of all its citizens and the whole Serbian society, and for the EU path and EU membership in the future.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Davor Ivo Stier, u ime kluba PPE. 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, deset mjeseci nakon novosadske tragedije Srbija je puno vi脜隆e polarizirana. Pove脛聡an je nivo nasilja i represije, 脜隆to jasno osu脛聭ujemo. EPP 脛聡e donijeti odgovaraju脛聡e odluke glede odnosa prema SNS-u, kao 脜隆to je danas najavio predsjednik Weber.

O naravi Vu脛聧i脛聡eve vlasti smo puno puta govorili. S kolegom Piculom i ostalim izvjestiteljima u sjeni pripremili smo rezoluciju u kojoj je gotovo sve ve脛聡 re脛聧eno, a i u prija脜隆njim rezolucijama Parlamenta se upozoravalo na probleme. Me脛聭utim, bilo bi naivno misliti da se problemi mogu rije脜隆iti smjenom samo jednog 脛聧ovjeka.

Problemi u Srbiji su puno dublji. Oni su strukturni i oni tako脛聭er obuhva脛聡aju dio oporbe i studentskog pokreta. To je postalo o脛聧ito jo脜隆 u lipnju na velikom mitingu u Beogradu. Radio Slobodna Europa je okarakterizirala taj protestni skup s ovim naslovom: Nacionalizam nadja脛聧ao borbu protiv korupcije. To se i vidjelo kada je na tom protestu profesor Milo Lompar sa Sveu脛聧ili脜隆ta u Beogradu ponovio gotovo sve postulate velikosrpske nacionalisti脛聧ke ideologije.

I stoga, kao 脜隆to je kriva dosada脜隆nja popustljiva politika prema Vu脛聧i脛聡u, tako bi bilo krivo dati bjanko 脛聧ek oporbi. Na脜隆 posao nije mijenjati vlast u Srbiji, ve脛聡 tra脜戮iti ispunjavanje kriterija za koje se sama Srbija obvezala predajom EU kandidature i to tra脜戮iti u prvom redu od vlasti, ali tako脛聭er od oporbe.

Mo脜戮emo u tome pomo脛聡i, ali je na Srbiji da sama odlu脛聧i kojim putem 脜戮eli i脛聡i.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tonino Picula, u ime kluba S&D. 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, eskalacija nasilja nad prosvjednicima, provokativni vanjskopoliti脛聧ki potezi, najnoviji napadi na novinare i projekt potpune kontrole medija razlozi su za jo脜隆 jednu ozbiljnu raspravu o Srbiji u Europskom parlamentu.

Uvrede koje je uputio zastupnicima ovog parlamenta, predsjednik Vu脛聧i脛聡 ve脛聡 godinama koristi protiv svojih neistomi脜隆ljenika u samoj Srbiji. Ni susjedne zemlje nisu po脜隆te脛聭ene od posljedica politika iz Beograda.

U me脛聭uvremenu, izostaju europske obveze i reforme dok se godinama ponavljaju fraze o ozbiljnoj zabrinutosti Komisije uz nastavak politike popu脜隆tanja Vu脛聧i脛聡evom Beogradu. Paradoksalno je provoditi 19 generacija sankcija ruskom re脜戮imu, a 脜隆utjeti o dr脜戮avi kandidatkinji za 脛聧lanstvo 脛聧iji 脜隆ef javno odbija uvo脛聭enje istih agresorskom re脜戮imu.

Ukratko, Bruxelles ne smije Vu脛聧i脛聡u nuditi mrkve da bi on isporu脛聧ivao batine svojim gra脛聭anima.

Po脜隆tovana povjerenice, nadam se da Va脜隆a izjava od ju脛聧er signalizira po脛聧etak jedne kredibilnije politike prema Beogradu od strane Komisije. Europski parlament, institucija koja najaktivnije podr脜戮ava nastavak politike pro脜隆irenja, ali i najglasnije kritizira nedemokratska postupanja u Srbiji, dat 脛聡e Vam na tom putu punu podr脜隆ku.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Annam脙隆ria Vicsek, a PfE k脙漏pvisel脜聭csoport nev脙漏ben. 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k Asszony! Az Eur脙鲁pai Parlament balliber脙隆lis sz脙隆rnya ism脙漏t Szerbi脙隆t 脙隆ll脙颅totta napirendre. Ahelyett, hogy az orsz脙隆g miel脜聭bbi uni脙鲁s csatlakoz脙隆s脙隆r脙鲁l besz脙漏ln脙漏nk, ism脙漏t a t脙录ntet脙漏sekr脜聭l vit脙隆zunk. De ha m脙隆r 脙颅gy alakult, besz脙漏lj脙录nk a val脙鲁s脙隆gr脙鲁l. Szerbi脙隆t k脙漏t sz脙露rny脜卤 trag脙漏dia r脙隆zta meg r脙露vid id脜聭n bel脙录l. A 2023-as Ribnikar iskolai l脙露v脙露ld脙露z脙漏s, amelyben 14 脙隆rtatlan vesztette 脙漏let脙漏t, majd tavaly novemberben az 脙潞jvid脙漏ki trag脙漏dia, amelyben tizenhat ember, k脙露zt脙录k gyermekek 脙漏s fiatalok haltak meg. Mindk脙漏t eset utcai mozgalmakat ind脙颅tott el. A t脙隆rsadalmi megr脙隆zk脙鲁dtat脙隆s 脙漏rthet脜聭 volt, a k脙露vetel脙漏sek pedig indokoltak. De amit ebb脜聭l az ellenz脙漏k csin脙隆lt, az m脙隆r nem a demokr脙隆cia, hanem a polg脙隆ri engedetlens脙漏g 脙漏s az anarchia receptje lett.

K脙漏pzelj脙漏k el, mi t脙露rt脙漏nne, ha 脙聳n脙露k valamely egyetem脙漏n t脙颅z-tizen脙露t ember megb脙漏n脙颅tan脙隆 az oktat脙隆st, vagy ha itt az Eur脙鲁pai Parlamentben egy tucatnyi ember f脙录stbomb脙隆val 脙漏s pet脙隆rd脙隆val akad脙隆lyozn脙隆 a munk脙隆t? Ez felh脙隆bor脙颅t脙鲁 脙漏s elfogadhatatlan lenne. Pedig ezek pontosan 脙颅gy megt脙露rt脙漏ntek Szerbi脙隆ban. Az Eur脙鲁pai Parlament nem alkalmazhat kett脜聭s m脙漏rc脙漏t. Igen, el kell 脙颅t脙漏lni a t脙录ntet脜聭kkel szembeni t脙潞lkap脙隆sokat, de ugyan脙颅gy el kell 脙颅t脙漏lni a rend脜聭r脙露k elleni er脜聭szakot is. 脙聣n 脙職jvid脙漏ken 脙漏lek. A t脙露rt脙漏n脙漏sekr脜聭l nemcsak a m脙漏di脙隆b脙鲁l, hanem polg脙隆rt脙隆rsaim, ismer脜聭seim vide脙鲁ib脙鲁l, t脙露rt脙漏neteib脜聭l is t脙隆j脙漏koz脙鲁dom. Innen tudom, hogy rend脜聭r脙露k sz脙隆zai s脙漏r脙录ltek meg, de l脙隆ttam olyan felv脙漏telt is, amelyen f脙漏m hus脙隆ngokkal vert脙漏k 脜聭ket. Nem fogadhatjuk el, hogy hulig脙隆nok pr脙鲁b脙隆lnak polg脙隆rh脙隆bor脙潞t kirobbantani. Az er脜聭szakot el kell 脙颅t脙漏lni, a politikai vit脙隆kat pedig az utc脙隆kr脙鲁l vissza kell vinni a parlamentbe.

脗聽脗聽Arkadiusz Mularczyk, w imieniu grupy ECR. 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Wysoka Izbo! Z niepokojem obserwujemy fal脛聶 przemocy wobec pokojowych demonstrant脙鲁w w Serbii. Od jesieni ubieg脜聜ego roku u脜录ycie si脜聜y wobec obywateli sprzeciwiaj脛聟cych si脛聶 decyzj脛聟 w脜聜adz jest dla nas nie do zaakceptowania. Ba脜聜kany to region, w kt脙鲁rym siln脛聟 obecno脜聸脛聡 dzisiaj ma Rosja, ale te脜录 Chiny.

Nasze wsparcie dla proeuropejskiego ducha spo脜聜ecze脜聞stw, nie tylko serbskiego, ale te脜录 ca脜聜ej ba脜聜ka脜聞skiej sz脙鲁stki jest kluczowe i bardzo wa脜录ne. Nie mo脜录emy jednak uwa脜录a脛聡, 脜录e Unia Europejska nie jest bez winy. Droga Ba脜聜kan脙鲁w Zachodnich do cz脜聜onkostwa trwa zbyt d脜聜ugo, a brak wyra脜潞nej perspektywy czasowej wyra脜潞nie rozzuchwala tych, kt脙鲁rzy chc脛聟 destabilizacji regionu w sercu europejskiego kontynentu.

Musimy jasno okre脜聸li脛聡 nasz脛聟 solidarno脜聸脛聡 z obywatelami Serbii, wsparcie ich europejskich aspiracji i stanowczo powiedzie脛聡 芒聙聻nie芒聙聺 dla przemocy wobec protestuj脛聟cych.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helmut Brandst脙陇tter, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Commissioner, I want to start with some very good news. My group, the Renew Group, will propose the Serbian students for the Sakharov Prize of this year, together with Irena Joveva. Thank you very much, Irena, for working with us for that. This is a very important sign. It was awarded for the first time in 1988 by Parliament to Nelson Mandela. It's a human rights prize, and that is the message to all parts of Serbian society, not only to those freedom-loving students and freedom-loving citizens, but also to Mr Vu脛聧i脛聡 and his people. Human rights are important for the European Union. They are an important part of our values. The Serbian people are welcome in the European Union, but the authoritarian Vu脛聧i脛聡 regime is not. That is a very important message.

The next thing is, Ms Vicsek, you talked about the tragedy in Novi Sad. It was not a tragedy 芒聙聯 it was corruption. It was corruption with China, and it was corruption of the Vu脛聧i脛聡 regime, and we have to say that openly. Yes, there is also corruption in the European Union, but we fight it here and we say very openly, you should fight it there if you want to be a member of the European Union.

One more point: he even has political prisoners now. That does not work in the European Union. I want to welcome here Biljana 脛聬or脛聭evi脛聡 芒聙聯 she is here today from the Green Party 芒聙聯 and my friends from the Liberal Party, Pavlo Grbovi脛聡 and Natan Albahari, and also Borko Stefanovi脛聡 from the Social Democrats. We had a very good conversation today. We feel that we want to be European and we want to work together. Of course, it's the decision of the Serbian people to decide which kind of government you want, but we want human rights.

脗聽脗聽Vladimir Prebili脛聧, v imenu skupine Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Gospa predsednica, spo脜隆tovani.脗聽Srbija obupano kli脛聧e na pomo脛聧. Namesto napredka se njeni dr脜戮avljani soo脛聧ajo z represijo, nadzorom medijev in nasiljem nad tistimi, ki si drznejo nasprotovati. 脜聽tudenti in demokrati脛聧na opozicija pogumno vztrajajo na ulicah ter zahtevajo svobodo, resnico in odgovornost. In nekaj morajo vedeti. Evropa vas sli脜隆i!

Ne moremo sprejeti Srbije, kjer huligani napadajo mirne protestnike. Ne moremo sprejeti Srbije, kjer predsednik Vu脛聧i脛聡 predstavnike te institucije ozna脛聧uje za drhal. Ne moremo sprejeti ukradenih volitev, uti脜隆anih glasov in strahu kot orodja mo脛聧i.

Demokrati脛聧na Srbija ni le potreba njenih dr脜戮avljanov, temve脛聧 evropska nujnost, klju脛聧na za stabilno, napredno in mirno regijo. In naj bom jasen. EPP mora prenehati z opravi脛聧evanjem Aleksandra Vu脛聧i脛聡a. Vsaki脛聧, ko njegove napake ignorirajo, 脜隆tudentje in dr脜戮avljani pla脛聧ajo ceno. Srbija si zaslu脜戮i demokracijo, Srbija si zaslu脜戮i svobodo in skupaj moramo poskrbeti, da bodo dobili oboje. Spo脜隆tovani voditelji srbske opozicije, hvala, ker ste danes z nami.

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Everding, im Namen der Fraktion The Left. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! In Serbien treten Presse, Studierende und viele andere f脙录r unsere europ脙陇ischen Werte ein, und wie werden diese Proteste beantwortet? Mit Blendgranaten, Tr脙陇nengas und Verhaftungen. Diese Menschen und auch Kolleginnen und Kollegen aus diesem Haus wurden von Pr脙陇sident Vu脛聧i脛聡 auf 脙录belste Art und Weise beleidigt und angegriffen. So ein Verhalten kann die EU und auch dieses Haus nicht tolerieren.

Novi Sad ist die Partnerstadt meiner Heimatstadt Dortmund, und Deutschland darf als wichtiger EU-Partner zu der Situation in Serbien nicht schweigen. Statt strategischer Interessen, Lithium-Deals und Co. m脙录ssen wir Menschen und auch die Umwelt 脙录ber den Profit stellen.

Die Situation des Tierschutzes ist ebenso alarmierend: Tierheime sind 脙录berf脙录llt, staatliche Unterst脙录tzung nahezu nicht existent; dennoch k脙陇mpfen engagierte Menschen f脙录r Tierrechte, f脙录r Menschenrechte vor Ort, und damit verk脙露rpern sie all unsere europ脙陇ischen Werte.

Wir m脙录ssen ganz entschieden handeln: gezielte Sanktionen gegen diejenigen verh脙陇ngen, die die Demokratie unterdr脙录cken, mutige proeurop脙陇ische Stimmen unterst脙录tzen und unmissverst脙陇ndlich klarmachen, dass Europa f脙录r gemeinsame Werte steht. Die Spirale von Gewalt in Serbien muss durchbrochen werden.

脗聽脗聽Tomasz Froelich, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Serbien droht zu einer zweiten Ukraine zu werden. Es begann mit Studentenprotesten gegen Korruption; die Mehrheit der Serben unterst脙录tzte das Anliegen. Nun mischen fremde Kr脙陇fte von au脙聼en mit, die Proteste werden f脙录r geopolitische Zwecke missbraucht. Warum? Weil Serbien pragmatische Beziehungen zu Russland und China unterh脙陇lt. Das ist Serbiens gutes Recht, denn Serbien ist ein souver脙陇ner Staat, aber das passt manchen hier nicht脗聽芒聙聯 daher nun die Eskalation.

Es geht um Destabilisierung, die Westbindung Serbiens, den Sturz von Vu脛聧i脛聡 und um Chaos. Chaos, das Sie befeuern, Sie und vermummte Schl脙陇ger, die auf serbische Polizisten einpr脙录geln. Lassen Sie das! Schluss mit diesem Pseudoimperialismus! Sie erreichen damit nur das Gegenteil, denn die Serben reagieren allergisch auf fremde Einmischung.

Der Westen hat dort ohnehin einen miserablen Ruf. Die NATO hat das Land in den Neunzigern platt gebombt. Die Wunden sitzen bis heute tief. Nur jeder zehnte Serbe w脙录nscht sich heutzutage eine pro-westliche Ausrichtung; nur jeder f脙录nfte f脙录hlt sich emotional mit der EU verbunden, und mittlerweile lehnt sogar die Mehrheit der Serben einen EU-Beitritt ab. Sie haben kein Recht, sich in serbische Angelegenheiten einzumischen.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, the recent events in Serbia remind us that European integration is much more than technical negotiations. Respect of our common values and fundamental human rights is crucial.

The ongoing protests started peaceful, but the reaction at the same time was the wrong one. We have received many reports of disproportionate use of force by security services, and we must stress that Serbia's authorities have to ensure that fundamental rights are fully respected.

A candidate country aspiring to join the European Union should show that it upholds our key principles. Yes, Serbia is an essential partner in the Western Balkans, and I still see the future of Serbia ultimately in the European Union. But the road to membership must be based on common democratic standards, and hopefully Serbia will come back to its path towards EU membership. Now it's not on this path.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kathleen Van Brempt (S&D). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, je vraagt je soms af hoe diep een regime kan zinken. Enerzijds maakt men grote sier bij Poetin en Xi Jinping 芒聙聰 voor alle duidelijkheid, leiders van uitgesproken autocratische regimes 芒聙聰 terwijl men anderzijds thuis vredevol, vreedzaam protest van studenten, maar ook van de bevolking tegen de neergang van de democratie en tegen corruptie, onderdrukt.

Laat me commissaris Kos bedanken voor haar duidelijke introductie. Ik hoop dat de boodschap wordt gevolgd door de hele Commissie en de Raad. Ik hoop ook dat de zogenaamde脗聽EP appeasement 芒聙聰 het ondersteunen van en het gezellig samen op de foto gaan met president Vu脛聧i脛聡 芒聙聰 stopt.

Servi脙芦 is immers niet eender welk land, maar een toetredingsland. Het wordt tijd voor actie en om de toetredingsonderhandelingen ook daadwerkelijk te gebruiken om het verschil te maken op de grond.

Daarvoor wil ik de commissaris alle steun geven. Het is heel belangrijk dat de pro-Europese meerderheid een duidelijk standpunt inneemt, voor de democratie en de脗聽rule of law in Servi脙芦.

脗聽脗聽Thierry Mariani (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, comme en G脙漏orgie, l'Union europ脙漏enne choisit, en Serbie, d'encourager le chaos, l脙聽 o脙鹿 elle devrait au contraire soutenir un gouvernement d脙漏mocratiquement 脙漏lu.

Depuis d脙漏j脙聽 neuf mois, la Serbie fait face 脙聽 une vague de manifestations dont la nature n'a plus rien de pacifique. Un d脙漏sordre qui affecte la majorit脙漏 de la population, captive du comportement violent et organis脙漏 d'une minorit脙漏. 23脗聽000脗聽rassemblements ill脙漏gaux, 12脗聽000脗聽blocages de routes, 750脗聽attaques de locaux politiques et d脙漏j脙聽 170脗聽policiers bless脙漏s. Ce n'est plus une contestation d脙漏mocratique classique. Il s'agit d'une violence dirig脙漏e contre l'脙聣tat et ses institutions. Un essai pour changer un gouvernement.

Face 脙聽 cela, le gouvernement serbe a multipli脙漏 les gestes d'ouverture. Un dialogue a 脙漏t脙漏 propos脙漏 aux 脙漏tudiants. Le pr脙漏sident Vu脛聧i脛聡 a m脙陋me propos脙漏 un r脙漏f脙漏rendum consultatif sur la confiance en son mandat ainsi que des 脙漏lections anticip脙漏es.

Chaque fois, les manifestants, bloqu脙漏s dans des postures st脙漏riles, ont refus脙漏 ces propositions. Car derri脙篓re ces mouvements se cachent des 脙漏l脙漏ments radicaux et des ONG politis脙漏es dont l'agenda n'est pas la r脙漏forme d脙漏mocratique et pacifique du pays, mais la d脙漏stabilisation de la Serbie par des calculs g脙漏opolitiques.

Le pr脙漏sident Vu脛聧i脛聡 a 脙漏t脙漏 脙漏lu avec plus de 60脗聽% des voix. 脙聙 ce titre, son mandat et sa majorit脙漏 sont clairs, l脙漏gitimes et d脙漏mocratiques. Bruxelles doit cesser de harceler ce pays sous le moindre pr脙漏texte. Cette r脙漏solution est une nouvelle ing脙漏rence contre un gouvernement d脙漏mocratiquement 脙漏lu, mais qui a le d脙漏faut de vous d脙漏plaire.

脗聽脗聽Stephen Nikola Bartulica (ECR). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, kolegice i kolege, kada se govori o Srbiji moramo govoriti realno, a ne o pustim 脜戮eljama u ovom visokom domu.

Prvo treba re脛聡i da u Srbiji i dalje politi脛聧ari njeguju stare mitove i 脜戮ive u nekom paralelnom svijetu. Nije samo pitanje Vu脛聧i脛聡a i smjene vlasti, potrebna je dublja katarza cijelog dru脜隆tva koje ne priznaje svoju odgovornost, pogotovo u agresiji prema Hrvatskoj 90-ih godina.

Zato 脛聡u jo脜隆 jednom ponoviti, za normalizaciju odnosa i za europski put Srbija treba priznati odgovornost iz 90-ih godina i ne samo rije脛聧ima, nego konkretnim djelima pokazati da je pokrenula novo poglavlje. To zna脛聧i isplatu ratne od脜隆tete svim 脜戮rtvama iz Domovinskog rata u Hrvatskoj.

脛聦uli smo puno rije脛聧i, puno deklaracija i sl., ali konkretno u politici jedino vrijede djela, konkretna djela i zato jo脜隆 jedna poruka opoziciji - ostavite velikosrpsku retoriku i priznajte svoju odgovornost iz rata 90-ih godina.

脗聽脗聽Irena Joveva (Renew). 芒聙聯 Gospa predsednica.脗聽"Budno spremljamo situacijo v Srbiji in res smo zelo zaskrbljeni." To je uradna linija vodstva oziroma predsednice Komisije in 脛聧lanic. Ampak oni mi脜戮ijo.

脛聦akajo, da mine? Da 脜隆e kdo umre, mogo脛聧e? Ne vem. Vem pa, da ni razumevanja, ni odgovornosti, ni niti najmanj脜隆e geste solidarnosti z ljudmi na ulicah. Ker je la脜戮je "tam zgoraj" ignorirati brutalnost re脜戮ima kot pritiskati nanj.

Protestniki vztrajajo, ampak seveda izgubljajo zaupanje v Unijo. Mi pa s tem izgubljamo tiste, ki v Srbiji sploh 脜隆e verjamejo v to Unijo.

Tisti, ki mi脜戮ite, torej in dopu脜隆脛聧ate nasilje in du脜隆ite upanje za bolj脜隆o prihodnost. Pa ne samo njihovo. Ker, veste, to upanje bo morda neko脛聧 potrebno tudi v va脜隆ih dr脜戮avah. Se boste takrat spomnili na gnile kompromise in 脜隆e bolj gnilo ti脜隆ino?

Vas bom jaz spomnila. Tudi brez dejanj, besede brez dejanj so izdaja.

(Govori v jeziku, ki ni uradni jezik EU)

(Govornica se je odlo脛聧ila odgovoriti na vpra脜隆anje, postavljeno z dvigom modrega karton脛聧ka)

脗聽脗聽Tomislav Sokol (PPE), pitanje koje je podizanjem plave kartice postavio. 芒聙聯 Hvala lijepa kolegici na Va脜隆em govoru, imam samo jedno pitanje.

脗聽脗聽Irena Joveva (Renew), odgovor na pitanje postavljeno podizanjem plave kartice. 芒聙聯 Hvala Vama, potrudit 脛聡u se da pri脛聧am na hrvatskom. Izvinjavam se, ide na neku drugu stranu, ali potrudit 脛聡u se.

Ne podr脜戮avam ni one koji su za dom spremni, a ne podr脜戮avam ni bilo koje druge koji su toliko borbeni u svom nacionalizmu da nekad zaboravljaju da to ustvari, ta pro脜隆lost nije najbitnija, nego je bitnija ljep脜隆a budu脛聡nost za ljude.

A to ne va脜戮i samo za Srbiju. Znate 脜隆to mislim? Hvala Vam.

脗聽脗聽Rasmus Nordqvist (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President,脗聽Friday evening I was in Novi Sad. I met students, ordinary families, health workers, veterans, all kinds of people peacefully protesting for democracy. It all began with 10 minutes of silence to commemorate the people that lost their lives in the tragic collapse at the railway station last year, 10 months ago. Never have I been at a place where thousands of people were so silent and it was only broken by the sounds of bird screams, almost like an alarm of the future that Serbia faces if we let Vu脛聧i脛聡 lead Serbia down the authoritarian path.

The free media have been suffocated, young people detained. And this for 10 months. Hundreds of thousands have taken the streets to protest Vu脛聧i脛聡's oppressive policies, widespread corruption and the police brutalities. We cannot have a leader right at the doorsteps of the EU, who kisses Putin's hands on one day and shakes hands with von der Leyen the next. The EU needs to review all funds that we give to Serbia, because I know for sure that right now the funds do not benefit the people of Serbia, but only Vu脛聧i脛聡.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽脛陆ubo脜隆 Blaha (NI). 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani predsedaj脙潞ca, v脙隆脜戮en脙颅 kolegovia, Slov脙隆ci a Srbi s脙潞 bratsk脙漏 slovansk脙漏 n脙隆rody a Slovensko plne podporuje Aleksandara Vu脛聧i脛聡a, demokraticky zvolen脙漏ho srbsk脙漏ho prezidenta.

Z脙隆pad sa v Srbsku pok脙潞脜隆a o Majdan. Chc脙潞 n脙隆sil脙颅m zvr脙隆ti脜楼 demokratick脙漏 vo脛戮by, tak ako kedysi na Ukrajine. N脙隆siln脙漏 protesty v Srbsku viedli u脜戮 k 750 脙潞tokom na stran脙颅cke centr脙隆ly. V脙陆sledkom je 170 zranen脙陆ch policajtov. Srbsk脙隆 vl脙隆da naopak pon脙潞ka dial脙鲁g, 脛聧i dokonca referendum. Ale nie, majdanisti v Srbsku nechc脙潞 re脜隆pektova脜楼 demokraciu. Ich cie脛戮om je re脜戮imov脙隆 zmena.

Srbsko sa previnilo t脙陆m, 脜戮e udr脜戮iava priate脛戮sk脙漏 vz脜楼ahy s Ruskom a chce mier, rovnako ako Slovensko. A 脙隆no, aj u n脙隆s sa Z脙隆pad pok脙潞脜隆al o Majdan. Britsk脙隆 vl脙隆da dokonca chcela zmanipulova脜楼 demokratick脙漏 vo脛戮by a financovala aj s脙潞脛聧asn脙潞 europoslanky脜聢u z frakcie Renew pani Ostriho脜聢ov脙潞, ktor脙隆 tu sed脙颅 priamo predo mnou.

My odmietame tak脙漏to imperi脙隆lne z脙隆sahy do n脙隆rodnej suverenity na脜隆ich kraj脙颅n. Nesta脛聧ilo v脙隆m, 脜戮e ste v roku 1999 bombardovali Belehrad a kv脙麓li v脙隆m umierali deti? Nesta脛聧脙颅 v脙隆m, 脜戮e v rozpore s medzin脙隆rodn脙陆m pr脙隆vom uzn脙隆vate Kosovo? My stoj脙颅me za Srbskom a demokratick脙陆m prezidentom Vu脛聧i脛聡om.
